The Space Shuttle was a reusable spacecraft that was used by NASA for a number of missions between 1981 and 2011. It was designed to be able to carry people and cargo into space, and then return to Earth and land like an airplane. However, the Space Shuttle had some limitations. It could only carry a certain amount of cargo and it could only stay in space for a limited amount of time before it needed to return to Earth. 
The Orion spacecraft is a new spacecraft that is being developed by NASA to take people further into space than ever before, including to the Moon and eventually to Mars. Unlike the Space Shuttle, the Orion spacecraft is not reusable. It is designed to be used just once for a single mission, and then discarded. 
One reason why the Orion spacecraft is designed this way is because it is going to be used for much longer missions than the Space Shuttle was. The Space Shuttle could only stay in space for a few weeks at a time, but the Orion spacecraft is being designed to stay in space for months or even years at a time. It needs to be able to carry a lot of supplies and equipment for the astronauts to use during those long missions. 
Another reason why the Orion spacecraft is designed to land in the ocean is because it is going to be traveling much faster and further than the Space Shuttle. When it re-enters the Earth's atmosphere, it will be moving at a much higher speed and will need to use a heat shield to protect itself from the heat and friction of re-entry. When the Orion spacecraft lands in the ocean, it will use parachutes to slow down and then float on the water until it can be retrieved by a special recovery team. 
I hope that helps to explain the difference between the Space Shuttle and the Orion spacecraft!

The term "S.O.S" is an international Morse code distress signal that is used to indicate a life-threatening emergency. It was first adopted by the German government in 1905, and it has been widely used by ships, airplanes, and other modes of transportation for many years. 
The letters "S.O.S." do not stand for any particular phrase, but they were chosen because they are easy to transmit in Morse code and can be easily recognized by people around the world. When the letters are transmitted in Morse code, they are sent as three dots, three dashes, and three dots (... --- ...). 
In an emergency, a person can use any means available to transmit the S.O.S. signal, such as flashing a light, sounding a horn, or using a radio. The S.O.S. signal is recognized as a distress call by rescue agencies and is used to alert them to the fact that someone is in danger and needs immediate help.

Electric poles are typically made of wood, steel, or concrete. The material used for an electric pole depends on the location and the purpose of the pole. 
Wooden electric poles are made from trees that are specifically grown for use in the construction of poles. These trees are chosen for their straightness and strength, and they are treated with preservatives to make them more resistant to rot and insects. 
Steel electric poles are made from rolled steel, which is a type of metal that is formed into a long, thin strip and then wound into a cylinder. Steel poles are very strong and durable, but they are also more expensive than wooden poles. 
Concrete electric poles are made from a mixture of cement, water, and aggregate, such as sand and gravel. Concrete poles are very strong and durable, but they are also more expensive than wooden or steel poles. 
Electric poles are designed to be long-lasting and able to withstand extreme weather conditions. However, they can still be damaged by storms, lightning, or other events, which may require them to be repaired or replaced. In general, electric poles are inspected regularly to ensure that they are in good condition, and any necessary repairs or replacements are made as needed.

input: There are a number of factors that have contributed to the strength of the modern German economy. input: The unemployment rate is a measure of the percentage of the labor force that is currently not working but is actively looking for work. The underemployment rate is a measure of the percentage of the labor force that is working, but not getting as many hours of work as they would like or not working in a job that fully utilizes their skills and abilities. There are a few other terms that are related to unemployment that you might want to know: Full employment: This is when almost everyone who wants to work is able to find a job. Labor force participation rate: This is the percentage of the population that is either working or actively looking for work. Discouraged worker: This is someone who is not currently looking for work because they believe there are no jobs available for them. These people are not counted as part of the labor force and are not included in the unemployment rate. Structural unemployment: This occurs when there is a mismatch between the skills and abilities of the workers and the needs of the job market. For example, if a factory shuts down and the workers there are trained in a specific type of manufacturing, they may have difficulty finding new jobs because the skills they have are not in demand in the current job market. input: Sure! So the basic idea is that matter cannot be created or destroyed, but it can change form. This is known as the law of conservation of matter. Now, let's talk about trees. Trees are made up of matter, and they are able to grow and change over time because they are able to take in matter from their environment and use it to build new cells and tissues. One way that trees are able to do this is through a process called photosynthesis. Photosynthesis is the process that plants use to convert sunlight into energy, and it happens in the leaves of a tree. During photosynthesis, plants take in carbon dioxide from the air and water from the ground, and they use the energy from sunlight to combine these substances and create a new type of matter called glucose. Glucose is a type of sugar that plants use as a source of energy and as a building block to make other types of matter, like wood, leaves, and flowers. So, in short, trees are able to grow and change because they are able to take in matter from their environment and use it to build new cells and tissues through the process of photosynthesis. input: Cold reading is a technique used by some people to make it seem like they have psychic abilities or are able to read people's minds. They do this by asking vague questions and making observations that could apply to a lot of people, and then gradually narrowing down their guesses based on the responses they get. For example, a cold reader might start off by saying something like "I sense that you have been through a lot of changes recently," which could apply to many people. If the person they are talking to nods or agrees, the cold reader might then say something more specific, like "I get the sense that you have recently moved to a new home or started a new job." If the person agrees again, the cold reader might then say something even more specific, like "I feel like you are having some difficulty adjusting to your new surroundings and are feeling a bit overwhelmed." Cold reading can be very effective because it is hard to prove that the person is not psychic, and people often want to believe that they have special abilities. It is important to remember, however, that cold reading is just a trick and that it is not a real psychic ability.

input: Sure! Terminal velocity is the maximum speed that an object can reach as it falls through a fluid, like air or water. The reason an object can't keep accelerating is because as it falls, it encounters more and more air or water molecules, which creates a force called drag that slows the object down. The faster the object is moving, the more drag it experiences, until it reaches a speed where the force of gravity pulling it downward is exactly balanced by the force of drag pushing it upward. At this point, the object is moving at its terminal velocity and it can't keep accelerating because the forces acting on it are in balance. Does that make sense?

There are a few reasons why some homes and hotels might have the light switch for the bathroom located outside of the bathroom: 
1. Safety: Having the light switch outside of the bathroom can help prevent accidents or injuries that might occur if the switch were located inside the bathroom. For example, if the switch were inside the bathroom, someone might accidentally touch it while they are in the bath or shower, which could cause them to get an electric shock. 
2. Convenience: In some cases, the light switch might be located outside of the bathroom to make it easier for people to turn the light on and off without having to enter the bathroom. This can be especially useful in a shared bathroom where multiple people might be using the space at different times. 
3. Aesthetics: Some people might prefer the look of having the light switch located outside of the bathroom, rather than having a switch inside the bathroom. This can be especially true in bathrooms with a more modern or minimalist design. 
Overall, the purpose of having the light switch for the bathroom located outside of the room is to provide a measure of safety and convenience, and to allow people to control the lighting in the bathroom without having to enter the space.
output: Competition is undesirable . Look at the African Savannah . If every herbivore on the planes at the same thing , they 'd be competing for one resource . Pretty quickly one species would prove be superior to the rest and start crowding them into extinction ( or forcing them to adapt and evolve into a different niche ) . Let 's go back to that Savannah and take a look at some of the inhabitants . * [ this is a water buffalo ] ( URL_4 ) it 's a big , heavy animal with a broad mouth like a shovel . It evolved to indiscriminately shovel as much vegetation into it 's guts as it can . Quality does n't matter , quantity does . It 's multi chambered stomach will extract energy from even the poorest feed . * [ this is an antilope ] ( URL_0 ) check out that slender little mouth this one takes care to use it 's narrow mouth to selectively graze on the lushest grass and leaves it can find , optimum nutrition with every bite , ignore the bad stuff . * [ this is a warthog ] ( URL_1 ) , it 's basically a living bulldozer . Between it 's sensitive nose and it 's bulldozer like head , it can sniff out and dig up nutritious roots , bulbs ( , grubs and small animals ) that are out of reach for the other grazers . * [ The giraffe ] ( URL_6 ) probably needs no explanation , it evolved to feed from the treetops where few other herbivores can reach . All of these animals share a home and a diet but none of them are in direct competition with each other . Why waste energy on a biological arms race when you can just avoid getting in each other 's way ? The other side of the matter is that there is no one size fits all solution . Let 's take a quick look at some sharks . Sharks have been around since before the dinosaurs wandered the earth and most have changed very little . They 're just that good at what they do . Sharks still have a staggering variety of species depending on what they do though . * [ The greenland shark ] ( URL_3 ) is a huge species of shark living under the arctic ice . It 's a slow moving , ponderous scavenger that is entirely geared towards conserving energy in it 's vast icy environment . In the arctic you never know how long it 's going to be until your next meal so the greenland shark evolved to be super energy efficient . * [ The great white ] ( URL_2 ) is our quintessential image of what a shark is . It frequently hunts large prey like seals and is fantastically adapted to incredible feats of raw power . Running down big seals and killing them with a single massive bite or impact . * [ The blue shark ] ( URL_5 ) lives in the open ocean . The open ocean is incredibly vast and throughout it 's life it 'll probably never see land , the bottom of the ocean or even any distinguishing features . Nothing but endless , empty , blue water . As a result open ocean fish need to travel incredible distances just to find their next meal . As a result the blue shark is streamlined like a torpedo . Everything about this shark has evolved to make it swim thousands of miles effortlessly , it never stops travelling just to get from one meal to the next . Three sharks , three different lifestyles , three different solutions to get to the top of the food chain . Beautiful diversity because there is no single solution to everything .

input: The United States has a two-party system because it has historically had two main political parties: the Democratic Party and the Republican Party. These two parties have dominated the political landscape in the United States for many years and have won most of the elections at the national and state levels. In contrast, many countries in Europe have a multiparty system, which means that they have more than two major political parties. This is because the political systems in these countries often allow for the formation of many smaller parties and coalitions, which can make it more difficult for any one party to win a majority of seats in their national governments. Overall, the reason why the United States has a two-party system while other countries have more parties is largely due to historical and cultural factors, as well as the specific structures of their political systems.
output: it 's actually an interesting concept ( well , if you 're a computer science kid ) . 32 or 64 bit stands for the relative size of the registers and possibly other components in the computer . Basically , when the computer is doing operations and stuff , it needs to remember where things are , so it has a little address system to write things down . This is so it can use the RAM , or random access memory , to remember lots of things . the RAM is composed of lines of space that can hold numbers , and each one needs an address . It needs to be able to fit these addresses in the Registers , which are used to do the most basic of calculations in a computer . But the registers are only so big , so it limits the size the addresses can be . A 32 bit machine is designed around a 32 bit architecture , meaning the largest number a register could hold would be 4,294,967,295 . That 's actually not that big nowadays , and meant that at around 3 - 4 gigabytes of RAM , you actually ran out of addresses . The computer could n't figure out how to use all the ram , because it did n't have enough addresses for it . If you buy a new computer nowadays , it probably comes with 4 gigs of ram minimum , especially if its not a laptop . That 's where 64 bit comes in , and the only change is that the architecture ( the registers , the basic operations the computer can perform , etc ) is designed around 64 bits of space . suddenly the largest number we can hold is 1.84467441 × 10 ^ 19 , way bigger than 4 billion , and we wo n't run into RAM limitations in the near future . There are other benefits to 64 bit computers that come with the ability to hold larger numbers , but RAM limitations was the driving force of the change . The difference between programs that are in 32 and 64 bit all relates to these registers . The programs are probably mostly the same ; they just have to take into account the new changes for the 64 bit computer , and hence the 64 bit version might be slightly faster or more efficient . In fact , you can actually run 32 bit programs on a 64 bit machine just fine ; many 64 bit computers right now are still optimized to run 32 bit well . output: Magnetic fields induce voltage into conductors ( like copper wire ) . To increase a magnetic field , one trick is to coil copper wire . The flow of electricity creates a magnetic field along the length of the wire , but it is relatively weak . If you coil the wire , the field compounds it 's effects and changes it 's magnetic flow to be around the entire coil instead of the wire alone . Certain materials are also used to further increase magnetism by allowing magnetic field lines to move . Like electricity , magnetic field flux lines move through materials differently . Magnetic field flow is called reluctance . Iron is very reluctant , and allows the magnet field lines to become stronger . Placing an iron core inside a coil increases the magnetic field strength , which increases the ability it has to induce voltage into a nearby copper conductor . Smaller coil , higher voltage , next to a large coil , and you 've stepped down the voltage , but what you lose in volts has become amperage . If you use a higher amperage , larger coil , and place a circuit with a smaller coil next to it , you induce a higher voltage . Again , you lose the amperage , but gain the voltage , or electrical pressure .

input: When the last remaining speaker of a language or dialect dies, it is very detrimental because the language or dialect will no longer be passed down to future generations. It is important to try to preserve a language or dialect, even if only a few people speak it, because it is a part of a culture and a way of life that is valuable and should be respected. Language and dialects are also important because they can contain important historical and cultural information that can be lost forever if the language is not preserved. Imagine if your family had a special recipe for a dish that had been passed down for generations. It might not be something that everyone in the world knows or enjoys, but it is something that is special and unique to your family. If the recipe was lost and no one remembered how to make the dish anymore, it would be sad because it would be a part of your family's history and culture that was lost forever. Similarly, when a language or dialect is lost, it is like a piece of a culture is lost forever, and that can be very sad for the people who speak that language and for the people who care about preserving and respecting different cultures.
output: Eugenics is the practice of trying to create a population or species with the best hereditary information possible . In the most common usage of the word , this refers to making it so humans are as genetically perfect as possible . * * Pros * * Eugenicists normally have good intentions in mind . They want to make the world a better place , so they want to eliminate genetic diseases , plus other things like low intelligence . * * Cons * * Lack of biodiversity . Changing environments call for adaptation . If a population has only similar genes , everyone is susceptible to the same diseases , allowing an epidemic to spread . What qualifies as an optimal trait ? Is deafness an undesirable trait ? Some people say yes , but others say it 's just a natural variation . Are dark eyes desirable ? That depends on who you ask . I could go on and on , but my point is that it 's hard to determine which traits to keep . Possibility of error . It 's one thing to build a machine incorrectly . If you screw that up , you can just build a new one . If you make a mistake with an entire population and/or there 's an unexpected mutation , you could wipe out a species . A lack of an ethical way to implement eugenics . Any sort of eugenics experiment would involve either isolating people with " inferior " genes or stop them from reproducing . Either way , that 's highly unethical . A dangerous past . Eugenics has been used in the past for the justification of ethnic cleansing , the practice of killing people of ethnicities deemed inferior or undesirable .

That sharp cramp you're describing is likely a side stitch, and it is a common problem that can occur when people are physically active, especially when they are doing activities like running or cycling. A side stitch is a pain that is felt in the side of the abdomen, usually just below the ribcage. It is often caused by a muscle spasm in the muscles that help to support the diaphragm, which is the muscle that helps you to breathe. 
Side stitches can be very painful, but they are usually not serious and will go away on their own after a few minutes. To help alleviate the pain of a side stitch, you can try slowing down your activity, taking deep breaths, and massaging the area where the pain is located. It may also help to bend forward slightly or to press on the area with your hand. 
It's important to drink plenty of water and stay hydrated when you are physically active, as this can help to prevent side stitches. It's also a good idea to warm up before exercising and to stretch your muscles to help prevent cramps and other muscle pains.

input: In general, civil court and criminal court are two different types of court systems that handle different types of cases. In civil court, people or organizations can sue each other over problems with contracts, damages, or other disputes. For example, if someone slips and falls on someone else's property and gets hurt, they might go to civil court to try to get money to pay for their medical bills. Criminal court is for cases where someone is accused of breaking a law. If someone is accused of stealing, hurting someone else, or doing something else that is against the law, they might go to criminal court. In criminal court, the government (usually the police) brings charges against the person, and if they are found guilty, they might go to jail or have to pay a fine. So, in general, civil court is for cases where people or organizations are trying to get money from each other, while criminal court is for cases where someone is accused of breaking a law.

input: It is not currently possible to replace a person's organs with "backup copies" from a younger clone of themselves and have them live forever. Here's a simple explanation of how radio waves can be used to transmit music: Radio waves are a type of electromagnetic radiation, which means they are a type of energy that travels through the air at the speed of light. Radio waves can be used to transmit all kinds of information, including music. To transmit music using radio waves, the music is first recorded onto a device, like a CD or a computer. The recording is then converted into a series of electronic signals, which can be transmitted over the air using a radio transmitter. The radio transmitter sends the electronic signals through the air as radio waves. The radio waves travel through the air until they reach a radio receiver, which can be a radio, a car stereo, or a device like a smartphone or a tablet. The radio receiver has an antenna that captures the radio waves and converts them back into electronic signals. The electronic signals are then sent to a device called a speaker, which converts the signals back into sound waves that we can hear. And that's how radio waves can be used to transmit music!

Molecular orbital theory is a way of understanding how the electrons in a molecule are arranged and how they behave. It helps us predict things like the shape of the molecule and how stable it is. 
Imagine that you have a bunch of balls that you want to stack on top of each other. Each ball represents an electron. The balls are attracted to each other because of the electrical charge they carry, and they want to be as close together as possible. 
In molecular orbital theory, we try to understand how the balls (electrons) are arranged in the stack (molecule) by creating "orbital" shapes that the balls can fit into. Each orbital can hold a certain number of balls, just like how a bucket can hold a certain number of marbles. 
The shapes of the orbitals are determined by the behavior of the electrons in the molecule. Some orbitals are more stable than others, which means the balls (electrons) are less likely to fall out of them. This can help us understand why some molecules are more stable than others.

The recommended daily allowance (RDA) of Vitamin C is the amount of Vitamin C that is considered sufficient for meeting the nutritional needs of most people. This amount can vary depending on a person's age, gender, and other factors. 
Vitamin C tablets are often labeled with the percentage of the RDA that a single tablet contains. So, if your Vitamin C tablets have 1250% of the RDA, it means that a single tablet contains 12.5 times the RDA of Vitamin C. This does seem like a lot, but it's important to remember that the RDA is just a guideline and that different people have different Vitamin C needs. Some people may need more Vitamin C than the RDA, while others may need less. 
It's also worth noting that it is generally safe to take high doses of Vitamin C, as the body can easily excrete any excess through the urine. However, it is always a good idea to talk to a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen to make sure it is safe and appropriate for you.

input: Sure! The concept of infinity can be a bit confusing, so let's start with a simple example. Imagine you have a really long piece of string. You could keep adding more and more string to it, and it would always be longer. You could never reach the end of the string because it goes on forever. We call this kind of infinity "countable infinity," because you could count the number of pieces of string you have, even though you could never get to the end. Now, imagine you have an even bigger kind of infinity, where there are so many things that you couldn't even count them all if you tried. This kind of infinity is called "uncountable infinity," and it's even bigger than countable infinity. Here's another way to think about it: imagine you have a jar with an infinite number of marbles in it. You could take out all of the marbles, one by one, and count them. Even though the jar has an infinite number of marbles, you could still count them, because there are a finite number of marbles in the jar. Now, imagine that instead of marbles, the jar has an infinite number of tiny grains of sand. There are so many grains of sand that you could never count them all, no matter how long you tried. This is what we mean by uncountable infinity: there are so many things that you could never count them all, even if you had an infinite amount of time. I hope that helps! input:
Changing your name is not necessarily difficult, but it does involve some steps that you need to follow. Here's a simple explanation of the process: 
1. First, you need to decide what you want your new name to be. This can be any name you choose, as long as it is not being used to commit fraud or deceive others. 
2. Next, you will need to fill out some paperwork and file it with the government agency that handles name changes in your area. This is usually a court or government office. You will need to provide some personal information, such as your current name, your new name, and your reason for wanting to change your name. 
3. After you file your paperwork, you will need to go to a court hearing to get approval for your name change. At the hearing, a judge will review your request and make a decision about whether to approve it. 
4. If the judge approves your name change, you will be given a court order that legally changes your name. You will need to provide this court order to any government agency or organization that needs to know about your new name, such as the Department of Motor Vehicles or your employer. 
Overall, the process of changing your name can take a few weeks or even a few months, depending on your location and the specific requirements of your government agency.

input: The Watergate scandal was a political scandal in the United States that occurred during the presidency of Richard Nixon. It was the result of a break-in at the Democratic National Committee (DNC) headquarters at the Watergate office complex in Washington, D.C. in 1972, and the subsequent cover-up of the administration's involvement by the Nixon administration. Here's a simplified explanation of what happened: Some people working for Nixon's campaign broke into the DNC office to get information about the Democrats' campaign plans. The people who broke in were caught, and it was discovered that they had been paid by the Nixon campaign. Nixon tried to cover up the break-in and the connection to his campaign. He ordered people to lie about what had happened and to destroy evidence. Some of Nixon's staff members refused to go along with the cover-up and told the truth to the FBI and to a special court that was investigating the case. As more and more information came out, it became clear that Nixon had been involved in the break-in and the cover-up. The scandal was a big deal, and many people in the United States were upset with Nixon. Eventually, Nixon resigned as President rather than face impeachment (a process where a President can be removed from office by Congress).

input: Fire is a chemical reaction that occurs when certain materials, called fuels, are exposed to oxygen and a source of heat. When the heat causes the fuel to reach a high enough temperature, a chemical reaction called combustion occurs. Combustion is the process of burning, and it releases heat and light as a result. Wood is made up of organic matter, which means it is made up of molecules that contain carbon and hydrogen. When wood is exposed to oxygen and a source of heat, the heat causes the molecules in the wood to break apart and release energy in the form of heat and light. This is why wood can burn and cause a fire. Rocks, on the other hand, are not made up of organic matter. They do not contain carbon and hydrogen, so they do not have the necessary fuel to burn. Therefore, rocks cannot burn or cause a fire.

input: The NES Zapper was a gun-shaped controller used with the Nintendo Entertainment System (NES), a popular home video game console in the 1980s. The Zapper worked by using a simple light sensor to detect when you pointed it at the screen and pulled the trigger. Here's how it worked: You would hold the Zapper in your hand and aim it at the screen, just like you would a real gun. When you pulled the trigger, the Zapper would send a beam of light out of the barrel of the gun and onto the screen. If the beam of light hit something on the screen (like a target or an enemy), the Zapper would send a signal back to the NES console, telling it that you had hit the target. The NES console would then respond by making the target disappear (if it was an enemy) or giving you points (if it was a target). So, in summary, the NES Zapper worked by using a beam of light and a light sensor to detect when you were aiming at something on the screen and pulling the trigger. This allowed you to play video games that involved shooting or aiming at targets.

input: Free radicals are molecules that have an unpaired electron, which makes them very reactive. They can damage cells in your body, which can lead to inflammation and diseases such as cancer and heart disease. Antioxidants are molecules that can neutralize free radicals and prevent them from causing damage. Some foods, such as fruits and vegetables, contain a high amount of antioxidants. These foods are good for you because they can help protect your body from the damaging effects of free radicals. Some foods have more antioxidants because they contain higher amounts of certain nutrients, such as vitamin C and E, which are powerful antioxidants. These nutrients can help protect your body from the harmful effects of free radicals. So, eating foods that are high in antioxidants can help keep your body healthy.

Hollow core doors are made with a thin layer of wood on the outside, and the inside is filled with a honeycomb-like structure made of cardboard or a similar material. These doors are usually cheaper than solid wood doors because they use less wood and are easier to manufacture. They are also lighter in weight, which can make them easier to open and close. 
However, hollow core doors are not as strong or durable as solid wood doors, and they may not be as effective at insulating sound or heat. They are often used in places where a strong, heavy door is not necessary, such as in a bedroom or closet.

input: Facial hair in humans is controlled by hormones called androgens, which are present in both men and women. However, men have higher levels of androgens than women, which is why men are more likely to grow facial hair. In our ape ancestors, both males and females likely had facial hair as a way to protect their skin from the sun and to keep warm in cold weather. However, as humans evolved and began to live in different environments, some traits that were once useful became less necessary. For example, as humans began to live in hotter climates, the need for thick body hair to keep warm diminished, and as a result, humans lost much of their body hair. Similarly, as humans began to use clothing to protect themselves from the elements, the need for facial hair to protect the skin from the sun and to keep warm also diminished. As a result, women lost much of their facial hair, while men continued to have more facial hair due to their higher levels of androgens.

Rendering shadows in video games can be challenging because it requires the computer to calculate how the light in the virtual environment should interact with the objects in the scene. This can be a lot of work for the computer, especially if there are a lot of objects or the objects are moving around. 
One way that shadows are often rendered in video games is by using something called a shadow map. A shadow map is a special texture that stores information about the shadows in the scene. The computer can then use this information to quickly draw the shadows on the objects in the scene. However, shadow maps can only store so much information, so the shadows might not always look as detailed as you would like. 
Another reason that shadows might not look as detailed in video games is because the developers might have chosen to prioritize other aspects of the game, such as the characters or the environment. This means that they might not have had as much time or resources to spend on making the shadows look as good as possible.

Fish need a certain balance of salt and other minerals in their bodies to survive. Freshwater fish have evolved to live in water that has a lower concentration of salt, while saltwater fish have evolved to live in water that has a higher concentration of salt. 
When a freshwater fish is placed in saltwater, the extra salt in the water can cause problems for the fish's body. The fish's cells will try to balance out the salt concentration by taking in more water, which can cause the fish to swell up. At the same time, the fish's cells will also try to get rid of the extra salt by releasing more water, which can cause the fish to lose too much water and become dehydrated. 
On the other hand, when a saltwater fish is placed in freshwater, the lack of salt can also cause problems for the fish's body. The fish's cells will try to balance out the salt concentration by taking in more salt, which can cause the fish to lose too much water and become dehydrated. 
So, while it is possible for some fish to survive in water that is different from what they are used to, it can be difficult for them and may cause problems for their bodies. That's why it's generally best for fish to stay in the type of water that they are adapted to.

input:
Bad or sloppy code can make a mobile app drain your battery faster in a few different ways: 
1. Poorly optimized algorithms: If the app is using algorithms that are not optimized for efficiency, it may take longer to complete certain tasks, which can drain the battery faster. 
2. Constant CPU usage: If the app is constantly using the CPU, even when it's not doing anything useful, it can drain the battery faster. 
3. Constant network usage: If the app is constantly making network requests or using data, it can drain the battery faster. 
4. Poorly designed user interface: If the app has a poorly designed user interface that requires the user to perform a lot of unnecessary actions, it can drain the battery faster. 
5. Lack of power management: If the app doesn't properly manage power usage, it may keep the device's power-hungry components, such as the CPU or display, turned on unnecessarily, which can drain the battery faster. 
To summarize, bad or sloppy code can make an app drain your battery faster by causing it to use more CPU and network resources, having a poorly designed user interface, or not properly managing power usage.

The President of the United States is not paid a wage or salary like most people are. Instead, the President is given a yearly allowance, which is used to cover expenses related to their duties as President. This allowance is set by Congress and is paid for by taxpayers. 
The President also receives a number of other benefits, such as a home (the White House), transportation (the Presidential limousine), and protection (the Secret Service). These benefits are also paid for by taxpayers. 
Before becoming President, a person may have had a job or owned a business, and they may have earned money from these activities. However, while they are serving as President, they are not allowed to earn money from any other sources. This is to prevent conflicts of interest and ensure that the President is focused on their duties as President.

Birth control pills are taken by women to prevent pregnancy. Pregnancy is the process in which a woman carries a developing baby inside her uterus (womb). Birth control pills work by preventing the release of an egg from the woman's ovaries and by changing the cervical mucus and the lining of the uterus, making it more difficult for sperm to reach the egg and for a fertilized egg to attach to the uterus. 
There are also other methods of birth control available for men, such as condoms and vasectomy (a surgical procedure that makes a man sterile, or unable to father a child). However, birth control pills are not typically used by men because they do not work in the same way as they do in women. 
One reason that birth control pills are not typically used by men is because they contain hormones that are specific to the female body. These hormones can have side effects that may not be safe or appropriate for men to take. Additionally, birth control pills are not as effective at preventing pregnancy as some other methods, such as condoms, which can be used by both men and women. 
In summary, birth control pills are used by women to prevent pregnancy, while other methods, such as condoms and vasectomy, are used by men to prevent pregnancy. It is not necessary or appropriate for men to take birth control pills.

input: Ireland is a country located on an island in the North Atlantic Ocean. It is divided into two main political entities: the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland. The Republic of Ireland is an independent country that is a member of the European Union. It covers about five-sixths of the island of Ireland and is a parliamentary democracy with a president and a prime minister. The capital of the Republic of Ireland is Dublin. Northern Ireland is a part of the United Kingdom, which is a country that also includes England, Scotland, and Wales. It is located in the northeastern part of the island of Ireland and is a parliamentary democracy with a first minister and a deputy first minister. The capital of Northern Ireland is Belfast. The division of Ireland into the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland is a result of a long and complicated history that dates back hundreds of years. In the early 20th century, the majority of people in Ireland wanted to be independent from the United Kingdom. This led to a war of independence, which ended with the creation of the Irish Free State in 1922. However, six counties in the north of the island, including Belfast and Londonderry, decided to remain part of the United Kingdom. This led to the creation of Northern Ireland. output: Imagine you get about 10 locked chests ( encrypted messages ) every day . Also imagine you have a cloning machine ( copy / paste ) and a set of keys ( keys ) . Whenever you get a locked chest , you try all your keys to see if any of them were for you . You clone them ( chests ) and you forward them to some other person ; Why ? Because everyone else also shares their unopened chests with you , and if you do n't do this , the chest delivery network wo n't work for long . Suppose if we wanted to secretly deliver the mail for our granny , we just make a chest that only granma can open . We then lump that in with all the other chests that look identical from the outside , and no one could tell I actually made that chest , and that it is going to my ( totally hardcore ) granny . --- So , we all share everyone 's messages with each other ... only they are encrypted and unreadable . We have some keys that we could try to see if any of them is for us . We try them and find out . We forward everything else to other people . We also get encrypted messages that other people have . Since the messages are actually thousands upon thousands every hour , and since we have many users around the globe , figuring out who created the messages is very very hard ( anonymity ) . Finding out who has the key to a given encrypted mail is nearly impossible . The whole network depends on each individual sharing and fowarding other people 's messages so that it does n't rely on each person too much . Taking out everyone using the network at the same time , would be the only way to take down the network .

input: Sure! The Monty Hall problem is a probability puzzle named after the host of the game show "Let's Make a Deal". It goes like this: Imagine you are a contestant on a game show, and the host (Monty Hall) shows you three doors. Behind one of the doors is a prize, and behind the other two doors are nothing. You get to choose one of the doors, and then Monty will open one of the other doors to reveal that it is empty. Finally, Monty will ask you if you want to stick with your original choice or switch to the remaining unopened door. Now here's the question: should you stick with your original choice or switch to the other door?, Intuitively, it might seem like it doesn't matter whether you stick with your original choice or switch, since there are only two doors left and the prize could be behind either one. But the surprising answer is that you should switch doors!, To see why, let's imagine that you choose door #1. There is a 1/3 chance that the prize is behind door #1 (since there are three doors in total and the prize is behind one of them), and a 2/3 chance that the prize is behind one of the other two doors. If the prize is behind door #1, then it doesn't matter whether you stick with your original choice or switch, since you've already chosen the correct door. But if the prize is behind one of the other two doors, then switching doors will give you the prize, while sticking with your original choice will not. So overall, there is a 2/3 chance that switching doors will give you the prize, while there is only a 1/3 chance that sticking with your original choice will give you the prize. That means that switching doors is the better choice in this situation. I hope that helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

input: Sleeping too much can actually make you feel tired because it can disrupt your body's natural sleep-wake cycle. When you sleep, your body goes through different stages of sleep, including deep sleep and REM (rapid eye movement) sleep. These stages are important for your physical and mental health, and they help you feel rested and refreshed when you wake up. When you oversleep, you may disrupt these stages and throw off your body's natural sleep-wake cycle, which can lead to feelings of fatigue. It's also possible that oversleeping may be a sign of an underlying health issue, such as a sleep disorder or depression, which can also contribute to feelings of fatigue. It's generally recommended to aim for 7-9 hours of sleep per night for adults to feel rested and refreshed.

input: AC stands for "alternating current," which means that the voltage or current in a circuit is constantly changing direction. The voltage in an AC circuit is often represented using RMS, or root mean square, because it provides a way to compare the average magnitude of the AC voltage to a DC voltage of the same value. Imagine that you have a toy that runs on batteries. If you were to measure the voltage of the batteries using a voltmeter, you would get a reading of the DC voltage. However, if you were to measure the voltage of the toy while it is running on AC power (like from a wall outlet), the voltage would be constantly changing. It might be positive for a while, then negative, then positive again. To compare the AC voltage to a DC voltage, we use RMS. RMS is a way to measure the average magnitude of an AC voltage over time. It's called the "root mean square" because it's calculated by taking the square root of the mean (average) of the squares of the values of the AC voltage over a specific period of time. For example, if you were to measure the AC voltage of your toy over a period of one second, you might get a series of values like this: 1 volt, 0 volts, -2 volts, 3 volts, -1 volt, The RMS value of this AC voltage would be calculated by taking the square of each value, finding the average of those squared values, and then taking the square root of that average. In this case, the RMS value would be about 1.8 volts. This allows us to compare the AC voltage to a DC voltage of the same value. For example, if the RMS value of an AC voltage is 120 volts, it has the same average magnitude as a DC voltage of 120 volts.

input: Sure! The Birthday Paradox is a phenomenon that occurs when you have a group of people, and you ask them what their birthdays are. You might expect that it would be uncommon for two people in the group to have the same birthday, but it turns out that it's actually quite common! Here's why: Imagine you have a group of just two people. The probability that they have the same birthday is very low, because there are 365 possible birthdays and only two people. But if you have a group of three people, the probability that at least two of them have the same birthday goes up. And if you have a group of four people, the probability goes up even more. As the size of the group gets larger and larger, the probability that at least two people have the same birthday gets closer and closer to 1 (which means it's almost certain to happen). In fact, if you have a group of just 23 people, there is a 50% chance that at least two of them have the same birthday! That's why it's called the "paradox" – because it seems counterintuitive that such a common event could happen with such a small group. So the next time you're in a group of people, don't be surprised if at least two of them share a birthday – it's actually pretty likely!

input: The Tea Party was a political movement that originated in the United States in the late 2000s. It was made up of people who were concerned about the size and scope of the federal government, and who believed that the government was overstepping its bounds in many areas. One of the main things that the Tea Party stood for was limited government and individual freedom. They believed that the government should not be involved in people's lives and should not have the power to regulate or control certain aspects of society. They also believed in the importance of personal responsibility and self-reliance, and opposed things like high taxes and big government programs. The Tea Party had a problem with what they saw as an increase in the size and power of the federal government, and with policies that they believed were infringing on individual freedom and personal responsibility. They opposed things like the Affordable Care Act (also known as "Obamacare") and government programs that they saw as wasteful or unnecessary. The Tea Party's ideal view of the country was one in which the government played a limited role in people's lives, and where individuals were free to make their own choices and decisions without interference from the government. They believed that this would lead to a stronger, more prosperous society.

input: The Treaty of Tripoli was a treaty signed between the United States and the Kingdom of Tripoli (now Libya) in 1796. The treaty stated that "the government of the United States of America is not, in any sense, founded on the Christian religion." This statement was included in the treaty to reassure the Muslims of Tripoli that the United States did not have any intention of trying to spread Christianity in their country. From a legal point of view, the treaty was a binding agreement between two countries, and the statement about the United States not being founded on the Christian religion was part of that agreement. However, this statement did not have any direct legal impact on the laws or policies of the United States related to religion. In terms of religion in public, the United States has a long tradition of separating church and state, which means that the government does not have the power to establish or endorse any particular religion. This means that people are free to practice any religion they choose, or no religion at all, without interference from the government. However, this does not mean that religion cannot be present in public at all. For example, it is common for people to display religious symbols, such as crosses or menorahs, on their private property, and it is also common for people to express their religious beliefs in public through words or actions. However, the government cannot endorse any particular religion or show favoritism towards one religion over another.

input: Sugar substitutes are substances that are used to sweeten food and drinks without adding calories or increasing blood sugar levels. They are used as a way to reduce the amount of sugar and calories in a person's diet, especially for people with diabetes or those trying to lose weight. Some sugar substitutes, like saccharin, are very sweet but don't have any calories because they are not broken down or absorbed by the body in the same way that sugar is. When you eat sugar, your body digests it and turns it into energy. But when you eat a sugar substitute, it passes through your body without being digested or absorbed, so it doesn't provide any calories or energy. So, sugar substitutes can taste sweet just like sugar, but they don't have the same effect on your body because they are not broken down and absorbed in the same way. This is why they are a good option for people who want to reduce their sugar intake or avoid adding extra calories to their diet.

input: Sure! Craps is a dice game played in a casino or at home. It can be a little complicated, but I'll try to explain it in a way that's easy to understand. In craps, players take turns rolling two dice. The person rolling the dice is called the "shooter." The shooter's goal is to roll a certain number, called the "point," before rolling a 7. To start the game, the shooter rolls the dice and the total of the two dice is called the "come-out roll." If the come-out roll is a 7 or an 11, the shooter wins and the game is over. If the come-out roll is a 2, 3, or 12, the shooter loses and the game is over. If the come-out roll is any other number, that number becomes the point. The shooter then keeps rolling the dice until they either roll the point again (in which case they win) or roll a 7 (in which case they lose). While the shooter is rolling, other players can bet on whether the shooter will win or lose. If the shooter wins, the players who bet on the shooter also win. If the shooter loses, the players who bet against the shooter win. That's the basic idea of craps! It can be a lot of fun to play, but it's important to remember that it's just a game and to gamble responsibly.

input:
When people fall backwards, they often throw their hands in the air as a natural reaction to try to regain their balance or protect themselves from falling. This is called the "extension reflex," and it is a reflexive action that occurs when the body senses that it is falling or losing balance. 
The reflex works like this: when the body senses that it is falling, it sends a signal to the muscles in the arms and hands to extend outward. This helps to stabilize the body and prevent it from falling too far. By throwing their hands in the air, people can often catch themselves and prevent themselves from falling all the way to the ground. 
So, when you see someone throw their hands in the air when they fall, it's not because they are trying to be dramatic or show off. It's just their body's natural way of trying to protect itself and keep from falling too hard.
output: Firstly , December 21st would be the " shortest day " in the Northern Hemisphere , not the longest . And it 's not . The dates of the longest and shortest days of the year change depending on your latitude . December 21st is the winter solstice -- which has to do with the position of the sun in the sky not the length of the day . Also , the winter solstice sometimes falls on the 22nd . Secondly , and to answer the question : it DOES get warmer immediately following the shortest day . However the air temperature that you experience in January and February is artificially cooler if you have land to the west of you . Mountains with snow pack , frozen lakes , and snow piles in parking lots all combine to steal heat from the air as they melt -- lowering the temperature in January and February . As evidence of this , look at the 100 - year median temperatures of Seattle and Los Angeles -- which upturn immediately after their respective " shortest days " . Because the Pacific does n't have frozen patches , it absorbs heat from the air uniformly . Thus the air temperature in those coastal cities are protected from the extremes and you can see on the graph that the coldest days are the shortest days . BTW , there are places on the Earth where for a few weeks a year there is no night at all . It 's called Midnight Sun .

output: In 1998 , a young game development studio called Valve released Half - Life . Half - Life is a first - person shooter about a scientist , Gordon Freeman , working on an experiment in teleportation that went horribly wrong , allowing alien monsters to enter the facility . Armed with his trusty crowbar and an increasing array of other weapons , he must close the rift and escape . The game was acclaimed for its gameplay and immersion , including the reasonably novel replacement of cut - scenes with scripted sequences where the player retained control of Gordon . In 2004 , Valve released Half - Life 2 . In the aftermath of the previous game , the world has been dominated by alien overlords known as the Combine . Gordon must evade the rulers of City 17 and eventually take the fight to them , leading a popular uprising against the invaders . In 2006 , Half - Life 2 : Episode 1 came out . Valve had decided to release smaller , less expensive " episodes " to continue the story in lieu of an all - in - one sequel . In Episode 1 , Gordon must sabotage the Combine citadel and escape the city before it is destroyed . In Episode 2 , released 2007 , he must make his way to a base outside the city and aid the resistance in preventing the Combine from bringing alien reinforcements . It ends in a dramatic cliff - hanger . Since 2007 , fans have been anxiously awaiting the release of Episode 3 . Valve more recently announced that they would be instead releasing a full sequel , Half - Life 3 . Valve has remained characteristically silent on the status of the game , but expectations are high . Gamers are willing to put a lot of faith in Valve because of the consistent quality of its releases . Valve has a history of extremely long dev cycles that culminate in excellent releases just about the time that everyone had given up . Team Fortress 2 , for instance , spent 9 years in development , and it was spoken of in the same breath as Starcraft : Ghost and Duke Nukem Forever . Unlike the former , it actually came out , and unlike the latter , it did n't suck . Over the years , given Valve 's silence , fans have tended to jump on rumors of the game 's imminent release . After so long , this has become a joke , where ordinary events are " interpreted " nonsensically as coded messages that Half - Life 3 's release is imminent .

input: Radio frequencies and telephone lines can be made secure by using encryption. Encryption is a way of scrambling the data that is transmitted over the radio frequency or telephone line so that it can't be understood by anyone who might intercept it. To make the data secure, a special "key" is used to encrypt the data before it is transmitted. The key is a secret code that is known only to the sender and the receiver of the data. When the data is received, it is decrypted using the key to turn it back into its original, unencrypted form. There are many different types of encryption that can be used, and they can be very complex. But the basic idea is that the data is transformed into a secret code that can only be understood by someone who has the key to unlock it. This helps to keep the data private and secure, even if it is transmitted over a radio frequency or telephone line that is not completely secure.
output: OK , most posts are helpful but IMHO not complete . You need a washing machine and knowledge of 2 main settings : * The first is a low temperature wash used for colours and delicate items . This is normally around 30 - 40 degree Celsius ( about 100 Fahrenheit ) . * The second is a high temperature wash used for white items and light bed linen . The hot temperatures help to get the bright white back and kill any bed bugs . This can be up to 90 degrees Celsius ( about 180 Fahrenheit ) but causes clothes to wear out quicker . You will need washing powder . Some are tablets , some you put in a ball which goes in the tumbler , some are just plain powders . I use powder as it 's cheap and easy ( like me ): * Biological Washing powder - Powerful cleaning enzymes so good for heavy stains . Bad though as it will cause colours to fade quicker , worse for the environment and some people can have allergic reactions . * Non - Biological power - More gentle on clothes , better for allergic types , environmentally friendly(ish ) , though not as powerful . Optional : * Fabric softener - This is a liquid that you pour into a special drawer / compartment of the washer . The machine puts this in after the wash so your clothes come out smelling lovely and feel softer to the touch . Do n't know how , it 's just magic . Great for shirts making them easier to iron . * Stain remover - These are often course powders that you can buy separately and contain powerful cleaning agents . Use sparingly . * * Step 1 * * - Sort your clothes into a colours pile and light / white items pile . Be careful with items that are white but have coloured parts / patterns . If in doubt chuck it in the colours pile . * * Step 2 * * - Put your selected pile of colours or whites in the machine . Check the setting : hot for whites , cool for colours . Put the washing powder and stain remover in together , fabric softener goes in a separate compartment . Double check the setting ! * * Step 3 * * - When the wash cycle is finished you want to decide where they go next sooner rather than later . If you leave wet clothes in the drum for a couple of days they go stale . If you want soft fluffy towels / bedding / underwear put them in a tumble dryer . If not save yourself the electricity and hang them up . It 's important not to tumble dry shirts as they become a nightmare to iron ! Hang them up whilst still wet !

The International Space Station (ISS) orbits the Earth in a path that is shaped like a slightly squashed circle, or an oval. This is because the ISS is being pulled towards the Earth by the Earth's gravity, but it is also moving very fast. When an object moves very fast, it is harder for gravity to pull it down, so it follows a curved path instead of falling straight down. 
To stay in orbit, the ISS has to keep moving at a specific speed and altitude. If it slows down or drops too low, it will fall back to Earth. If it speeds up or climbs too high, it will fly off into space. To maintain its orbit, the ISS uses thrusters, which are small engines that produce a jet of gas to push against. By firing the thrusters, the ISS can adjust its speed and direction to stay in its desired orbit. 
In the attached image, you can see a diagram of the ISS's orbit around the Earth. The curved line shows the path that the ISS follows as it orbits the Earth. The Earth is at the center of the diagram, and the ISS is shown as a small blue dot moving around it.

output: Before the Islamic Revolution , Iran was a dictatorship run by the shah ( the Persian equivalent of an emperor ) . The shah 's regime was pretty unpopular for a variety of reasons . One was that the regime was put in place after a coup in 1953 that ousted the popular democratically - elected Prime Minister Mosadegh and installed the shah as a dictator whereas before he was becoming more of a figurehead monarch , like in the UK . This coup was carried out with significant American and British support , and was done to maintain British control of Iran 's vast oil resources , because Mosadegh had plans to nationalize the oil companies . The shah 's regime was also unpopular because it was undemocratic and there was a lack of civil rights and liberties , including political prisoners and extrajudicial executions . The ayatollah Ali Khomeini had lived in exile for a long time because of his opposition to the regime , but he was popular with Iranians and after the initial stages of the revolution in 1979 , he returned to Iran and was greeted as a hero . A new government was formed and Iran 's government is very complicated . It has some democracy , but it also has a " Supreme Leader " who is not elected .

Dogs do sweat, but not in the same way that humans do. Humans have sweat glands all over their body, but dogs only have a few sweat glands on their paws and nose. The main way that dogs regulate their body temperature is by panting, which helps them to evaporate moisture from their tongues and mouth. 
The "dog smell" that you are referring to is actually produced by the oils and bacteria on a dog's skin and coat. Dogs have special glands on their skin that produce oils to help keep their coat healthy and shiny. These oils can have a strong, distinctive smell, especially if the dog is not regularly groomed or bathed. 
In addition to the oils on their skin, dogs can also have bacteria and other microorganisms on their skin and coat that can contribute to their overall smell. This is especially true if the dog has not been groomed or bathed in a while, or if they have been rolling around in something smelly. 
So, to summarize, the "dog smell" comes from the oils and bacteria on a dog's skin and coat, as well as any other substances that they may have come into contact with. It is important to regularly groom and bathe your dog to help keep their coat and skin healthy and to reduce any strong smells.

output: tl;dr : GIF is an image compression format , which is n't really suited well to compressing video data . Basically the only advantage is that browsers support the format natively without loading plugins . Actual videos compressed with video encoders are way more efficient . --- In an animated GIF , each frame of the animation is encoded as a separate file , and then the files are more or less just stuck together in the final animation . You can do something similar with JPEG ( Motion JPEG ) , although it 's not supported by browsers in quite the same way as GIFs . Also GIFs do a better job of compressing line art rather than photographs . Thus , making a video out of GIF images yields pretty poor compression anyhow . In an encoded video file , the video compressor takes into account that successive frames are ( probably ) related to each other . The first frame is encoded completely , in a similar ( but more advanced ) method as a JPEG might be . However , the next frame is typically encoded as just the difference from the first frame to the second . This is way more efficient for most videos where motion happens slowly . Say the video is of a ball falling . In an animated GIF , each frame is a standalone , complete picture of the ball and any surroundings . It 's compressed rather poorly , and a lot of redundant information is sent , so each individual frame is very large . In a video encoded with a video codec ( MPEG1 , MPEG2 , MPEG4 , h.264 , h,265 , etc ) , the first frame is compressed somewhat similarly to a JPEG . Then the second frame basically says " see the region of the first frame where the ball is ? Move that down a bit . Fill in the background with this information . " - This frame is * way * smaller and easier to transmit than the first frame . It 's also easier and faster to decode . There are some drawbacks to the video compression approach , but in general it does a way better job of representing the video data with way fewer bits . --- EDIT : Wow , this is getting a lot of attention :) I left out a lot of stuff to keep the answer simple , but I 'm getting pinged about a lot of the same things over and over , so here 's a few quick additions : - Yes , compression algorithm differences matter as well . I said something to this effect , but to extend : GIF uses a compression method that works well for color patterns , and video encoders use something that works better for photographs / live video . I did n't try to explain DCT / Quantization / entropy coding since that 's a super technical topic for ELI5 . - Yes , GIF can represent a simple delta between two frames . However , in the case of video data , it is rare that this is useful . Someone asked for a more detailed explanation of this , [ my response is here ] ( URL_1 ) . - Yes , there may be other factors at work besides the bitrate / coding issues . I think it is safe to say that GIF is a terrible video compression format , and that 's probably the root of many other issues . However , I have n't profiled browsers to see where they spend time decoding animated GIFs . I made a list of other places that could be inefficient [ here ] ( URL_0 ) . - Yeah , your " five year old did n't understand this " el - oh - el :P If my 7 - yr - old had the interest I 'd try to explain it to him and get his feedback , but I think he 'd rather play LEGO . --- EDIT 2 : A couple more things people are asking / mentioning : - Several people have mentioned HTML5 Video and WebM as alternatives to animated GIFs . There 's a lot of back - and - forth , legal issues , competitors refusing to agree and so forth that is really hampering the HTML5 video tag . [ Wikipedia has a long article on the subject . ] ( URL_2 ) We 've had better video technology than GIF for years and GIF is still going strong . I think we 're stuck with it , despite it being a terrible technology for the problem . ( See also : practically every other technology used on the web ) - A common question is " So does this mean that simpler videos with less movement will be encoded to smaller file sizes ? Or that complex or really random videos make larger or less efficient video files ? " The answer to this somewhat depends on the encoder settings , but in general the answer is yes . If you encode a simple video with little motion it will take less data than a more complex video with a lot of motion and random stuff going on . Modern video encoders are tuned towards encoding video sequences from cameras . They make assumptions based on what the real world looks like most of the time , and use those assumptions to compress more efficiently . They also take into account what our eyes are good at seeing .

Programming bugs, also known as software bugs, are mistakes that are made by programmers while writing code. These mistakes can cause the program to behave unexpectedly or not work at all. 
There are many ways that bugs can be introduced into a program. For example, a programmer might forget to include an important line of code, or might use the wrong type of data in a calculation. Sometimes, bugs are caused by mistakes in logic, where the programmer has written code that doesn't do what they intended it to do. 
It can be difficult to create something without bugs because programming is a complex task that involves many steps and requires attention to detail. It is easy to make mistakes when writing code, especially if the programmer is working on a large and complex program. In addition, it is often difficult to test all possible scenarios and combinations of inputs to a program, so it is possible for bugs to remain hidden until the program is being used in the real world. 
To reduce the number of bugs in a program, programmers often use techniques such as testing, debugging, and code reviews to identify and fix problems before the program is released. However, it is still possible for bugs to slip through and be discovered after the program is in use.

Sure! A car engine is a machine that converts fuel into energy. It does this by burning a mixture of fuel and air in a small, enclosed space called a cylinder. The energy produced by the burning fuel pushes a metal rod called a piston back and forth inside the cylinder. This movement turns a shaft, which is connected to the car's wheels and makes the car move. 
Cylinders are the tubes inside the engine where the fuel is burned and the pistons move. Most car engines have several cylinders, arranged in a line or in a "V" shape. The more cylinders an engine has, the more fuel it can burn at once and the more powerful it is. 
The size of an engine is usually measured in liters. This refers to the volume of the cylinders, or how much space there is inside them for the fuel to burn. A larger engine will have bigger cylinders and will be able to burn more fuel, which can make the car go faster. 
Horsepower is a unit of measurement for the power of an engine. It tells you how much work the engine can do in a certain amount of time. A car with a higher horsepower engine will be able to go faster and accelerate more quickly than a car with a lower horsepower engine.

input: There are many different conspiracy theories about the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks, and it is important to note that none of these theories have been supported by credible evidence. Here is a brief overview of some of the most common conspiracy theories about 9/11: The government was involved in the attacks: Some people believe that the U.S. government was somehow involved in the attacks, either by planning them or by allowing them to happen. There is no credible evidence to support this theory. The Twin Towers were brought down by controlled demolition: Some people believe that the Twin Towers were not destroyed by the impact of the airplanes and the resulting fires, but rather by explosives that were planted in the buildings. This theory is not supported by the available evidence, which shows that the towers were brought down by the combined effects of the impact and the fires. The Pentagon was not hit by a plane: Some people believe that the Pentagon was not hit by a plane on 9/11, and that the attack was staged or faked in some way. This theory is not supported by the available evidence, which includes eyewitness accounts, physical damage to the building, and wreckage from the plane that was recovered at the scene. The World Trade Center was not hit by airplanes: Some people believe that the World Trade Center was not hit by airplanes on 9/11, and that the attacks were faked in some way. This theory is not supported by the available evidence, which includes eyewitness accounts, video footage of the planes hitting the towers, and wreckage from the planes that was recovered at the scene. It is important to remember that conspiracy theories are not supported by evidence and should not be taken seriously. The best way to understand what happened on 9/11 is to rely on credible sources of information, such as news reports, official investigations, and eyewitness accounts.

Gravity is a force that attracts two objects with mass towards each other. It is what makes things fall down to the ground when you drop them. Gravity exists because of the mass of an object. The more mass an object has, the more gravity it has. This is why things with more mass, like the Earth, are able to attract smaller things, like a person standing on the ground. 
Imagine you have a ball and a feather. If you drop the ball and the feather at the same time, the ball will hit the ground first because it is heavier and has more mass. This is because the Earth's gravity is pulling the ball towards it faster than it is pulling the feather. 
Now, why does mass attract other mass? This is because all objects with mass have something called "gravitational force." Gravitational force is what causes objects with mass to be attracted to each other. The more mass an object has, the stronger its gravitational force is. This is why the Earth, which has a lot of mass, is able to pull smaller things towards it. 
So to sum it up, gravity is a force that exists because of the mass of an object. It causes objects with mass to be attracted to each other because of their gravitational force.
output: Words have lots of meanings . Fast means quick - moving , but also unmoving and secure , as in ' stuck fast ' . Deconstruction is pointing out how the other meanings of the words used in , say , a poem or book , actually go against the meanings intended by the author . One of the big ideas that came out of modernism and postmodernism is the idea of the ' subtext ' . Not just that there are things implied but not stated in the text , but a whole story that is very different from the story the author is trying to tell . Here 's an example : > Tommy was not a bad boy . What this means on the surface is that Tommy was n't a bad boy . But saying he was NOT a BAD boy is very different to saying that he WAS a GOOD boy . Just by saying that he is n't bad , we get the suggestion that he might at some point do something bad , even though he might not be in general a bad boy . In the end , the author 's choice of language has given us completely the opposite impression to the surface meaning of his words . Obviously this is a simple example . In fact , if a writer started a story with the line ' Tommy was not a bad boy ' he 'd probably want us to think that in the course of this story Tommy will do some very questionable things . But in a longer piece , picking out the opposite meanings of words can completely undermine what the writer wants to say . The point of deconstruction is to show that words are so full with meanings that it 's almost impossible to know that when I say ' fast ' and mean quick - moving , you could be thinking the complete opposite . By pulling apart the meanings of the words on the page you create another story that is completely at odds with the morals and beliefs of the writer . Hope that helps :)

input: The stock market is a place where people buy and sell shares of companies. When you buy a share of a company, you own a small part of that company. The stock market shows how much people are willing to pay for shares of different companies. One way to understand the stock market is to think of it as a big game. Just like in a game, there are winners and losers. Some people make money by buying shares of a company when the price is low and then selling them when the price is high. Other people lose money by buying shares when the price is high and then selling them when the price is low. To read the stock market, you need to look at a few different things: The price of a share: This is how much a single share of a company is worth. The price can go up or down depending on how well the company is doing and how much people are willing to pay for its shares. The volume of shares traded: This is how many shares of a company are being bought and sold in a given period of time. A high volume of shares traded can indicate that there is a lot of interest in the company. Market indices: These are lists of stocks that are grouped together based on certain characteristics, such as the type of industry they are in or the size of the companies. The most well-known market index is the S&P 500, which includes 500 of the largest publicly traded companies in the United States. Stock charts: These are graphs that show the price of a stock over a period of time. By looking at a stock chart, you can see how the price of a stock has changed in the past and get an idea of how it might change in the future. By understanding these things, you can get a better idea of how the stock market is doing and make informed decisions about buying and selling shares. However, it's important to remember that the stock market can be unpredictable and investing in stocks carries risks, so it's important to do your research and be cautious.
output: Throughout history , Christianity has been split on 2 different occasions . Catholicism was the major ( and basically only ) version of Christianity for hundreds of years [ debated , see replies to this comment ] , but in the early 11th century [ The Great Schism ] ( URL_1 ) divided Roman Catholicism in the west and Eastern Orthodox in the east [ ( visual example ) ] ( URL_2 ) . This split was over " theological disputes " such as " the issues of the source of the Holy Spirit ( " filioque " ) , whether leavened or unleavened bread should be used in the Eucharist , the Pope 's claim to universal jurisdiction , and the place of Constantinople in relation to the Pentarchy " . The schism can still be observed today in the fact that many Western European states still hold Catholic faiths and traditions , while the East Europe holds Orthodox faiths and traditions . The second split was in 1517 , called the [ Protestant Reformation ] ( URL_0 ) , was led by theological reformist Martin Luther against the ( at the time ) very corrupted Catholic Church . Luther nailed his [ Ninety - Five Theses ] ( URL_4 ) to a Catholic Church for all to read . The Ninety - Five Theses were basically a long list of things that was wrong with the Catholic Church , one example is having to [ pay to be forgiven by a priest ] ( URL_3 ) . His movement gained enough notoriety and support that his followers eventually started their own Protestant Church . In short , there 's a handful of differences in beliefs , but a majority of it is location . The hundreds of years of division has molded the countries it 's believers live in .
