In Christian theology, Hell is often described as a place of punishment for people who have chosen to reject God's love and grace and to live in a way that is opposed to His will. According to some Christian beliefs, Lucifer, also known as Satan, is the ruler of Hell and is responsible for punishing those who are sent there. 
It is important to note that the concept of Hell in Christian theology is not meant to be taken literally. Rather, it is often used as a metaphor to describe a state of separation from God and the consequences of choosing to live in opposition to His will. 
As for why God would allow people to be punished in Hell, it is important to remember that God is just and fair, and that He gives people the freedom to make their own choices. If someone chooses to reject God's love and to live in a way that is opposed to His will, then it is believed that they will face the consequences of their choices, including the possibility of being separated from God and experiencing punishment in Hell. 
However, it is also important to note that God's love and mercy are boundless, and that He is always willing to forgive those who turn to Him and ask for His forgiveness. Even if someone has made choices that have separated them from God, they can always turn to Him and ask for His forgiveness and be reconciled with Him.

input:
PGP (Pretty Good Privacy) is a way to encrypt and decrypt messages so that they can be sent securely over the internet. It works by using two keys: a public key and a private key. 
The public key is used to encrypt a message. It is called a "public" key because you can give it to anyone who wants to send you a message. When someone wants to send you a message, they use your public key to scramble it so that no one else can read it. 
The private key is used to decrypt the message. It is called a "private" key because you should keep it a secret and not share it with anyone. Only you should have access to your private key. When you receive an encrypted message, you use your private key to unscramble it and read the message. 
This is how PGP keeps your messages safe: even if someone else intercepts an encrypted message, they won't be able to read it unless they have your private key. Since you are the only one who should have your private key, only you will be able to read the message. 
So to summarize, PGP works by using two keys to encrypt and decrypt messages. It is considered safe because only the person who has the private key can read the message, even if someone else intercepts the encrypted message.

Prussia was a European country that existed from the 17th to the 20th century. It was located in northern Europe, on the eastern side of Germany. The name "Prussia" is not related to the country of Russia, but it does come from the same root word as "Prussia" which means "land by the sea." 
Prussia was originally a collection of separate states that were united under a single ruler. Over time, these states were gradually brought together and became one country. Prussia was known for its strong military and its efficient government, and it became an important power in Europe. 
In the late 1800s, Prussia became part of the newly formed country of Germany, along with other German-speaking states. This new country, called the German Empire, was led by the King of Prussia, who was also the Emperor of Germany. Prussia continued to exist as a state within Germany until the end of World War II, when it was dissolved and its territory was divided between Poland and the Soviet Union.

input: In the context of data privacy and security, first-party refers to a company or organization that is collecting or using data that it has directly collected from the individual or entity the data is about. Third-party refers to a company or organization that is collecting or using data that it has obtained from another source, rather than directly from the individual or entity the data is about. Second-party refers to a company or organization that is collecting or using data that it has obtained from another company or organization, rather than directly from the individual or entity the data is about. So, second-party data is similar to third-party data in that it is collected from a source other than the individual or entity the data is about, but it is different in that it is obtained from another company or organization rather than from a completely unrelated source. We don't hear about second-party data as much because it is less common than first-party or third-party data. However, it can be useful in certain situations, such as when two companies want to share data in order to improve their products or services.

input: In Chinese, each character represents a word or a concept. Some characters are simple and represent a single word, while others are more complex and made up of two or more smaller characters that represent different words or concepts. For example, the character for "tree" is a simple character that represents the word "tree." But the character for "forest" is made up of two smaller characters: one that represents the word "tree" and another that represents the word "many." So the character for "forest" means "many trees." To use characters in Chinese, you put them together to form sentences and paragraphs, just like you would with words in other languages. You can use different characters to show tense and verb endings, and you can also use particles to indicate things like time and place. So even though Chinese doesn't use hiragana like Japanese does, it still has its own set of rules and ways to form sentences and communicate ideas.

The United States national debt is the total amount of money that the federal government owes to its creditors. The debt is currently about $28 trillion and it is possible for it to be repaid, but it would require the government to make regular payments to its creditors to reduce the balance. 
There are a few ways that the government could make these payments: 
1. By running budget surpluses: This means that the government would bring in more money through taxes and other revenues than it spends on things like defense, education, and healthcare. The extra money could be used to pay down the debt. 
2. By selling assets: The government could sell things like land, buildings, or other assets to raise money to pay off the debt. 
3. By growing the economy: If the economy grows, the government will bring in more money through taxes, which could be used to pay down the debt. 
It is important to note that the national debt is not necessarily a bad thing. In fact, it can be a useful tool for the government to fund important projects and stimulate the economy. However, if the debt gets too large, it can become difficult for the government to make the necessary payments, which could lead to financial problems.

input: The United States has a two-party system because it has a winner-takes-all system for elections. This means that the political party that gets the most votes in an election wins all the seats in that election. This is different from many other countries, which have a proportional representation system, where each political party is awarded a number of seats based on the percentage of votes they receive. In a winner-takes-all system, it is difficult for a third party to win any seats because they are competing against the two main parties, which often have more resources and support. As a result, the two main parties, the Democrats and the Republicans, tend to dominate the political landscape in the United States. However, it is important to note that there are other political parties in the United States besides the Democrats and Republicans, and some of these parties do run candidates in elections. It is just that they tend to have less support and are not as successful at winning seats in the legislature.

input: Sure! A clutch is a part of a car's engine that helps the car switch between gears while driving. It's kind of like a switch that can turn off and on the power between the engine and the wheels. Imagine you are riding your bike and you want to go faster. To do that, you have to pedal harder, right? But if you pedal too hard, the bike will go too fast and it might be hard to control. The same thing happens in a car. The engine is like the pedals on a bike, and the gears are like different speeds you can choose. When you shift to a higher gear, it's like pedaling harder on your bike. But if you shift too quickly, the car might jerk or shake. The clutch helps make shifting gears smoother. When you press down on the clutch pedal, it "disconnects" the engine from the wheels, kind of like how you might take your foot off the pedals on your bike to coast. This makes it easier to shift gears without the car jerking or shaking. When you release the clutch pedal, the engine and the wheels are "connected" again, and the car starts moving. I hope that helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

input: Hydrogen doesn't turn into people directly. However, hydrogen is an important part of the process that allows living things, including people, to exist. Hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe, and it is found in many different places, including the sun and other stars. The sun is a giant ball of gas that is mostly made up of hydrogen and helium, and it gives off light and heat. This light and heat are what allow life to exist on Earth. On Earth, plants use the energy from the sun to turn carbon dioxide and water into food through a process called photosynthesis. When plants make food, they use the energy from the sun to combine hydrogen and oxygen atoms from water molecules to form glucose, which is a type of sugar. Glucose is a source of energy for plants, and it is also a source of energy for animals that eat plants. So, in a way, hydrogen is an important part of the process that allows living things, including people, to exist because it is used by plants to make food. When animals eat plants, they get the energy from the food that the plants made using hydrogen, and that energy is used to fuel their bodies and keep them alive.
output: Imagine you are on a big empty playground that only has merry - go - round in the middle of it . You tired of the merry - go - round so you invent a new game . You mark out and number squares all over the play ground and in each square you put a card with the number of some other square . The game is look at the card and move to the new square as indicated by the card . Now repeat . For the Mandelbrot Game you want to use very special numbering and directions for moving . First use two numbers , not just one number . The first number is the distance to the merry - go - round for that square , for the second number you can imagine someone on the merry - go - round pointing at both the school and at you standing on the square . Depending on the square , there will be an angle between their arms . The second number is that angle ( so one arm is always pointing at the school house , the other is swinging around pointing at you depending on the square . ) Now what do the directions say on them ? Just the double angle of the current square and square the distance to the merry - go - round . So if you start on square ( 3 , 30 degrees ) you move to ( 9 , 60 degrees ) . The angle doubles , and the distance squares . Maybe you are at ( 1/4 , 210 degrees ) , well now you move to ( 1/16 , 60 degrees ) . Notice that 360 degrees is all the way around , so if you double 210 degrees it is 420 degrees and that is 60 degrees more than a full circle , so you just go to the 1/16 card that has 60 degrees on it . OK , that is sort of the Mandelbrot Game , you square your distance , and double your angle and move to a new square . But we add a twist . We add a Standard Hop . The numbers for your first square have a distance and angle ( as there are on all the squares ) and you will remember those numbers for your standard hop . A standard hop says use your compass , and move that fixed distance by that fixed angle . The standard hop never changes ( it is standard ) , always the same distance , in the same direction . Now for the Mandelbrot game . Starting from the merry - go - round move to any square . That fixes your standard hop . Check the card for the new numbers . Get to the new square by doubling the angle and squaring the distance , but do NOT look at that card , first take a Standard hop , look at that card . The next new number to move to is the square of that distance and that double of the angle . When you get to the new square you do not use that card , you take a standard hop . Now you repeat the moves . Always look at the numbers for the new square AFTER the standard hop . So it is a repeating pattern of standard hop , look at card , square distance , double angle , move to new square , standard hop and keep so on . The Mandelbrot set is all the starting squares ( and , therefore , all the standard hops ) that keep you close to the merry - go - round . With all this squaring distances , double angles , and taking standard hops it should be no surprise that some starting points just get further and further away and never get close to the merry - go - round . Some might be close for a while and then wander off , others might always stay close . The Mandelbrot set is the ones that stay close .

input: There is no evolutionary explanation for the social pressure that some Western women feel to remove their body hair. The idea that women should remove their body hair has more to do with societal and cultural norms than with any biological or evolutionary considerations. In some Western cultures, it is considered more attractive for women to have smooth, hairless skin. However, this is not a universal standard, and there are many other cultures where body hair is not viewed as a problem or something that needs to be removed. Ultimately, the decision to remove body hair or not is a personal one and should be based on what makes a person feel comfortable and confident, rather than on societal expectations or pressure.

input: The high pitched ringing sound you sometimes get in your ears is called tinnitus. It is a condition that causes you to hear sounds that are not actually present in your environment. There are many things that can cause tinnitus, including loud noises, ear infections, certain medications, and age-related hearing loss. In some cases, tinnitus may be a symptom of a more serious underlying condition, such as a brain tumor or an aneurysm. If you are experiencing tinnitus, it is important to speak with a healthcare professional to determine the cause and find the appropriate treatment.

input:
Open-back headphones have a design where the ear cups are not sealed and allow sound to pass through them. This can give a more natural and open sound, as if you are listening to speakers in a room rather than headphones on your head. The sound from open-back headphones may also be more spacious and have a wider soundstage, which can be beneficial for certain types of music and audio recording. 
On the other hand, closed-back headphones have a design where the ear cups are sealed and do not allow sound to pass through them. This can provide a more isolated listening experience and can be helpful for blocking out external noise. Closed-back headphones are often preferred for noisy environments or for use in situations where you do not want others to hear what you are listening to. They are also often used for monitoring in recording studios, as they can help to prevent sound leakage into microphones. 
Both open-back and closed-back headphones have their own advantages and disadvantages, and which one is best for you will depend on your specific needs and preferences. Some people prefer open-back headphones for their natural sound, while others prefer closed-back headphones for their isolation and ability to block out external noise. Ultimately, it comes down to personal preference and the specific use case.

input: There are a few different ways that restaurants can play music. Some restaurants might use a music streaming service like Pandora or Spotify, which allows them to choose specific songs or playlists to play on a loop. Other restaurants might use a CD player or a portable music device like an iPod to play CDs or digital music files. Some restaurants might even have a special radio frequency that they use to play music, although this is less common. In general, the method that a restaurant uses to play music will depend on the size of the restaurant and its budget, as well as the preferences of the owners and employees.

input: Browsers are software programs that you can use to access and view websites on the internet. Most browsers are free to download and use, so they don't directly make money by charging people to use them. Instead, many browsers, like Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox, make money through advertising and partnerships. For example, Google might pay a company to show an advertisement in Chrome, or Mozilla might partner with a company to make their browser the default choice for that company's products. In addition, some browsers, like Chrome, offer additional services that users can pay for, such as cloud storage or a virtual private network (VPN). Overall, browsers make money by showing advertisements, partnering with companies, and offering paid services to users.
output: Do you want to know about the theory behind them , or about which ones are better in which situations and how to use them ? Video is a series of still images ( called * frames * ) played rapidly in a row . Most commonly , people will use 24 frames per second ( * 24fps * ) for video ; that 's 24 still images played quickly enough that it seems like smooth motion . Each of these images is made up from many tiny coloured squares , called * pixels * . Think of a [ mosaic art piece ] ( URL_0 ) ; the more pieces of mosaic -- pixels -- you use , the more detailed the image can be . The number of pixels used in an image is called its * resolution * . To illustrate the differences in resolution : a DVD has 345,600 pixels per frame , HDTV has around 921,000 , and Blu - ray has over 2 million . For every second of video you see on a Bluray , the player is putting 49.76 million tiny coloured squares on the screen in a specific order . That 's a * lot * of information . The player would have to say " Put a red square first . Then a blue square . Then a yellow square . Then a green square " to the TV almost 50 million times a second . When it 's speaking in plain English like that , just saying what squares go where , we call it * uncompressed video * . Uncompressed Blu - ray video takes up 149.3 megabytes per second ( without even counting audio ) . * Codecs * are our solution to that problem . They * encode * the video -- that is , they turn that list of " Put red square here " plain - English instructions into a special kind of code that 's a lot faster and takes less space to store . They will also change parts of the video to make it easier to describe in this code . One trick they might use is to keep non - moving portions of the screen static . Imagine your video is a Simpsons episode where Homer is mowing the lawn . Your uncompressed video , which does n't use a codec , will describe every single part of the scene 24 times per second , using a LOT of space . You use a clever codec that says " Oh , look -- * Homer and the lawnmower * are moving , but the house stays perfectly still in the background . We 'll describe the house once , and just say * keep that on the screen for 10 seconds * , so we do n't have to keep describing it over and over . " You 've saved a lot of space that way . Likewise , while your uncompressed video describes Homer 's face as " yellow pixel , then yellow pixel , then yellow pixel , then yellow pixel " over and over again a million times , your clever new codec would say " Repeat yellow pixel 500 times " , saving space . It might even say , depending on what codec you 've chosen and what settings it has , " That tree in the background is made up of 500 shades of green , but let 's trim that down to 300 shades , it wo n't look that much different but it 'll save some more space . " After enough of these tricks , your 900 GB video is down to 8 GB , small enough to put on a DVD . Why are there so many different codecs ? Well , it takes a lot of computer brainpower to process a video using an advanced codec . Back in 1990 , when computers were slow and computers were just starting out , they would 've made quite simple codecs that did n't take a million years for the computer to understand . Now in 2011 , because our computers are hundreds of times faster , we can write new codecs that employ very in - depth and complicated tricks . Our priorities have also changed -- now that we can stream videos online without worry , and now that we have 2 TB hard drives instead of 2 GB ones , we prefer to have big - but - pretty videos , while 1990s folks wanted to keep them as small as they possibly could . The codec used on DVDs is called MPEG-2 . MPEG-2 dates from 1996 , and was written with the goal of being decoded by cheap , low - power DVD players , so it 's not very thorough or clever . The codec used on Blu - ray , iTunes video and many online sites is called H.264 or AVC . It is much newer , and was written knowing that it would be used on the strong computers of the late 2000s , rather than low - power devices from the mid-90s . As such , it is much smarter and more powerful -- because it knows it 'll be played by powerful processors -- and provides a much higher quality of video . If you yourself are looking to encode video , the three most popular codec formats are * * MPEG-2 * * ( used only for DVDs nowadays ) , * * Xvid * * ( used in the AVI files you find online , good for making sure everyone can see it , or that it can be played on phones and other low - power devices ) and * * H.264 * * ( used for most online , Blu - ray , and HD videos ; the best choice in 95 % of cases ) . Now , as a footnote , let 's note what a * container format * is . Many people are confused by this . Containers are things like AVI , MKV , or MP4 . These file extensions do not tell you what codec was used to make the video . Think of them more like ZIP files . Most video files contain the video , one or two audio tracks , maybe some subtitles , and some chapters . Each of those things is a separate piece , in a different format , messily separated . So you put them together into a * container * , one file that contains them all , just like ZIP . The exact same video can be represented inside an AVI , an MKV , or an MP4 , but they have different features . For example , AVI is 20 years old , made specially for Windows , and only really supports one video with one piece of audio on the top -- but everyone can view it . MKV is more modern , and supports multiple viewing angles , chapters like a DVD , built - in subtitles , multiple audio tracks ( so you can embed director 's commentary or different languages ) , but because it 's relatively modern , older software does n't know how to deal with it . When you make a video , you choose what container you want based on your needs , but it has absolutely zero effect on the image quality , and it is very easy to change later . For example , today I made a file where the video format was H.264 , the audio format was MP3 , the subtitle format was SRT , and the container format was MKV . That 's an MKV file , but the * video * is n't MKV , it 's H.264 . MKV 's just the container I put all the stuff in .

input: Communism is a type of government and economic system where everyone in society shares everything equally. This means that all the wealth, property, and resources are owned by the community as a whole, rather than by individual people or businesses. Under communism, there is no private ownership, and the government controls the economy. The idea behind communism is that everyone works and contributes to the community, and in return, the community takes care of their basic needs, such as food, shelter, and healthcare. The goal of communism is to create a classless society where everyone is equal and no one is oppressed or exploited.

input: The Japanese bombing of Pearl Harbor, which occurred on December 7, 1941, was a surprise military attack by the Imperial Japanese Navy against the United States naval base at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii. The attack led to the United States entering World War II. There were several reasons why the Japanese decided to attack Pearl Harbor. One reason was that Japan was seeking to expand its empire and saw the United States as a potential obstacle to its ambitions. Japan also wanted to secure access to the natural resources of Southeast Asia, and believed that by attacking the United States, it could deter the United States from interfering with its plans. Another reason for the attack was that the United States had imposed economic sanctions on Japan in response to Japan's aggressive actions in China and Southeast Asia. These sanctions, which included a ban on the export of oil and other vital resources to Japan, were hurting Japan's economy and military capabilities. The Japanese government believed that by attacking the United States, it could force the United States to lift these sanctions and give Japan the resources it needed to continue its military expansion. In short, the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor was a strategic move motivated by a desire for empire and resources, and was also a response to the economic pressure being exerted on Japan by the United States.

input:
There are many factors that can contribute to a team's record fluctuating from year to year in the NFL. 
One reason is that each team has a roster of players, and the players on a team can change from year to year. For example, a team might lose some of their best players to injury or to other teams, or they might bring in new players through the draft or free agency. When a team has a lot of changes to their roster, it can take time for the new players to get used to playing together and for the team to find their rhythm. This can lead to a team's record fluctuating. 
Another reason is that each team plays a different schedule of games each year. One year, a team might have a lot of tough opponents and have a harder time winning games. The next year, they might have an easier schedule and have more success. 
There are also many other factors that can affect a team's performance, such as the coaching staff, the team's overall strategy and game plan, and the overall level of talent and depth on the team. All of these factors can contribute to a team's record fluctuating from year to year.

input:
Computers get slower over time because they start to accumulate clutter and junk files. These files take up space on the computer's hard drive and can slow down its performance. Additionally, as a computer gets older, it may not have the same processing power and speed as it did when it was new. 
Video game consoles, on the other hand, do not typically get slower over time. This is because they are designed specifically for running games, and they do not have as many background tasks or processes running as a typical computer. However, like any electronic device, video game consoles can break or malfunction due to wear and tear, or because of a hardware or software issue.

output: The email protocols that are generally used such as SMTP were written back when 10 MB was stupidly large . To download it on a typical home connection would take several hours , if not days . SMTP servers generally work by passing the email from one to another to another ( although there are now things in the address resolution protocols that allow them to talk directly to the receiving server for efficiency ) . Since the email servers that are ' out in the wild ' are of a wide range of ages and capabilities the limits of how big your attachments can be are set by the lowest limits in that chain , which generally used to be 10 MB . Nowadays it 's often higher than that but you ca n't tell without trying it , and next time your email may take a different route , so 10 MB is a ' safe ' lowest common denominator . Also , many corporate email servers find it convenient to leave the 10 MB limit in place even though they could easily change it because that reduces the amount of data they have to archive , backup or even keep for years for legal reasons . Having a 10 MB limit forces people to use other methods like SharePoint or shared folders , or offsite solutions like Dropbox if the business rules allow it .

input: It's not likely that Canada, the United States, and Mexico will adopt a common currency like the Euro in the near future. While it's true that having a common currency can have some benefits, such as making it easier for people and businesses to trade and do business with each other, there are also many challenges and considerations that would need to be addressed before a common currency could be implemented. For example, each country has its own central bank that is responsible for setting monetary policy, such as controlling the supply of money and setting interest rates. If the three countries were to adopt a common currency, they would need to agree on how to coordinate monetary policy and ensure that it is in the best interests of all three countries. Additionally, the economies of Canada, the United States, and Mexico are all quite different, so it would be difficult to determine what the exchange rate between the NAD and other currencies should be. This could cause problems for businesses that rely on exports and imports, as changes in the exchange rate could affect the cost of goods and services. Overall, while a common currency for North America could potentially bring some benefits, there are also many challenges that would need to be addressed in order to make it a success.
output: [ Speculation alert ] There 's probably an evolutionary benefit to being wary of having your back to an open area of darkness , since in the ancestral environment there was a non - trivial chance that in a ' going upstairs in the dark'-type situation , there actually is a ' monster ' ( predator of some kind ) waiting to strike . Having the feeling of something behind you means you avoid that kind of vulnerable situation more , you move more quickly , and you are more alert and ready to respond if there turns out to actually be a predator there . Evolution is not tailored for your comfort , but for your survival . It 's better to be nervous for no reason , than to get eaten because you felt you were safe .

input:
The numbers 11 and 12 are not considered to be part of the "teens" because the word "teen" refers to a number that is between 13 and 19. The word "teen" comes from the Old English word "ten," which means "ten." So, when we say "teens," we are referring to the numbers that are one "ten" (10) and nine more, which are 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, and 19. 
11 and 12 are not part of this group because they are not between 10 and 19. 11 is one more than 10, and 12 is two more than 10. They are not "teens" because they are not between 13 and 19. Instead, we say "eleven" and "twelve" to refer to these numbers.

input: Yes, the number .999999... (also known as "the repeating decimal" or "the decimal representation of 1") is the same as 1. This is because when you have an infinite series of 9s, no matter how many 9s there are, the value of the number will always be very close to, but slightly less than 1. However, as you add more and more 9s, the value of the number gets closer and closer to 1. So, even though the number is not exactly equal to 1, it is very, very close to 1, and in a sense, it is "the same as" 1. Here's an example to help you understand: Imagine you have a bag of candy and you want to give out all the candy to your friends, but you only have 9 pieces of candy. You can give each of your friends 1 piece of candy and still have 1 piece left over. However, if you had an infinite amount of candy (like in the number .999999...), you could give each of your friends 1 piece of candy and still have an infinite amount of candy left over. Even though you don't have exactly enough candy to give out to everyone, you have more than enough candy to give out to everyone, and that's what makes it "the same as" having enough candy. input: Darknet is a type of network that is not accessible through normal means. It is often used for illegal activities, such as buying and selling drugs or weapons, or for sharing stolen information. To access the darknet, you need to use special software called a "darknet browser." These browsers allow you to visit websites that are not indexed by search engines like Google, and that are only accessible through the darknet. The regular internet, also known as the "clearweb," is what most people use every day to access websites, send emails, and do other online activities. It is called the clearweb because it is easy to access and is not hidden. The darknet is different from the clearweb because it is not as easy to access, and because the activities that take place on it are often illegal or secretive. It is called the "dark" net because it is hidden and not easily accessible to most people.
output: /u / Nygmus has the cause very solidly explained , but the reason why goes a little deeper . When you purchase a pre - order , you 're giving the game developer money for a promise of delivered content . Many times , pre - order sales are driven by trailers and E3 presentations that create a certain set of expectations for the consumer . However , the developer is under no obligation to meet those expectations when they deliver the final product . Watch_Dogs is an excellent example of showing customers one thing to convince them to purchase pre - orders , and then ultimately delivering something that is less than what was shown . They can do this without consequence , because they already secured pre - order sales . By the time the game launched and people started complaining about the graphics being lower quality than what was shown at E3 , and the gameplay being rather generic instead of the groundbreaking experience that was promised , Ubisoft had already secured a heavy influx of sales revenue ... from pre - orders . The push to stop people from pre - ordering is to force developers to actually face the consequences of launching under - developed games . Assassin 's Creed Unity was a disaster at launch , but Ubisoft did n't face any real consequences for such a terrible release ... they already profited from the pre - order sales . Before pre - orders were so common , a game studio that attempted to launch a game infested with so many bugs would have suffered immeasurable losses , and either gone under or emerged from the experience with a powerful lesson learned . As /u / Nygmus mentioned in the case of Assassin 's Creed Unity , the blackout for reviews allowed Ubisoft to sell a broken game to a large number of people . But let 's ignore reviewers as they get early - access copies regardless of whether pre - ordering is a thing . If no one pre - orders a new game , that game 's sales figures will rely entirely on the game 's merit . If the game is good , you 'll hear about it and can go pick it up . If its bad , you 'll hear about that , and can save your money for something else . And that 's the core of it . By not pre - ordering , we are removing the cushion of revenue protection from developers . Without that cushion , they can not risk releasing a game that under performs . This will encourage some developers to exercise more quality control before releasing a game . It will also have the side - effect of causing some developers to just cancel projects that seem too risky . As a whole , its still a net benefit for the consumer . 2014 was a year where a lot of people gave up a lot of money and ended up being given an unsatisfying experience in return . The only way that consumers can influence developers is through their pocketbooks . And the only way this kind of behavior from developers will end , is if they ca n't financially get away with it . Hence , no more pre - orders . EDIT : I should clarify that in my train of thought above , I use the term developers as a rather loose blanket encapsulation for both developers and publishers . Publishers are usually the ones guilty of the above practices I mention , but developers can still be held responsible , and in some cases are even the ones driving the pre - order trend forward . I think both should be held equally accountable . And for what its worth , Indie developers are n't exempt ... early - access could have been a great tool for indie 's to polish and shape their creations into a profitable consumer - oriented product . But 2014 was determine to make early - access as nasty and filthy a word in this community as DRM and Day 1 DLC . EDIT2 : Oh wow , this frickin ' exploded completely unexpectedly ! I 'll do my best to respond to each of you as I can through the day , and thank you kind stranger for the Gold !

input:
Sure! Converting a number from binary to decimal is actually pretty easy. Here's how to do it: 
1. Write down the binary number. 
2. Starting from the rightmost digit, assign the value of 0 to the rightmost digit, 1 to the second rightmost digit, 2 to the third rightmost digit, and so on. 
3. For each digit, multiply the value of that digit by the corresponding power of 2. For example, the value of the rightmost digit (which is 0 or 1) should be multiplied by 2 to the power of 0, the value of the second rightmost digit should be multiplied by 2 to the power of 1, and so on. 
4. Add up all of the products from step 3 to get the decimal equivalent of the binary number. 
Here's an example: let's convert the binary number 1011 (which is 11 in decimal) to decimal. 
1. Write down the binary number: 1011 
2. Assign values to each digit: 1 * 2^3 + 0 * 2^2 + 1 * 2^1 + 1 * 2^0 
3. Calculate the products: 8 + 0 + 2 + 1 = 11 
4. Add up the products: 11 
So the decimal equivalent of 1011 is 11. 
I hope that helps! Let me know if you have any questions.

input: Fermat's Last Theorem is a famous math problem that states that there are no whole numbers (also called integers) that can be used to solve the equation x^n + y^n = z^n for any value of n greater than 2. For example, the equation x^3 + y^3 = z^3 has no solutions in whole numbers. Fermat's Last Theorem is called a theorem because it has been proven to be true. It was first proposed by the French mathematician Pierre de Fermat in the 17th century, but it wasn't until 1994 that a mathematician named Andrew Wiles was able to provide a complete proof of the theorem. The proof of Fermat's Last Theorem is quite complex and requires a lot of advanced math. However, there is a simple way to understand why the theorem is true: if we try to find whole number solutions to the equation x^n + y^n = z^n for any value of n greater than 2, we quickly run into problems. For example, if we try to find whole number solutions to the equation x^3 + y^3 = z^3, we can quickly see that there are no solutions. This is because no matter what values we choose for x, y, and z, the left side of the equation (x^3 + y^3) will always be a multiple of 3 (since 3 is a factor of every whole number), while the right side of the equation (z^3) will never be a multiple of 3. This means that the equation can never be true, no matter what values we choose for x, y, and z. In conclusion, Fermat's Last Theorem states that there are no whole number solutions to the equation x^n + y^n = z^n for any value of n greater than 2. This theorem was famously difficult to prove, but it has now been proven to be true by mathematician Andrew Wiles.

China is a country in Asia that is governed by the Communist Party of China, which is a political party that has been in power since 1949. The Communist Party of China is the highest authority in the government and the country's political system. 
The President of China is the head of state and is elected by the National People's Congress, which is the highest state body in China. The Premier of China is the head of government and is appointed by the President with the approval of the National People's Congress. The Vice Premier of China is also appointed by the President with the approval of the National People's Congress. 
The President, Premier, and Vice Premier are responsible for running the government and making important decisions that affect the country. They work with other government officials and departments to make sure that the government is working well and that the needs of the people are being met. 
The Communist Party of China has a lot of influence over the government and the country's political system. The Party has control over who is elected or appointed to important positions in the government, and it also has the final say on important decisions that are made by the government. 
The legislature in China is the National People's Congress, which is made up of representatives from all parts of the country. The National People's Congress is responsible for making laws and overseeing the work of the government. 
There are also other governing bodies in China, such as the State Council, which is made up of the Premier and other government officials, and the Supreme People's Court, which is the highest court in the country. 
Overall, China is a country that is governed by the Communist Party of China, with the President, Premier, and Vice Premier leading the government and making important decisions. The National People's Congress and other governing bodies also play important roles in the country's political system.

Filibustering can be used to try to stop a new law or rule from being passed, because the politicians who are filibustering don't agree with it.
output: [ souirce ] ( URL_0 ) > Fighter jets are equipped with radar warning receivers , which are basically antennas that detect electromagnetic waves emitted by radar . By checking the wavelength and patterns of the emissions , the computer in charge of the system can give a reasonable guess as to what model of radar is being detected ( and therefore , what aircraft ) . It can also identify emitters on active radar guided missiles , and therefore provide some warning if such a missile is incoming . > The system can also detect when the emissions are part of a sweep of a larger part of the sky , or when the radar is emitting a focused beam . The latter means that the other aircraft decided to " lock on , " and their radar antenna is constantly tracking that one target instead of sweeping the sky in front of the aircraft . ELI5 / TLDR : Radar gives off certain clues ( electromagnetic waves ) and therefore can be used to identify the signal

output: Hey , US Army Infantryman who is actually in Afghanistan right now here . In an Infantry platoon it is the Platoon Sergeant 's job to maintain accountability of all men , weapons , and equipment . He does this by maintaining a MAL ( master authorization list ) given to him by the Company 's Armorer when weapons are signed out from the arms room . When a Soldier is injured or killed during combat operations he / she is put onto a MEDEVAC ( medical evacuation ) vehicle , whether it be a helicopter or some form of ground transportation . Prior to the Soldier being loaded all of his equipment is stripped off of him / her and retained by the unit . If the Soldier carried a crucial casualty producing weapon , like a machine gun with a higher rate of fire than the standard M4 , it will be given to the next most qualified person along with all the ammo . This is to try and maintain the combat effectiveness , AKA how many bullets you can throw at people , despite having less people . As for the gear , after it is recovered back to a FOB ( Forward Operating Base ) all gear that was worn by a casualty is inspected by people certified to determine whether it is serviceable . If an item is not serviceable and the casualty is still alive the inspectors will make an effort to send the item to the casualty 's home . It is not uncommon for Soldiers who received gunshots to their helmets to get to keep those for life . Enemy equipment is another story . In a perfect scenario where we eliminate the threat and have time to investigate any enemy equipment is gathered by a TSE team ( Tactical Site Exploitation ) on the spot and processed similar to a crime scene . Bagged , tagged , photographed , and turned into Military Intelligence for processing so they can determine ways to fight better against the enemy 's weapons and equipment . In other situations enemy equipment is destroyed on the spot to permanently prevent the enemy from using that against us . TL;DR Platoon Sergeant is responsible for recovering friendly equipment to the best of his abilities and repaired as quickly as possible . Enemy equipment is analyzed by Military intelligence or destroyed on the spot .

input: There is some evidence to suggest that diet drinks may not be as healthy as some people think. Caffeine: Some diet drinks, such as diet soda, contain caffeine, which is a stimulant that can have negative effects on the body if consumed in large amounts. Overall, it's important to be mindful of what you're drinking and to make sure you're getting enough water in your diet. input: Triglycerides are a type of fat that is found in your blood. They are made up of three fatty acids attached to a glycerol molecule. Triglycerides are an important source of energy for your body, but it's important to have them at healthy levels. If you have high levels of triglycerides in your blood, it can increase your risk for certain health problems. High triglycerides can increase your risk for heart disease and stroke, as well as other problems such as pancreatitis and fatty liver disease. There are several things that can contribute to high triglycerides, such as eating too much sugar and unhealthy fats, not getting enough exercise, and being overweight. If you have high triglycerides, your doctor may recommend making lifestyle changes, such as eating a healthier diet and getting more exercise, to help lower your triglyceride levels. They may also prescribe medication to help lower your triglycerides if needed. It's important to work with your doctor to keep your triglyceride levels within a healthy range. This can help you reduce your risk for health problems and live a healthier life.

input: Socialism and liberalism are two different political ideologies that have different ideas about how society and the government should function. Socialism is a political ideology that believes in the idea of the government owning and controlling the means of production and distribution of goods and services. The goal of socialism is to reduce inequality and provide more equal opportunities for everyone. Some upsides of socialism include the idea that it can provide a safety net for those in need and ensure that basic needs are met for all members of society. Some downsides of socialism include the idea that it can lead to less economic efficiency and less individual freedom. Liberalism is a political ideology that believes in the idea of individual freedom and the protection of individual rights. Liberalism emphasizes the importance of free markets, individual liberty, and limited government intervention in people's lives. Some upsides of liberalism include the idea that it can lead to economic growth and innovation, and that it allows for individual freedom and choice. Some downsides of liberalism include the idea that it can lead to greater inequality and that it may not adequately address social issues. In short, socialism is an ideology that focuses on equal opportunities and the government providing for the needs of the people, while liberalism is an ideology that focuses on individual freedom and limited government intervention.

input:
Déjà vu is a phenomenon that occurs when a person has a strong feeling or belief that they have experienced something before, even though they are certain that they have not. It's like a glitch in the brain where something seems familiar, but you can't place where you've seen or experienced it before. It can be a confusing and strange feeling, but it's actually quite common and is experienced by many people at some point in their lives. 
There are a few different theories about why déjà vu happens, but nobody is really sure. One theory is that it might be caused by a small delay in the brain's processing of new information. When we see or experience something new, our brains have to process it and store it in our memories. Sometimes, there might be a small delay in this process, which could lead to the feeling of déjà vu. 
Another theory is that déjà vu might be related to our memories and the way that our brains store and retrieve them. Sometimes, our brains might mix up or confuse different memories, leading to the feeling that we have experienced something before when we actually haven't. 
There's still a lot that we don't know about déjà vu, but it's generally considered to be a normal and harmless experience. If you ever have déjà vu and it's causing you stress or concern, you can talk to a doctor or mental health professional for more information and support.

input: Cold fusion is a type of nuclear reaction that happens at low temperatures and high pressures. It is a way to create energy by fusing two light atomic nuclei together, just like in the sun. In cold fusion, two hydrogen atoms are squeezed together with a lot of pressure, and they fuse together to form a heavier atom, like helium. This process releases a lot of energy, which can be used to generate electricity. Practical uses for cold fusion could include generating electricity for homes, businesses, and vehicles. It could also be used to power space rockets and other vehicles that need a lot of energy to function. One of the main benefits of cold fusion is that it produces very little pollution or waste, compared to other forms of energy generation. It could also be a potentially unlimited source of energy, since hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe. However, cold fusion is still a very new and experimental technology, and scientists are still working on finding ways to make it work more reliably and efficiently. So it is not yet a practical source of energy on a large scale.

output: It 's not a sixth sense ; it 's one of your five senses . It 's your hearing . Even the most quiet room has small amounts of ambient noise ( in film , they even have a name for it , called " room tone " or " presence " ) . Our brains are evolved to detect the direction of sound and how it " wraps " around us . When someone is near you , their presence disrupts the subtle sound reflections , causing a stereophonic " hole " . The room tone will sound slightly different to each ear , having the brain sense something is there in that hole , with 3D precision . Bats use a more evolved version of this to see in the dark . They make chirps and hear the reflections to determine their surroundings . Blind people tapping their canes can " see " by using two senses : touch ( feeling the vibrations in the cane ) , and hearing ( noting the sound of the cane sound , but also how the reflected echo sounds ) . There are a few reasons why it might be cheaper to buy a flight ticket from a travel agent rather than directly from the airline. 
One reason is that travel agents often have access to discounted rates that are not available to the general public. These discounts can come from various sources, such as bulk purchasing agreements with airlines or partnerships with hotels and other travel providers. By buying through a travel agent, you may be able to take advantage of these discounts and get a lower price on your flight. 
Another reason is that travel agents may be able to bundle your flight with other travel services, such as a hotel room or car rental, which can result in a lower overall price. This can be especially useful if you are planning a multi-destination trip or need to make multiple bookings for a group of people. 
Finally, travel agents may also be able to offer additional services and support, such as helping you choose the best flight options or assisting with rebooking or cancellations. These added services can be worth the additional cost for some travelers, even if it means paying a slightly higher price for the flight ticket. 
Overall, it's worth considering both options – buying directly from the airline and buying through a travel agent – and comparing the prices and services offered to find the best deal for your specific travel needs.

input: Cancer is a disease that occurs when cells in the body start growing out of control. Normally, our bodies are made up of many different types of cells, and they all have specific jobs to do. But when a group of cells starts growing and multiplying too quickly, it can form a tumor. Tumors can be cancerous or noncancerous. Cancerous tumors, also called malignant tumors, can spread to other parts of the body and cause serious problems. Noncancerous tumors, also called benign tumors, usually don't spread and aren't usually life-threatening. As for why we haven't cured cancer yet, it's because it's a very complex disease and there are many different types of cancer. Each type of cancer behaves differently and requires different treatments, so it's difficult to develop a one-size-fits-all cure. Scientists and doctors are constantly working on new treatments and ways to improve survival rates for people with cancer, but it's a challenging problem to solve. As for a person's chances of survival, it really depends on the type of cancer they have and how advanced it is. Some types of cancer can be cured if they are caught early, while others are more difficult to treat. In general, the earlier cancer is found and treated, the better the chances of survival. However, it's important to note that every person and every case is different, so it's hard to predict someone's chances of survival with any certainty.

Planes with multiple wings, like the one in the image you provided, are called "multiplane" or "sesquiplane" designs. These designs were experimented with in the early days of aviation, but they were not widely used because they did not perform as well as planes with fewer wings. 
There are several reasons why planes with multiple wings are not typically used. One reason is that they are more complex and expensive to build than planes with fewer wings. They also tend to be heavier and less efficient, which means that they require more power to fly and use more fuel. 
Planes with two wings, or "biplanes," were the most common design in the early days of aviation. They are still used today in some specialty applications, such as crop dusting or airshows. Most modern planes, however, have a single, long wing called a "monoplane." This design is more aerodynamic and efficient, and it allows the plane to fly faster and further than a biplane. 
Four-winged planes, or "quadruplanes," have also been experimented with, but they have not been widely used because they do not offer significant advantages over other designs. 
Overall, the number of wings on an airplane is determined by a combination of factors, including the size and weight of the plane, the type of mission it will be used for, and the desired performance characteristics. Engineers and designers carefully consider these factors when designing a new plane to ensure that it is safe, efficient, and capable of meeting the needs of its intended use.

input: Torque is a measure of how much force a car's engine can produce to turn the wheels. It's what makes a car able to accelerate and climb hills. Imagine you have a wrench and you're trying to loosen a bolt. If you push really hard on the handle of the wrench, you'll be able to turn the bolt more easily. That's because you're applying a lot of force, or torque, to the bolt. Now imagine you have a car with a really powerful engine. When you step on the gas pedal, the engine starts turning faster and producing more force, or torque. This torque is used to turn the wheels of the car, which makes the car go faster. So, in general, a car with a lot of torque will be able to accelerate quickly and climb hills more easily, while a car with less torque might struggle a bit in those situations.
output: Here 's an attempt at a LI5 answer which says more than " most upvotes in littlest time . " Imagine a racetrack for horses that goes on forever in one direction . The start of the track is labeled " Jan 1 , 1970 12:00am " and there are equally spaced notches labeled " Jan 1 , 1970 12:01am " , " Jan 1 , 1970 12:02am " , and so on . When you submit a post , a horse representative is dropped onto the racetrack at the time of submission . If I submit a post now , it will be dropped at the marker reading " Aug 15 , 2011 1:02am . " Now hopefully , my horse will start running ! Every time my post gets an upvote , it gets a little burst of energy and moves forward . Every time my post gets a downvote , it trips up and loses some distance . If it does n't get much attention , it 'll stay around the marker of the time it was submitted . But if it gets lots of upvotes , it will start to race faster and faster , even past the horses that are being dropped onto the track upstream for newer posts ! * * The front page of r / all is a top 25 list of the horses currently winning this race . * * The front page of a given subreddit is the top 25 list of the horses * from that subreddit * currently in the lead . Even the very best submissions will stop getting upvotes after a while , so the horse will start to slow down and eventually come to a stop . Even if it ran out way in front of the other horses , newer horses will have the advantage of being dropped further along the racetrack , so even if my post got a whole ton of upvotes in its lifetime , it wo n't stay in the top 25 for much longer than a couple hours . To make up for this , there 's also the " top all time " option , which lists the horses who traveled the longest distance , so the time they were submitted plays no role anymore .

input: The Getting Things Done (GTD) method is a way to organize your tasks and responsibilities in a way that helps you feel more productive and in control of your life. It's a system that helps you figure out what you need to do, when you need to do it, and how you can do it most effectively. Here's how it works: Capture: The first step in the GTD method is to capture everything that you need to do. This includes writing down all of your tasks, appointments, and ideas, so that you have a clear picture of everything that you need to take care of. Clarify: The next step is to clarify what each of these tasks means and what the next action is. This helps you figure out what you need to do next and how you can move forward with your work. Organize: After you've captured and clarified your tasks, the next step is to organize them. This might involve creating lists or using tools like calendars or to-do apps to keep track of everything. Reflect: The GTD method also involves regularly taking time to reflect on your progress and adjust your system as needed. This might involve reviewing your lists and tasks, moving things around, or adding new tasks as you think of them. Engage: The final step is to engage with your tasks and start working on them. This might involve setting aside specific blocks of time to focus on your work, or breaking larger tasks into smaller pieces and tackling them one at a time. People might choose to use the GTD method because it helps them stay organized and on top of their tasks, which can be especially helpful if they have a lot of responsibilities or if they struggle with procrastination. It's also a flexible system that can be customized to fit each person's unique needs and preferences.
output: Ok , Twitter is I guess similar to facebook , but instead of posting things to be read by your friends , you 're posting things that can be read by anybody in the world . Some people do use twitter like facebook and just tell you when they 've had a nice shower , or that they hate haters , or whatever people post about on facebook . These people , I find , are pretty boring . On twitter , you do n't have ‘ friends ’ . You have followers and people you follow . In other words , no one has to request or accept anything to read what you 're posting . They just click follow and their feed will be full of messages from you . This is great because you can follow genuinely interesting people and groups easily . Who do I follow on twitter ? I follow talented comedians who write and post jokes on twitter . I follow newsrooms who keep me updated with what is going on in the world . I follow news critics who criticise those newsrooms . As a computer gamer , I follow Steam Support , who let me know when Steam will be down for maintenance . I follow QI who give me interesting trivia facts , and I follow my favourite musicians to hear about their latest recordings and shows . I also follow , out of a sense of obligation , some of my friends who post * far * too often . Tweets themselves are limited to 140 characters , so brevity is important . When you tweet , you can post a link and twitter will automatically shorten the link for you . There are also things called hashtags , which is where you post a word like # this . You 've probably seen people do that before . What hashtags do is they make searching for tweets really easy . Let 's say you wanted to find out about a currently interesting thing , like Stop Online Piracy Act . People who are tweeting about this act might add # StopOnlinePiracyAct to their tweet . Now anyone can easily search # StopOnlinePiracyAct and get all the tweets which include this . Also , in twitter , the hashtagged word becomes a link - clicking on it will show all the other tweets with that topic . There is are also ' mentions ' . A mention looks like @myfriendharry . If you want to mention another twitter user in a tweet , putting a @ symbol before their name will turn their name into a link to their profile . The person who has been mentioned will be informed of this . There are two special kinds of mention . One is called a reply , and it should be pretty obvious what that is . If someone tweets something which you wish to publicly respond to , clicking reply on their tweet will create a mention which links specifically to the tweet you 're replying to . The other kind is a private message . Private messages can only be sent between people who are following each other . You can not just send private messages to celebrities ! Finally , there is re - tweeting . If someone makes a great post and you think your followers will like it , or a news story that needs spreading , clicking re - tweet will post that tweet to your profile while giving all credit to the original author . Tweets are not limited to just words . You can post images as well . You should try it out , search for some things that are interesting to you . If you have a good mobile phone , you can usually get twitter for it , which can be a nice way to get news . Also , tweets can be updated via text message if you wish .

input: Falling asleep is not something that you can just control on command. It is a natural process that occurs when your body is ready to rest and relax. There are many different factors that can affect your ability to fall asleep, such as how tired you are, how comfortable your environment is, and whether or not you have any stress or anxiety. To help yourself fall asleep, it's important to create a relaxing bedtime routine and a comfortable sleep environment. This might include things like reading a book, taking a warm bath, or practicing deep breathing or meditation. It's also important to try to avoid screens (like phones or TVs) for at least an hour before bed, because the blue light from these devices can make it harder for your body to relax. If you are still having trouble falling asleep after trying these things, it might be a good idea to talk to a doctor or a sleep specialist. They can help you figure out what might be causing your sleep problems and suggest other things you can try.
output: While reducing volume of the trash is novel , it 's not economically viable in most places on Earth because we 're not hurting for the space . But that 's not why we do n't do it . Sweden BUY 'S trash from surrounding countries because they 're so efficient at recycling and trash management , and incinerators are a significant part of their energy production . Incinerators have a substantial startup and operation cost . It obviously only makes sense if it 's going to net a profit . Dumping trash in places with the space to do it is pretty damn cheap , so they have to contend with that , and energy production can itself be pretty cheap , so an incinerator is competing on two financial fronts . While it 's probably the eco friendly and responsible thing to do , markets are n't driven by what is moral and ethical , no , they 're dragged kicking , clawing , and screaming , after dishing out a few punches . They 're driven by what makes investors the most money . As for the punches , a local power company successfully waged a slander campaign against an incinerator development near me , some 25 years ago , and the facility is abandoned , having never been fired .

input: Sure! Here's a rundown of some common pain medications and what they're typically used for: Tylenol (also known as acetaminophen) is a pain reliever and fever reducer. It's generally considered safe for most people, but it can cause stomach irritation and ulcers in some cases, so it's important to follow the recommended dose. Aspirin is another NSAID. It's often used to treat headaches, muscle aches, and other types of pain, as well as inflammation and fever. It's also sometimes used to help prevent heart attacks and strokes. Aspirin can cause stomach irritation and ulcers in some people, so it's important to follow the recommended dose. Excedrin is a combination medication that contains acetaminophen, aspirin, and caffeine. It's often used to treat migraines, as well as other types of headache. The caffeine in Excedrin can help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which may help to relieve headache pain. Advil (also known as ibuprofen) is another pain reliever and NSAID. It's similar to Motrin in that it helps to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever. It's often used to treat headaches, muscle aches, and other types of pain, as well as inflammation and fever. Like Motrin, it can cause stomach irritation and ulcers in some cases, so it's important to follow the recommended dose. Overall, these medications can be helpful in relieving pain and reducing inflammation, but it's important to follow the recommended dose and to talk to a doctor or pharmacist if you have any questions or concerns.

input: Water doesn't really have a taste of its own. It doesn't taste sweet like sugar, or salty like salt, or sour like lemon juice. This is because water doesn't contain any of the chemicals that give other things their own specific flavors. Instead, water is just a liquid that helps to carry other flavors to your tongue. For example, if you drink a glass of water that has a slice of lemon in it, you might taste a hint of lemon flavor. This is because the lemon has released some of its own chemical compounds into the water, which your tongue can then sense. But without any other flavors added to it, water itself doesn't have much of a taste. Water is important to our bodies because it helps to keep us hydrated and healthy. It's also a great way to clean our bodies and wash away any unwanted flavors or smells. So even though water might not have a strong taste of its own, it's still an important part of our lives!

input: Speed limits are enforced by aircraft by using specialized cameras and radar equipment mounted on planes that fly over the roads. input:
Uninstalling an application and deleting it are two different things. When you delete an application, you are simply removing the app from your device. The files that the app created on your device will also be deleted. This means that the app will no longer be visible on your device and you won't be able to use it. 
On the other hand, when you uninstall an application, the app is not only removed from your device, but any system files and settings associated with the app are also removed. This includes things like registry entries and configuration files. An uninstaller is a program that helps you uninstall an application from your device by removing all of these associated files and settings. 
Many apps come with an uninstaller because they create system files and settings on your device when they are installed. These files and settings need to be removed when the app is uninstalled in order to completely remove the app from your device and restore your system to its original state. 
In short, deleting an app simply removes the app from your device, while uninstalling an app removes the app and any associated files and settings from your device.

input: A pencil eraser works by using a soft, spongy material that can rub against paper and remove the graphite marks that a pencil makes. The soft material is able to pick up the tiny particles of graphite and lift them off the page. Some pencil erasers work better than others because they are made of higher quality materials. For example, an eraser that is made of rubber will work better than one that is made of a cheaper, lower quality material. The better the material, the more effectively it will be able to pick up and remove the graphite particles. Additionally, some erasers are designed to be more gentle on paper, while others are more abrasive and may cause tearing or smudging. Some erasers may also leave a pink residue, which is caused by a dye that is added to the eraser to make it more visible. This dye can sometimes rub off onto the paper and leave a pink mark.
output: Professional software engineer here . The most common types of bugs are : 1 . A logic error . These are most often just mistakes made by the programmer . The mistake might be because the programmer had a momentary lapse of concentration or because they did n't quite understand something . For example , testing that two things are equal when the test should have been if they are * not * equal . 2 . Lack of or poor error handling . For almost everything that can be done in software , there are at least 3 other ways in which that might go wrong . Trying to write to a new file , for example , might fail if a file with that name already exists , if there are permission problems , if the disk is full or a quota is exceeded , if another program is simultaneously trying to write to the same file , if the file system has become corrupt , if the system is running out of memory and a whole host of other things . Handling these cases is an important part of writing a program , but because many of them rarely happen in practice and programmers can be lazy , they 're not handled correctly or at all . So most of the time the program works fine , but on the rare occasion when the disk is full , for example , the program does n't properly check for and handle that error and does something unexpected . 3 . Mismatching expectations from different programmers . Often programs contain code written by more than one programmer , and these different pieces of code must work together . Well engineered software is broken down into separate independent components that can be independently designed , implemented and tested . Sometimes when two components that appear to work fine independently are used together , something goes wrong . They might both be stepping on each other 's feet by fighting over the same resource or one might try to use the other , but expect it to work slightly differently from how it actually does . 4 . The programmer makes assumptions that are not necessarily true . They might be true on the programmer 's own system where the program works fine , but if the program is run on a different system , it might fail because the assumptions are no longer valid . For example , they might assume that another program is already installed , or that the OS is installed to the C:\ drive .

Holocaust denial is the act of denying the genocide of Jews during World War II, also known as the Holocaust. This belief is not based in fact, but rather is an attempt to downplay or completely ignore the overwhelming evidence that the Holocaust occurred. 
Holocaust deniers may use a variety of tactics to try to justify their beliefs. For example, they may claim that the Holocaust did not happen, or that it was not as widespread or as severe as it is generally believed to be. They may also claim that the victims of the Holocaust were not predominantly Jews, or that the deaths of the victims were not intentional. 
There is no legitimate evidence to support the arguments of Holocaust deniers. The vast majority of historians and scholars, as well as the survivors of the Holocaust, agree that the Holocaust was a horrific event in which millions of Jews and other minority groups were systematically murdered by the Nazi regime. 
It is important to remember that the Holocaust was a real and tragic event in history, and denying it only serves to diminish the suffering of the victims and their families.

input: When you pee, the water in the toilet bowl moves around and some of it goes down the drain, which is a hole at the bottom of the toilet. The water goes through a pipe and into the sewer, which is a big underground system of pipes that carries water and waste away from houses and buildings. The water in the sewer is cleaned and treated before it is released into rivers, lakes, or the ocean. So, even though you might be adding more water to the toilet bowl when you pee, some of it is also going down the drain and into the sewer. That's why the water level in the toilet bowl doesn't go up very much, or at all. It's also why the toilet doesn't overflow and make a big mess. The drain and the pipes help to keep everything moving and flowing properly.
output: Most of the photographs you see of nuclear mushroom clouds are of stereotypically " mushroomy " appearance , and taken within the first minute or so of the blast . This is when they look maximally like mushrooms , because the extremely hot nuclear fireball is rising up , encountering resistance from the dense air of the atmosphere , and flattening . If you look at pictures of late - stage mushroom clouds — e.g. 10 minutes later , when they are at maximum altitude — they often do n't look very mushroomy . So [ here is Hiroshima quite some time after detonation ] ( URL_0 ) — it has " anvilled . " [ Here is late - stage Nagasaki ] ( URL_6 ) — a lot less mushroomy than the classic early stage photos . [ Here is Castle Bravo at 60 seconds ] ( URL_7 ) — still mushroomy — and [ here it is at 16 minutes ] ( URL_1 ) — not very mushroomy , more of a column . [ Here 's a nuclear cloud that was set off too high to create a stem , so it is just the top ] ( URL_5 ) . And so on — there is a lot more variation in nuclear mushroom clouds than most people realize , because [ they often see just the same clouds repeated time after time ] ( URL_2 ) and do n't realize that these are usually very early - stage clouds . As an aside , the size of the " top " of the cloud is related to the size of the fireball in question . Nuclear detonations have very large fireballs most of the time . The heat of the fireball determines how high it rises ( and nuclear fireballs are _ much _ hotter than conventional explosions ) . As it rises , it encounters air resistance which sets up what is known as a [ Rayleight - Taylor instability ] ( URL_4 ) condition . This means the less dense part ( the fireball ) starts to bend back on itself as a torus . The higher it rises , the more it squishes out into an anvil or pancake shape . If it is large enough to hit the troposphere , that can squish it out even more ( [ and for very high yield weapons , a little " tendril " of cloud can squirt through the very top , which looks weird ] ( URL_3 ) ) . Whether there is a stem or not depends on how low the fireball was detonated and its width varies as a percentage of the total size of the fireball .

Ten years ago, processors were being made using transistors that were around 22 nanometers (nm) in size. Today, processors are being made using transistors that are only a few nanometers in size. This allows for more transistors to be packed into a smaller area, which means the processor can do more calculations in a given amount of time. It's not possible to make 20 nm chips that are as fast as they are now because technology has advanced significantly since 2003. Processor manufacturers have come up with new and improved ways to design and build processors, which allows them to make faster and more efficient chips. In addition, as transistors have gotten smaller, they have also become more efficient at performing calculations, which has also contributed to the increase in processor speed.

For example, if you're riding up a hill and it's feeling hard to pedal, you might want to shift to a lower gear. To do this, you would use the gear shifter on the right handlebar to move the chain to a smaller cog (a gear on the back wheel). At the same time, you would use the gear shifter on the left handlebar to move the chain to a larger chainring (a gear on the pedals). This will make it easier to pedal because the chain will be pulling on a larger cog, which will make the pedals turn more slowly. On the other hand, if you're riding on a flat surface and you want to go faster, you might want to shift to a higher gear. To do this, you would use the gear shifter on the right handlebar to move the chain to a larger cog, and use the gear shifter on the left handlebar to move the chain to a smaller chainring. This will make it harder to pedal, but the bike will go faster because the pedals will be turning more quickly. It's important to shift gears smoothly, without jerking the pedals or the handlebars. With practice, you'll get the hang of it!
