input: Sure! An IP address is a unique number that is assigned to every device that connects to the internet. It's a bit like a phone number for your device - it allows other devices on the internet to find and communicate with it. On a local network, like the one in your home, all of the devices (such as your computer, phone, and smart TV) are connected to a router. The router is like a traffic controller that sends information between your devices and the internet. Each device on the local network has its own unique IP address on that network. This is called a "private" IP address. The router also has a unique IP address on the local network, called the "gateway" address. The router also has a "public" IP address that is used when it connects to the internet. This is the address that other devices on the internet use to communicate with your local network. DNS, or Domain Name System, is a way of translating human-readable website names (like "google.com") into the IP addresses that computers use to communicate with each other. When you type a website name into your browser, your computer sends a request to a DNS server to look up the corresponding IP address. The DNS server then sends the IP address back to your computer, which uses it to connect to the website's server and download the webpage. I hope this helps! output: Graduate school is so diverse that it does n't really make any sense to lump it all into one category . As a general term , graduate school is some sort of education beyond the undergraduate . Generally , a specific field ( say , chemical engineering ) is chosen before beginning the program . The programs may last 1 - 6 years or more , depending on the degree , institution , and field . A Master 's degree is sometimes a terminal degree ( meaning that it is the highest degree in a given field ) . The Master of Business Administration ( MBA ) is a terminal degree . The Master in Social Work is also considered a terminal degree , even though there is a Doctorate of Social Work ( though it is uncommon and very specialized ) . Master 's degrees in the sciences are generally not considered terminal degrees , as they are often used as a stepping stone to get a doctorate ( Ph.D. ) . Master 's degrees may be coursework - only or may include a research / thesis / project aspect . Doctorates ( Ph . D.s ) generally require several years of research and a dissertation in addition to coursework . Depending on the field and institution , this may take anywhere between 3 and 8 years . There are other graduate degrees , for example in law ( JD ) , medicine ( MD ) , dentistry ( DDS ) , education ( EdD ) and others . Each program is specialized and includes the highest level coursework available in that field . The application process is very similar to undergrad . Main differences include : * taking the GRE ( and , if applicable , individual subject GREs ) , LSAT , MCAT , GMAT , or other instead of the SAT * essays topics are limited to professional / research experiences EDIT : added more graduate tests

input: Magnetic engines, also known as magnetic motors, are devices that use magnetism to generate rotational force or torque. While it is possible to build devices that use magnets to produce rotational force, it is not currently possible to build a perpetual motion machine using magnets alone. Perpetual motion machines are devices that can operate indefinitely without any external energy input. Such machines violate the laws of thermodynamics, which state that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted from one form to another. Therefore, it is impossible to build a machine that can operate indefinitely without any energy input. In order for a magnetic motor to work, it would need some external energy source to power it. For example, a magnet could be used to generate rotational force by using electricity to create a magnetic field. However, the electricity used to power the magnet would eventually run out, and the magnet would stop working. In summary, while it is possible to use magnets to generate rotational force, it is not currently possible to build a perpetual motion machine using magnets alone.
output: Suppose you wanted to draw a solid square on paper but you only have rubber stamps of circles . How do you do draw a square ? You can use the biggest circle stamp , call it " size 10 " , to make one big circle , then stamp a smaller " size 5 " circle four times around the big circle , then use progressively smaller circle stamps to fill in the gaps until you have a solid square . As you can imagine you 'd be using the smallest circle stamp ( " size 1 " ) a lot to fill in the small white spaces at the sharp corners of the square . Now suppose you needed a shorthand notation to describe this to another stamp artist how you drew a square using only circle stamps : 1 square = 1 size10 + 4 size5 + 16 size3 + 86 size2 + 158 size1 Why is this useful ? In the world of electronics and signals it 's almost as if you 're trying to communicate information using only one type of rubber stamp : the sine wave . The Fourier Series allows you to express and communicate arbitrary information using the the rubber circle stamp of the electronics world : the sine wave . Added bonus , once information is translated into a sum of sine waves it can be transmitted , stored , and processed very easily . In fact you can use this for compression . Suppose you do n't need to draw a perfect square , an approximate square would be good enough . Then you can transmit less information : 1 square = 1 size10 + 4 size5 + 16 size3 It 's a lumpy square but good enough . This is basis for JPEG and MPG signal compression : it translates the data into a wave , expresses that wave an approximate sum of sine waves , some of the fine detail information is lost , the information about how to reconstruct the signal is what is stored and transmitted , but overall our eyes and ears do n't notice that fine detail that was lost .

When we see or do something startling or unexpected, our bodies often respond with a physical reaction called the "fight or flight" response. This is a natural instinct that helps us prepare to either fight or run away from a perceived threat. 
During the fight or flight response, our bodies release a hormone called adrenaline, which increases our heart rate and blood pressure. This can make our heart feel like it is racing or "dropping," and can also cause us to feel scared or anxious. 
Sometimes, when a teacher calls us over to their desk, we may not know what to expect and our brains may perceive this as a potential threat. This can trigger the fight or flight response and cause us to feel scared or anxious. 
It's important to remember that these physical reactions are normal and can help us prepare to handle unexpected situations. However, if we feel scared or anxious a lot of the time, it might be helpful to talk to a trusted adult or a mental health professional for support.

output: Caliber is the width of the barrel of a firearm , which should also essentially be the diameter of the bullet . You 'll see two different naming conventions , depending on where / when a round was invented . Rounds invented under the metric system will have the diameter in millimeters , followed by the length of the cartridge case . For example , 5.56x45 has a 5.56 mm wide diameter and a 45 mm long case . The length of the cartridge matters because a longer case holds more powder , which means the round will travel faster . Rounds invented under imperial ( standard ) units will name the diameter in hundreths of an inch . For example , .50 BMG is half an inch wide . Standard rounds do not list the length of the cartridge . However , most standard - unit rounds have some sort of extra name attached to them to help identify the specific round ( .22 " long rifle , " .38 " special , " .223 " Remington , " etc . )

Standoffs are used to physically separate the motherboard from the case of a computer. This separation is important for a number of reasons. 
First, the motherboard and the case are made of different materials, and they can have different electrical charges. If the motherboard is in contact with the case, these charges can flow between the two, causing a short circuit. Standoffs help to prevent this by providing a physical barrier between the motherboard and the case. 
Second, standoffs help to support the motherboard and keep it level within the case. This can help to prevent the motherboard from bending or warping due to the weight of the components that are attached to it. 
Finally, standoffs also help to provide airflow within the case by creating space between the motherboard and the case. This can help to keep the components on the motherboard cooler, which can help to extend their lifespan. 
In short, standoffs are an important component of a computer case because they help to protect the motherboard from damage, support it, and improve airflow.

output: The theory is more to do with the * unpredictability * of a system , and the fact that even though we may know the exact state of a system at the beginning , we can not predict what it will be like later on . For example , think of a weather system ; from the way the different variables are behaving at this instant in time , we can probably predict what the state will be like tomorrow , or even perhaps a week from now . However , due to the number of variables that need to be considered , and the fact that they all influence one another , we could n't say with any certainty what the weather will be like in , say , a year from now . It is therefore a * nonlinear * system , and tiny changes in these sorts of systems can drastically alter the way the system is evolving . The term ' butterfly effect ' is often used because a change as tiny as a butterfly flapping its wings on the other side of the world could be just enough to ' push over the edge ' one of these many variables in a weather system , causing a hurricane to begin when there may not have been the conditions in place otherwise . In other words , a minute change in initial conditions can have huge effects on the final state of a system .

It's true that no two snowflakes are exactly alike, but it's not because some snowflakes are hard to find or observe. Instead, it's because of the way that snowflakes are formed. 
Snowflakes are made of ice crystals, which form in the sky when water vapor turns into a solid. The exact shape and size of a snowflake depends on the temperature and humidity in the air as it falls to the ground. Because these conditions are always changing, it's unlikely that two snowflakes will form under exactly the same conditions and therefore be exactly the same. 
Scientists have studied snowflakes for many years, and they have never found two that are exactly the same. While it's possible that two snowflakes could be very similar, it's very unlikely that they would be exactly the same. So while it's not impossible that two snowflakes could be the same, it's very unlikely, and we can say with confidence that no two snowflakes are exactly alike.

Abortion is a medical procedure that ends a pregnancy. There are different ways to have an abortion, and the method used can depend on how far along the pregnancy is and other factors. 
One way to have an abortion is with medication. This can be done during the first 10 weeks of pregnancy. The person seeking the abortion takes two different medications. The first one, called mifepristone, is taken at a doctor's office. This medication makes it so the pregnancy cannot continue. A few days later, the person takes a second medication called misoprostol at home. This medication causes the uterus to contract and empty, which ends the pregnancy. 
Another way to have an abortion is with a procedure called aspiration or suction abortion. This can be done during the first 16 to 20 weeks of pregnancy. During the procedure, a healthcare provider uses special instruments to remove the pregnancy from the uterus. The provider may use local anesthesia to numb the area and prevent pain. 
There are other types of procedures that can be used to end a pregnancy, but these are the most common. 
After an abortion, the pregnancy tissue (including the fetus) is usually removed from the body. In some cases, the tissue may be examined to make sure the abortion was successful and to check for any complications. 
It's important to note that abortion is a safe and legal medical procedure. However, it's also a personal and often difficult decision that can involve a range of emotions. If you're considering an abortion, it's important to talk to a healthcare provider or a trusted adult about your options and how to get support.

output: As per color : It 's because black hair is the default color for hair and Africans never had environmental pressures to lose the pigment like Caucasians did . To survive in dark , cloudy Europe , Caucasians needed to lose pigment so that they could absorb more of the sun 's loving rays -- presumably this occurred over thousands of years of mutations , survival of those with the trait , natural selection , and sexual selection . This loss of pigment occurred in both the hair and the skin because the amount of pigment in both is somewhat related . Additionally , different types of pigment changes occurred in hair , but it did not particularly matter what color the hair turned to . As long as the skin lightened , the color of the hair did not matter . Thus , you could have had a genetic line with pale skin and black hair ( with the mutation only being on the skin pigmentation gene , but not the hair pigmentation gene . But , this line would still survive thanks to the light skin ) . Likewise , you could have had a genetic line with pale skin and brown or red hair ( with a mutation on the skin pigmentation genes AND on the hair pigmentation genes . This line would also survive because it had light skin . The mutation providing for red or brown hair was not necessary but not harmful , either ) . Now , you are probably saying--"Well Asians lived at high latitudes , too ! " True , but the two groups ate different types of food . The food being consumed by persons in northerly Asia eliminated their need for as much sun exposure . So , the basic answers are ( 1 ) climate and ( 2 ) diet . As to hair texture , I have no idea . I could explain some theories as to west African hair , but I do n't know much about the genetics of white hair .

input: The Pope, bishops, and the Vatican are all part of the Catholic Church, which is a Christian denomination that traces its roots back to Jesus and his apostles. According to the Catholic Church, Jesus founded the Church and chose Peter, one of his apostles, to be the leader of the Church. Peter is considered the first Pope, or the Bishop of Rome, and the Catholic Church believes that the Pope has a special role as the successor of Peter and the leader of the Church. Over time, the Church grew and spread to different parts of the world. Bishops were appointed to lead local communities of believers, and they answered to the Pope as the head of the Church. The Vatican is the headquarters of the Catholic Church and is located in Rome, Italy. It is the home of the Pope and the central governing body of the Church. So, in short, the Pope, bishops, and the Vatican are all part of the Catholic Church, which was founded by Jesus and has a long history dating back almost 2,000 years.
output: Anarchism does n't mean no rules . It means no hierarchy . I do n't know of a serious political philosophy that advocates no rules . At the moment , our rules come from our leaders . In a totalitarian society , it 's as simple as that . In a democratic one like America , the leaders need to pay some heed to the citizens and motivated citizens are able to influence those rules to an extent but the extent is very limited . Be wary of anyone who maintains America is a police state or that it is a democratic republic rather than a democracy . The former has no clue about what they are talking about and the latter is resting on a semantic quibble . Anarchists ( and Libertarians in the American sense ) see the state as a centre of power where it is concentrated in the hands of a few . Sure , you could go to college , join some young Labor / Republican / Democrat / Liberal group , become a lawyer and get on track to join this elite from the beginning but much of your ability to do this is determined by luck . More so than most people recognise . And even if you succeed , it still does n't solve the problem of power being concentrated in the hands of people at the centre of the state 's structure . Until Rothbard and some other American thinkers became prominent last century , the term Libertarian was more akin to what we think of as anarchist today . That is why I make the distinction of American - style libertarians . Those libertarians and the anarchists agree that the state is mostly malevolent but after that , they part ways . Libertarians feel the single best improvement is the minimisation of the state and things will all be great after that . Anarchists are just as derisive of the state but feel there are other hierarchies that are just as , if not more , detrimental than the state . Religious authorities and corporations for example . This is not to say anarchists are * necessarily * atheist or anti - business . Tolstoy was a devout Christian and espoused a style of anarchism that thrived while he was alive . The point is that the religious authority should n't be vested in a pope or caliph and should look more like the Quakers for example . Also , the rewards of business should n't accrue to a shareholder / boss class . Profits should be shared by the producers of goods and services . Another difference is with the view of land ownership . Libertarians feel land is just another kind of property and almost all of it should be held in private hands . Most anarchists feel land is the main type of property that must not be held privately . All land is stolen from someone , or bought from someone who stole it or bought from someone who may not have stolen it but acquired it without work via inheritance . Anarchists feel all property should have rents paid back to the community . Georgists for example believe these rents should constitute the only type of tax allowed in a Georgist community . A general principle is that power should devolve to the lowest possible organisational level . This is one of the stated objectives of the EU but they do n't prioritise it as highly as anarchists . If I want to smoke weed , it should be for me to decide whether I will or not . On the other hand , the lights in my street are what economists call a public good and so it would n't be in my self - interest to set them all up and maintain them myself . In that case , that task would fall to the neighbourhood council . There might only be a few hundred people covered by that council 's borders so I could have real influence in its decisions without quitting my day job and becoming part of a dedicated political class . That council might also coordinate the neighbourhood 's creche , meals for the housebound and so on . The next neighbourhood over might be overwhelmingly from a particular immigrant community and might come to a consensus to conduct its business in that group ' language . However , they might be interested in a football stadium , high school or some other infrastructure that would be too big for a neighbourhood collective to handle on its own . So a group of neighbourhoods who wish to remain distinct but feel they can work together would build confederations to handle that sort of thing . Rinse and repeat and you could have Universities or Large Hadron Colliders .

Dinosaurs didn't evolve to be as smart as humans because evolution doesn't work that way. Evolution is a process that takes place over many, many generations and it is driven by natural selection. Natural selection is the process by which certain traits that help an organism survive and reproduce are passed down to its offspring. 
For example, let's say there is a group of dinosaurs that live in a forest. Some of these dinosaurs have long necks, which helps them reach high branches and find food. These dinosaurs are more likely to survive and reproduce, so over time, more and more dinosaurs in the population will have long necks. This is an example of how natural selection can lead to the evolution of a particular trait. 
However, natural selection doesn't always lead to the evolution of bigger brains or greater intelligence. In fact, many times, smaller brains are more advantageous for survival. For example, small brains require less energy to maintain, which means that the animal can use its energy for other things, like finding food or avoiding predators. 
So, to answer your question, dinosaurs didn't evolve to be as smart as humans because natural selection didn't favor the evolution of larger brains or greater intelligence in dinosaurs.

input: Sure, I can help clarify the differences between satire, sarcasm, and parody. Satire is a form of humor that uses wit to make fun of or criticize something, usually a person, institution, or idea. Satire is often used to expose flaws or shortcomings in a humorous way, in the hopes of inspiring change. Sarcasm is a type of irony that involves saying or writing something that is the opposite of what you really mean, usually in a mocking or sarcastic tone. Sarcasm is often used to make fun of something or someone, or to convey a sense of irony or absurdity. Parody is a type of humor that involves imitating or exaggerating the characteristics of something, often for the purpose of making fun of it. A parody can be of a person, an institution, an idea, or even a whole genre of art or literature. To summarize, satire and sarcasm are both forms of humor that involve making fun of something or someone, but satire often has a more serious or critical purpose, while sarcasm is more often used just to be amusing or sarcastic. Parody, on the other hand, involves imitating something in order to make fun of it.

Shale is a type of sedimentary rock that contains a high amount of oil and natural gas. In the United States, there are large deposits of shale in places like Texas and North Dakota. In the past decade, new technologies have been developed that make it possible to extract oil and natural gas from shale deposits in a process called hydraulic fracturing, or "fracking." 
When the demand for oil and natural gas is high, companies will extract and sell as much as they can, which can drive up the price of these fuels. On the other hand, when there is a lot of shale oil and natural gas being produced, it can increase the overall supply of these fuels and lead to lower prices. This is because there is more of the fuel available, so there is less competition among buyers, which can drive down the price. 
So, in short, the production of shale oil and natural gas can affect the price of these fuels by increasing the overall supply and decreasing the demand for them. When there is a lot of shale being produced, it can lead to lower gas prices.

Teeth grinding, also known as bruxism, is a common condition that can occur during sleep or while awake. It is when a person clinches, grinds, or gnashes their teeth together. Some people grind their teeth because of stress or anxiety, while others do it because of an abnormal bite or misaligned teeth. It can also be caused by sleep disorders, such as sleep apnea. 
Bruxism can cause tooth damage, such as chips or cracks, and can also cause jaw pain and headaches. If your roommate is grinding their teeth, they may want to see a dentist or doctor to help identify the cause and find a solution. In the meantime, they can try wearing a mouthguard at night to protect their teeth and reduce the grinding noise.

Inside your phone, there is a component called a battery that stores electrical energy and provides power to the phone when it is turned on. The battery is made up of a group of small cells that are connected together and contain chemical substances that can produce electricity. 
Inside the battery, there are positive and negative electrodes, and a chemical called an electrolyte. When the phone is turned on, the battery produces an electrical current by moving electrons from the negative electrode to the positive electrode through the electrolyte. 
As the battery is used, the chemical reactions inside the battery start to run down and produce less and less electrical current. When the battery is almost empty, it will produce very little electrical current, and your phone will eventually turn off. 
To measure how much charge is left in the battery, your phone has a small chip called a "battery management system" (BMS) that keeps track of the amount of electrical current flowing in and out of the battery. The BMS sends this information to the phone's operating system, which then displays the remaining battery charge as a percentage on the screen. 
So, in short, your phone knows how much charge is left in the battery by measuring the electrical current flowing in and out of the battery and using a special chip to keep track of this information.

input: Trickle-down economics is a theory that suggests that if you give tax breaks and other economic benefits to wealthy people and businesses, they will use that extra money to invest in the economy, creating jobs and wealth that will eventually trickle down to the middle class and poor. The idea is that by helping the "top" of the economic ladder, everyone else will eventually benefit. Some people find this theory funny because they don't believe it works in practice. They argue that giving tax breaks and other benefits to the wealthy and businesses does not necessarily lead to increased investment and job creation, and that the benefits of these policies often do not "trickle down" to the middle class and poor as intended. Instead, they argue that these policies often result in the concentration of wealth and income among the wealthy, rather than benefiting the entire economy. It's important to note that trickle-down economics is just one theory about how to stimulate economic growth and improve the lives of people across all income levels. There are other theories and approaches that focus on different ways to support and invest in the middle class and poor, such as through progressive taxation, government spending on education and infrastructure, and other policies.

input:
Seasonal lag is a phenomenon that occurs because the Earth's atmosphere and oceans take time to warm up or cool down. This means that the temperatures we experience on the Earth's surface do not change as quickly as the amount of sunlight we receive. 
For example, after the Winter Solstice, the amount of sunlight we receive starts to increase. However, it takes time for the Earth's atmosphere and oceans to warm up and for this warmth to be reflected in the temperatures we experience on the surface. This is why temperatures may continue to decrease for a while after the Winter Solstice, even though the amount of sunlight is increasing. 
The same thing happens in the summer. After the Summer Solstice, the amount of sunlight we receive starts to decrease, but it takes time for the Earth's atmosphere and oceans to cool down and for this cooling to be reflected in the temperatures we experience on the surface. This is why temperatures may continue to increase for a while after the Summer Solstice, even though the amount of sunlight is decreasing. 
In short, seasonal lag is the result of the Earth's atmosphere and oceans taking time to warm up or cool down in response to changes in the amount of sunlight we receive. This is why we may experience temperatures that are different from what we might expect based on the amount of sunlight we are receiving.

As for why someone's mouth doesn't have to smell bad up close, it's because the mouth and saliva have natural bacteria and enzymes that help to break down food and keep the mouth clean. These bacteria and enzymes also help to prevent bad breath. However, if a person's mouth is not properly cared for, such as by brushing and flossing regularly and having a healthy diet, it can lead to a buildup of bacteria and cause bad breath or a bad smell in the mouth.

input: Hydrochloride is a common name for a compound that contains hydrogen and chlorine atoms bonded together. Many medications include a hydrochloride group as part of their chemical structure. One reason that hydrochloride is used in some medications is because it can help to make the medication more stable and easier to store. It can also help the medication to dissolve more easily in water, which can make it easier for the body to absorb the medication. In some cases, the hydrochloride group can also help to make the medication more effective by changing its chemical properties in a way that allows it to work better in the body. Overall, the use of hydrochloride in medications is mostly driven by the specific chemical and physical properties of the compound, and how those properties can be used to improve the effectiveness and stability of the medication.

input: Our eyes move in rapid jolts, or saccades, because it helps us see things more clearly and efficiently. When we look at something, our eyes don't stay completely still. Instead, they make tiny movements all the time to help us see things more clearly. These movements are called saccades. Saccades help us to quickly move our gaze from one place to another and to focus on different objects. They also help us to scan a scene and gather information about our surroundings. When our eyes make saccades, we don't see the rapid eye movement because our brains process the information from our eyes in a way that makes it seem like the image is smooth and continuous. This is called saccadic masking. So, even though our eyes are making rapid jolts, our brains make it seem like we are seeing a smooth, seamless image. This helps us to see things more clearly and efficiently.
output: See , you do n't actually fall asleep all at once . It 's more of a well - defined process that everyone makes their way through in their own time . I 'll attempt to explain it . ----------------------------------------------------------------------- You start off moving into what 's called Stage 1 , from being completely awake . When you first lay down to go to sleep , you pass from your awake state to what 's called Alpha state . You 've daydreamed before , right ? That 's basically Alpha state . You 're still mostly conscious , but you start to see some little bouts of color behind your eyes ( hypnogogic hallucinations ) and you start to feel more relaxed . After Alpha , you enter Theta state . Theta state is when you could technically be considered asleep . This is when you move completely into sleep paralysis ( have you felt like you were falling then woken up with a start ? that 's sleep paralysis setting in and you not being completely unaware when it happened ) . You 're still sleeping relatively lightly , but if you can get through this stage you move into deeper sleep . ----------------------------------------------------------------------- The next major stage of sleep is called Stage 2 . Your brain starts to produce short periods of rapid brain waves that are called Sleep Spindles . This is the precursor to what comes next in sleep , deep sleep . Your body temperature begins to drop and your heart rate slows down , settling you in for the night . ------------------------------------------------------------------------ You then enter Stage 3 . When you enter stage 3 , really slow brain waves called Delta waves start happening . This is the true transition between light and deep sleep . ------------------------------------------------------------------------ You then enter Stage 4 . Stage four is commonly referred to as Delta Sleep because of the brain waves associated with it . It 's a deep sleep that lasts for about thirty minutes . You are the technically the most asleep in this stage . ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Here 's where it gets interesting . There is another stage of sleep unlike all the others . It 's called Stage 5 , or REM sleep . REM stands for Rapid Eye Movement , your eyes literally start moving rapidly behind your eyelids . Your breathing rate increases , and your brain waves shoot up to almost as active as if you were awake . Your voluntary muscles become completely paralyzed , and you start dreaming . REM is also where most of the body 's repairs happen . REM sleep is what makes you feel rested when you wake up , and where the body actually rests and restores itself ( which is why you usually do n't feel rested after a night of drinking or smoking weed , they both inhibit REM sleep ) . ----------------------------------------------------------------------- These stages come in cycles . It takes roughly 90 minutes for you to go from awake into REM . The first cycle typically has a short REM period , but subsequent cycles increase the duration of REM . When we actually fall asleep , we go from stage 1 into 2 , then 3 , then 4 , but here 's where something curious happens . We then go from 4 to 3 , then 3 to 2 , then into REM . After REM , we usually return to stage 2 , then go back to 3 , 4 , then 3 , 2 , REM . We have as many cycles as we stay asleep for , when we wake up , feeling refreshed and ready or the day . ------------------------------------------------------------------------ I do n't know if that answered your question , but I hope it shed some light on the subject . Edit : formatting . Edit 2 : I 'll be here if anyone has any other questions they 'd like answered about this topic . Edit 3 : Holy crap ! Thanks for popping my gold cherry anon ! Much love ! < 3 Edit 4 : Wow this blew up ... Thanks for all the questions ! I 'm getting to them all , do n't worry !

This made it very difficult for the bank to keep operating and it had to ask for help from the government. So, to sum it up, the subprime crisis happened because a lot of people took out loans to buy houses, but they couldn't afford to pay them back. input:
When you have a cold, your throat may feel itchy because your body is trying to fight off the infection. When you're sick, your immune system kicks into gear and works to rid your body of the virus or bacteria that is causing the illness. One way that your body does this is by producing more mucus. Mucus is a thick, sticky substance that helps to trap and remove germs from your body. 
When you have a cold, your body may produce more mucus than usual, and this can make your throat feel itchy. The extra mucus can also cause your throat to feel sore or scratchy. 
In addition to mucus, your body may also produce more saliva when you're sick. Saliva is a watery substance that helps to keep your mouth and throat moist. When you have a cold, you may produce more saliva than usual, which can also make your throat feel itchy. 
It's also possible that the itchy feeling in your throat is caused by irritation from coughing or sneezing. When you cough or sneeze, the muscles in your throat and chest contract, which can cause your throat to feel itchy. 
Overall, the itchy feeling in your throat when you have a cold is caused by a combination of factors, including an increase in mucus and saliva production, as well as irritation from coughing or sneezing.

This has led to a number of conspiracy theories about the project and its true purpose.

input: Computers use a system called "binary" to store and process information. In binary, there are only two numbers: 0 and 1. These numbers are called "bits." To turn 0s and 1s into text, video, and other programs, computers use a special set of instructions called a "program." These instructions tell the computer what to do with the 0s and 1s it has stored. For example, if you want to type a letter on your computer, the program that runs your keyboard will take the keys you press and turn them into a series of 0s and 1s. It will then send these 0s and 1s to another program, called the "operating system," which knows how to turn them back into the letters and symbols you typed. Similarly, if you want to watch a video on your computer, the program that runs the video player will take the 0s and 1s that make up the video and turn them into images and sounds that you can see and hear. I hope this helps to explain how computers use 0s and 1s to do all kinds of useful and interesting things!

input:
When a candle burns, it produces a small amount of smoke. This smoke is made up of tiny particles of unburned wax and other substances that are released into the air as the candle burns. 
However, when you blow out a candle, you are extinguishing the flame by cutting off the oxygen supply. This can cause the candle to smoke more heavily because the wax is still hot and the flame is still burning, but it is not getting enough oxygen to continue burning cleanly. The smoke you see when you blow out a candle is made up of these unburned particles of wax and other substances that are released into the air. 
Overall, the amount of smoke that a candle produces depends on how efficiently the candle is burning. A candle that is burning cleanly and efficiently will produce less smoke than a candle that is not burning as well.

input: Cancer can kill people in several ways. Here are a few examples: A cancerous tumor can grow and press against other organs or tissues in the body, which can cause them to stop working properly. For example, if a cancerous tumor grows in the lungs, it can make it difficult for a person to breathe. Cancer cells can spread (metastasize) to other parts of the body, which can cause problems in those areas. For example, if cancer cells spread to the bones, they can weaken the bones and cause them to break more easily. Cancer can affect the body's immune system, which is the part of the body that helps fight off infections and diseases. If a person's immune system is weakened by cancer, they may be more likely to get sick or have infections that are more difficult to treat. Some cancer treatments, such as chemotherapy and radiation, can be very hard on the body and can cause side effects that make a person feel very sick. Overall, cancer can kill people by causing problems with the way the body functions and by making it harder for the body to fight off other health problems.

input: The price of a flash drive, or any other product, is determined by a variety of factors, including the cost of materials, labor, and manufacturing, as well as the demand for the product and the price that the market will bear. In the case of flash drives, the cost of materials and labor is likely to be similar for a 2 GB drive and an 8 GB drive, since both will require a similar amount of plastic, electronics, and labor to manufacture. However, the 8 GB drive will likely have a higher price because it can store more data and may be more useful to some customers, so it may be in higher demand and therefore command a higher price. In addition, the cost of manufacturing may be higher for the 8 GB drive due to the additional materials and labor required to produce it. This may also contribute to the higher price of the 8 GB drive. Overall, the price of a flash drive is determined by a combination of factors, including the cost of materials and manufacturing, as well as the demand for the product and the price that the market will bear.
output: So you start the game with a deck . A deck can be made up of five different colors or as few as one . There are also colorless cards . Each color has it 's own flavor and if you get cards with more than one card you combine the flavor . White is usually geared towards protecting , red towards attacking fast , green towards attacking with big things , blue with making your opponents life hard and black towards being straight out mean . Each color has a few distinctions , that can but do n't always overlap . Now let 's get to playing . You have a few kinds of cards . These can change by set . However the baseline are , lands , enchantments , creatures , sorceries , artifacts and instants . Lands are your basic source of mana . Mana allows you to pay for the spells you want to caste . Some spells have a few green symbols , or red or etc symbols on the top of the card along with a grey symbol with a number in it . This means that you have to use at least two green and any assorted mana to pay for that spell . So if the spell is 4 and a green , you have to pay 4 and at least one green mana to play it . Now let 's get to the other card types . Sorceries are spells that help you , but do n't necessarily summon a creature onto the field . Depending on the flavor of the color the spell is from it can do any number of cool things . These effects of course are specific to the card in question . Instants are like sorceries , but they can be played at almost any time . Instants are usually used in reaction to something else . Creatures are what goes onto the field and usually what damages the enemy creatures and the person themselves . To make a creature you have to tap it , meaning turn it sideways . Creatures that are tapped can not be used to block though . You can also tap creatures to use their abilities . The other option is to pay mana to use the creatures ability . Much like cards most of these abilities require a specific kind of mana to use . Creatures also have attack and defence , a pair of numbers in the button corner of the card . If a creature attacks and it 's a 5/5 then it will deal five damage and take five damage to destroy . Extra damage from say if a 1/1 blocks a 5/5 does not roll over to hit the summoner . There ate of course several mechanics that can change the outcomes of matches like this . Next are artifacts . Artifacts are some of the most varied cards in the game . Most of the time they 're summoned into play and do n't do anything immediately . They 're a static on the field that can be used later if a certain condition is met . They can be equiped to buff a creature , tapped to do damage or get mana , and so on and so on . For the most part they 're also colorless . Then get to enchantments . Enchantments effect the field as a whole . They can make things harder or easier pending what they do . Enchant auras effect a specific thing that it 's attached to . Now we go to the phases . They are , to my knowledge , untap , upkeep , draw , first main phase , attack phase , second main phase , end phase . During untap upkeep and draw sorceries and creatures ca n't normally be summoned or played . Instants and effects can be used . During untap , all cards that are tapped on your side are untapped making then useable again . During upkeep , any card that requires mana or some other effect to stay on the field is paid . If you choose to not pay then the card is destroyed and goes to your graveyard . During your draw phase you can draw a card and play instants . Now we 're in the main phase . That means you may play sorceries and your opponent can play instants . You may also summon artifacts , creatures and enchants to the field . Creatures summoned must wait one turn before they can attack . Next is the combat phase . This is broken down into parts . Declare attacker phase . Tapping creatures to determine which will attack . Declare defenders phase follows . Your opponent chooses which creatures block your attack and which get through . Then damage is dealt . Multiple creatures can block one creature . Accordingly , a group block on a strong creature can kill it . Next is the second main phase , the same as the first , and then the end phase . The end goal is to lower the opponents life to zero through any means available . Now keep in mind , this is a simplified explanation from a player that 's been out of the loop for the last few sets . Each set adds new mechanics and cycles old ones out of play . It would be best to find a local judge and talk to them to keep updated on rules . Not bad for writing on my phone .

input:
Airlines typically don't lower the price of flights close to the departure time because they want to maximize their profits. If an airline has a flight that isn't fully booked, they may try to sell the remaining seats for a lower price to try to fill the plane, but they usually don't want to lower the price too much because they want to make as much money as possible from the flights they operate. 
Think of it this way: if an airline has a flight that costs $1000 per seat and they sell all the seats, they will make a profit of $1000 per seat x the number of seats on the plane. However, if they only sell half the seats and then lower the price to try to fill the remaining seats, they will make less money overall, even if they are able to sell the remaining seats for a lower price. 
In some cases, airlines may offer last-minute discounts or promotions to try to fill flights that aren't fully booked, but they generally don't want to do this too often because it can devalue the overall price of their flights and make it more difficult for them to sell tickets at full price in the future.

There are a few reasons why it might take companies until the deadline to get W-2 forms to their employees. 
First, companies have to gather all the information they need to complete the forms. This includes things like how much money you earned, how much tax was withheld from your pay, and any other deductions that were made from your pay. This can take some time, especially if the company is large and has many employees. 
Second, the company has to make sure that the forms are accurate. This means double-checking all the information to make sure it is correct. This can also take some time, especially if there are errors that need to be corrected. 
Finally, the company may need to print and mail the forms to their employees. This can take a few days or even a week, depending on how far the forms need to be sent and how many forms need to be mailed. 
So, even though most year-end tax forms are now automated, it can still take some time for companies to get them to their employees. But it's important for companies to take the time to make sure the forms are accurate and complete, so that employees can file their taxes correctly.

input: Keyboard and mouse controls are generally considered to be superior to dual-joystick controllers for first-person shooters because they offer more precision and faster movement. With a keyboard and mouse, you can move your character around more smoothly and make quicker, more precise shots. Imagine you're playing a game where you have to aim and shoot at targets. If you're using a dual-joystick controller, you might have to move one joystick to aim and another joystick to move. This can be a little bit slow and clunky, especially if you're trying to aim quickly. On the other hand, if you're using a keyboard and mouse, you can use the mouse to aim and the keyboard to move. The mouse is much more precise than a joystick, so you can aim more accurately. And because your fingers are already positioned on the keyboard, it's faster to move around with the keyboard than it is to use a joystick. Overall, keyboard and mouse controls offer a more intuitive and precise way to play first-person shooter games, which is why they are often the preferred choice among serious gamers.

input:
Marijuana is still illegal at the federal level in the United States, despite the fact that many states have legalized it for medical or recreational purposes. In order for marijuana to be federally legalized, it would require a change in the law. 
The process for changing a federal law in the United States involves several steps. First, a bill (a proposed law) must be introduced in Congress. This bill must then be debated and voted on by members of the House of Representatives and the Senate. If the bill passes in both chambers, it goes to the President to be signed into law. 
There are several ways that a bill can become a law, but this is the most common process. It can be a long and difficult process, and it requires the support of a majority of lawmakers and the President. 
So, in order for marijuana to be federally legalized, a bill would need to be introduced in Congress, debated and voted on, and then signed into law by the President. This would require a lot of support from lawmakers and the general public, as well as some negotiations and compromise.

input:
During the Cold War, the city of Berlin was located deep within the territory of the German Democratic Republic (GDR), also known as East Germany. The city was divided into two parts: West Berlin, which was controlled by the Western powers (the United States, the United Kingdom, and France), and East Berlin, which was controlled by the Soviet Union. 
West Berlin was located on the western side of the Berlin Wall, which was a heavily fortified barrier that separated the two parts of the city. The wall was built in 1961 to prevent people from East Germany from fleeing to the West. It was guarded by East German soldiers, and anyone who tried to cross the wall illegally could be shot. 
Despite being surrounded by East Germany, West Berlin was not controlled by the GDR or the Soviet Union. Instead, it was a free and democratic city, similar to other cities in Western Europe. The Western powers provided the city with food, fuel, and other supplies to help it survive. They also allowed people from West Berlin to travel freely to other parts of Western Europe. 
East Berlin, on the other hand, was a communist city and was controlled by the GDR and the Soviet Union. It was not as open or free as West Berlin, and people who lived there had fewer freedoms and rights. 
The situation in Berlin remained like this until the end of the Cold War in the 1990s, when the Berlin Wall was finally taken down and the city was reunited.
output: This has been touched upon in other topics about assembly and machine code , but in the best example I can think of is a series of translators . Let 's say you have someone who 's a really smart professor ( the processor ) and you want him to help you with a problem . The only thing is that this professor only speaks Russian ( binary or machine code ) . So you get someone who knows Russian but he can only translate that into Spanish ( assembly ) . Now let 's say you know some Spanish , but communicating with the translator that way is just annoying . So you finally get a friend to translate from Spanish to English ( programming languages like C or C++ ) . Basically when programming in a programming language they get translated to harder and harder to understand languages until it gets to just 1 's and 0 's . From there the CPU can then send 1 's or 0 's to other physical parts of the computer ( i.e. the professor can talk to his Russian speaking colleagues who know more about other subjects ) . Technically you could use a English to Russian dictionary to talk to the professor or even learn Russian , but it 's hard to do that and takes time . As a side note though , some people prefer going directly from English to Russian because using a string of translators blurs the lines , and things get mixed up ( i.e. if you code in assembly language , the first step above 1 's and 0 's , you have more direct control over what the computer does compared to C ) . As for data to physical action , a hard drive just might be programmed to see " 001001010 " to " write this here " . I 'm not too well versed on the exact specifics of how it works .

Energy drink companies use caffeine because it is a legal and safe stimulant that is widely available and has been extensively studied. Caffeine is a stimulant that can help to increase alertness and improve mental and physical performance. It is also relatively inexpensive to obtain and use in energy drinks. 
Ephedrine is a stimulant that is similar to caffeine, but it is not as widely available and is not as well-studied. It has also been linked to some potential negative side effects, such as increased heart rate and blood pressure, and it is not legal for use in dietary supplements in many countries. 
Modafinil is a prescription medication that is used to treat narcolepsy, a condition that causes excessive daytime sleepiness. It is not typically used in energy drinks because it is a prescription medication and is not legal to sell or use without a prescription. 
In summary, energy drink companies use caffeine because it is a legal and safe stimulant that is widely available and has been extensively studied, while other stimulants like ephedrine and modafinil may not be as widely available or have as much research to support their use, and may also have potential negative side effects or be subject to legal restrictions.
output: How do you think most of the developing countries came about in the first place ? The majority are former colonies of one European power or another , set up as nation - states on the European model during the 20th century independence movements . Most of the British Empire , in particular , was acquired not by an official government policy of expansion but by wealthy individuals going off to seek their fortune . Men like Cecil Rhodes or [ James Brooke ] ( URL_1 ) . The British government often only took over later when [ some disaster or other ] ( URL_2 ) reminded these people that running your own country is not all that easy . Imperialism is usually associated these days with rapacious exploitation of native people and their resources , and with irresponsible meddling with half - understood tribal rivalries and customs and other complicated local issues that has set up many of the problems of the world today . This is all partially true , although it should be remembered that , like the Billionaire in your question , many of these people had good intentions . They really thought they were helping the natives out of poverty , savagery and paganism , and that it was not only the right but the duty of civilized white men to rule other races that could n't rule themselves . And if they made a fortune out of resources that the natives were hardly using , why not ? After all if the British did n't occupy these places [ someone else who was really nasty would instead . ] ( URL_0 ) This might all seem horribly racist but you are basically making the same assumption : that some rich guy from a rich country can run a country better than the natives . It 's not just you either . Despite all the condemnation of imperialism we continue to have the same debate all the time that they had in the 19th century . Is it better for a powerful country to leave other countries alone , however horrible the things that happen there , or to stop those things and then assume all the expenditure , responsibility and resentment of running that country for years to come ?

One reason that super glue sticks to most surfaces is because it can chemically bond with the molecules on the surface of the material. When the glue comes into contact with the surface, it starts to harden and form a strong bond.

input: A pyramid scheme is a type of investment where each person involved recruits others to join. Money made by the new members funnels up to the higher members. It is called a pyramid scheme because it usually takes the shape of a pyramid, with the top layer having one person, the next layer having two people, the next four, and so on. Here's an example of how a pyramid scheme might work: The person at the top of the pyramid (the one who started the scheme) recruits two people to join, promising them that they will make a lot of money. These two people then recruit four more people each, promising them the same thing. The four people each recruit eight more people, and so on. The idea is that as more and more people join the pyramid, the money will keep flowing upwards to the top. However, eventually, the pyramid will collapse because there are not enough new people joining to sustain it. When this happens, the people at the bottom of the pyramid (the ones who joined last) will lose all of the money they invested. Pyramid schemes are illegal in many countries because they are fraudulent and do not provide any real value to the people who invest in them. They are simply a way for the person at the top to get rich by tricking others into investing money.

input: Homeopathy is a system of alternative medicine that was developed in the late 18th century. It is based on the idea that substances that can cause certain symptoms in a healthy person can also be used to treat those same symptoms in a person who is sick. Here's how it's supposed to work: A person with a particular health problem is given a very small amount of a substance that is believed to cause similar symptoms in a healthy person. The idea is that this small amount of the substance will stimulate the person's own healing response and help their body to get better. The substances used in homeopathy are often plant, mineral, or animal-based, and are typically diluted many times over in water or alcohol. Some people believe that the more a substance is diluted, the more powerful it becomes. Homeopathic remedies are usually taken in the form of pills or drops. Homeopathy is based on the belief that the body has the ability to heal itself, and that by using very small amounts of certain substances, it can be helped to do so. However, there is little scientific evidence to support the effectiveness of homeopathic remedies. Many mainstream medical organizations, including the National Institutes of Health (NIH) and the World Health Organization (WHO), have concluded that there is no scientific basis for the effectiveness of homeopathy.
output: I 'll try to actually explain it simple terms , because it really is surprisingly simple compared to sports in other countries . Things " in quotes " might be unfamiliar phrases . 2 teams , 11 players per team on the field at once . One team is " in possession " of the ball at a time . The team with the ball is " on offense " . The object of the game is to somehow end up in possession of the ball at the far side of the field , the side the team without the ball ( " on defense " ) is trying to protect . The game is not fluid . It is broken up into " downs " , essentially single plays . The ball is placed wherever it was at the end of the previous " down " . The teams must all be on one side of the ball . The down begins whenever the team on offense decides ( there is a time limit , or " play clock " ) . They " snap " the ball , which simply means they pick it up ( more to this , I can explain in more detail if you 'd like ) . During a down , a whole lot happens at once , but it 's all to try and achieve the same goal . The team on offense is trying in any way they can to move the ball forward , the team on defense is trying to prevent that . players can run forward holding the ball ( a " running play " ) or try to throw the ball to someone ( a " receiver " ) who is closer to the goal - zone ( " endzone " ) . A down ends when either the ball touches the ground ( in the case of an incomplete pass ) or when someone carrying the ball touches the ground . The game is then essentially paused ( timing is harder to explain ) , and the teams regroup on their side of the ball for the next down . An offense gets 4 downs to try and move the ball 10 yards . If they reach that 10 yard mark , their number of downs get refilled to 4 . If they do n't the other team goes on offense , switching out all of their players . Reaching the other team 's endzone is worth 6 points . If you 're close , but out of downs , you can kick a " field goal " by kicking the ball through the posts at the end of the field instead of running or passing it . This is worth 3 points . During a down , the big mess in the middle is called " the line " , in which large player from both the offense and the defense try to push each other around . If the defense is pushing forward , they have a better chance to stop the ball from moving forward . If the offense is winning here , they have an easier time simply running the ball straight forward . The rest of the positions involve either running or passing the ball , or defending running or passing the ball . On offense , a " quarterback " begins with the ball at the beginning of a down . His skill set almost always includes a strong and accurate throwing arm , so as to complete passes . Sometimes , he is also very fast , and essentially tricks the defense by running the ball forward himself . Behind the quarterback ( or sometimes to his side ) is the " running back " . This player is very good at holding on to the ball , and is usually either very fast / quick to elude defenders , or very strong , to essentially push them away . On the edges of the field , away from the line , are " wide receivers " . They usually specialize is finding space to run to to catch a pass and/or having sure hands when the pass reaches them . Together , these 3 players are called the " skill positions " , because they require some special talent . On defense , there are " linebackers " , who , as the name suggests , stand behind the line . They usually judge what the offense is trying to do , and either jump forward to defend against a running play or drop back to defend against a short pass . The rest of the defense , besides the line and the linebackers , are collectively called " defensive backs " . There are all sorts of various positions here , but they all do the same thing-- attempt to stop a pass from being completed . I think i covered most of it ... any questions ?

input: Sleep paralysis is a type of sleep disorder that can cause you to feel like you can't move or speak when you are trying to sleep. It can be a very scary and unsettling experience, especially if you don't know what's happening. During an episode of sleep paralysis, you may feel like you are awake, but your body is completely still and you are unable to move or speak. You may also feel like there is someone or something in the room with you, or that you are being pressed down or suffocated. These sensations can be very frightening and may make you feel like you are in danger. There are a few things you can try to help prevent sleep paralysis, such as getting enough sleep, following a regular sleep schedule, and avoiding alcohol and drugs. You can also try sleeping on your side instead of your back, as this can help reduce the likelihood of sleep paralysis. It's important to remember that sleep paralysis is usually not dangerous and will go away on its own after a few minutes. If you are concerned about your sleep or are experiencing sleep paralysis frequently, it's a good idea to talk to a doctor or a sleep specialist. They can help you figure out what might be causing your sleep paralysis and suggest ways to manage it.

input: Sure! A cousin is a relative who is the child of your aunt or uncle. So, if your mom's sister has a child, that child is your cousin. Second cousins are the children of your cousins. So if your cousin has a child, that child is your second cousin. Removed cousins are a little more complicated. When people are "removed," it just means that they are not as closely related as other cousins. For example, if you and your cousin are first cousins (meaning you have the same aunt or uncle), and your cousin has a child, that child is your first cousin once removed. This is because you are one generation apart from each other. If your cousin's child has a child, that child is your second cousin once removed. This is because you are two generations apart. input: Piezoelectricity is a property that some materials have, which means that they can change shape when an electric current is applied to them, or produce an electric current when they are squeezed or deformed. For example, if you have a piezoelectric material and you apply an electric current to it, the material will start to vibrate or move. On the other hand, if you squeeze or deform a piezoelectric material, it will produce an electric current. It's a bit like a rubber band that you can stretch and let go of. When you stretch the rubber band, it stores energy, and when you let go, the energy is released and the rubber band snaps back to its original shape. Piezoelectric materials work in a similar way, but instead of storing and releasing energy as movement, they store and release energy as electricity. Does that make sense? One reason is that it can be less expensive to hire workers in other countries, especially if the cost of living is lower there. Another reason is that there may be a shortage of qualified candidates in the company's home country, or the company may have difficulty finding people with the necessary skills or experience. In these cases, the company may look to other countries to find the talent it needs. 
While it's true that language barriers can sometimes be a challenge when working with foreign technical or customer support staff, many companies provide language training or hire staff who are fluent in the language used by their customers. While there can be challenges, many companies have found ways to successfully work with foreign staff and provide high-quality support to their customers.

input: Flammability is the ability of a substance to burn or ignite, and this depends on the chemical makeup of the substance. Some substances have a chemical structure that allows them to easily react with oxygen in the air, and this reaction releases energy in the form of heat and light. These substances are said to be flammable. Hydrogen and helium are flammable gases because they can react with oxygen to produce water and heat. Nitrogen, on the other hand, is not flammable because it does not react easily with oxygen. To understand this better, think about a candle. A candle is made of wax, which is a flammable substance. When you light a candle, the heat of the flame causes the wax to melt and vaporize. The vaporized wax reacts with the oxygen in the air, and this reaction produces heat, light, and water vapor (which is just steam). In the same way, when hydrogen or helium burns, it reacts with oxygen to produce heat, light, and water (or water vapor). Nitrogen, on the other hand, does not burn easily because it does not react with oxygen in this way. I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

One reason why not all money is made of paper is that paper money can wear out or get damaged easily. If you carry paper money in your wallet or purse, it can get wrinkled, torn, or stained. Metal coins, on the other hand, can last for many years and are less likely to get damaged. 
Another reason is that different countries and economies have different needs when it comes to money. Some countries may prefer to use metal coins because they are more durable and can be used for small transactions. Other countries may prefer paper money because it is more convenient to carry and easier to use for larger purchases. 
Overall, the choice of what material to use for money is based on a combination of factors, including cost, durability, and convenience.

Black light is made by creating light with a specific type of wavelength. This type of light is called ultraviolet light, or UV light for short. UV light has a shorter wavelength than the light that we can see with our eyes, which is why it appears black to us. 
There are different ways to create UV light. One common way is to use a special type of bulb or lamp called a black light bulb. These bulbs contain a chemical called phosphor, which is a substance that emits UV light when it is excited by electricity. When electricity passes through the phosphor, it causes the phosphor to emit UV light. 
Another way to create UV light is by using a device called a black light flashlight. These flashlights use a special type of LED (light emitting diode) that is designed to emit UV light. 
UV light is used in many different applications, such as for sterilizing surfaces in hospitals and laboratories, detecting fake currency, and creating special effects in stage lighting and nightclubs.

input: Canceling all the world's debt means that all the money that countries, companies, and individuals owe to each other would no longer have to be paid back. This might seem like a good idea because it would allow people and countries to start fresh and not have to worry about paying back their debts. However, canceling all the world's debt would also have some negative consequences. For example: Governments and companies use debt to finance important projects, like building schools, roads, and hospitals. If they can't borrow money, they might not be able to do these things. If debt is canceled, the people and organizations that lent the money (like banks and investors) might not get their money back. This could lead to financial instability and make it harder for people to borrow money in the future. Canceling all debt could also lead to inflation, which means that the value of money would go down and things would cost more. Overall, canceling all the world's debt might seem like a simple solution to some problems, but it would also create new problems and could have serious consequences for the global economy.

input: Sure! Imagine you have a bag of marbles and you want to know how many ways you can arrange them in a line. If you have no marbles, you have only one way to arrange them: an empty line. This is like x^0, where x is the number of marbles you have. Since there are no marbles, the number of arrangements is 1. Now, imagine you have one marble. You still only have one way to arrange them, because there is only one marble and it goes in the only spot available. This is like x^1, where x is the number of marbles you have. The number of arrangements is still 1. As you add more marbles to the bag, the number of arrangements increases. For example, if you have two marbles, you can arrange them in two different ways: either with the first marble first and the second marble second, or with the second marble first and the first marble second. Eating a diet that consists mainly of junk food, such as pizza and Kraft Dinner, can lead to malnutrition over time. While it is possible for someone to live off of junk food and not experience immediate negative consequences, the long-term effects of a diet high in processed and unhealthy foods can be serious. 
Malnutrition occurs when a person does not get enough nutrients from their diet. Nutrients are substances found in food that are necessary for our bodies to function properly. They include things like protein, carbohydrates, fat, vitamins, and minerals. 
Junk food is often high in calories, but low in nutrients. While it may provide some energy, it does not provide the nutrients that our bodies need to stay healthy. People who eat a lot of junk food may be at risk for developing nutrient deficiencies, which can lead to a variety of health problems. These can include problems with growth and development, weak bones, and problems with the immune system. 
It's important to eat a balanced diet that includes a variety of healthy foods, such as fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and proteins. This can help ensure that you are getting all of the nutrients that your body needs to function properly.

An itch is a feeling that you get that makes you want to scratch a part of your skin. Itches can be caused by many different things, such as insect bites, dry skin, allergies, or infections. 
When something touches or irritates your skin, it sends a message to your brain through nerve fibers. Your brain then interprets this message as an itch, and you feel the urge to scratch. Scratching can help to relieve the itch because it can help to remove the thing that is causing the irritation or it can stimulate certain nerve fibers that can help to block the sensation of the itch. 
Sometimes, you might get an itch even when there is nothing touching your skin. This can be because of things like dry skin, stress, or certain medical conditions. 
In general, it is a good idea to try to resist the urge to scratch an itch too much, as scratching too hard or too often can damage your skin and make the itch worse. Instead, you can try using a gentle lotion or cream to help moisturize your skin and reduce the itch, or you can try taking a warm bath or using a cool, damp cloth to help soothe the itch.

Finally, some scientists believe that the Earth's climate was warmer in the past, which could have allowed prehistoric animals to grow larger. Warmer temperatures would have meant more food was available, which would have provided these animals with the resources they needed to grow and thrive. Overall, there are a lot of different factors that may have contributed to the large size of prehistoric animals, including the Earth's atmosphere, the lack of large predators, and the warmer climate.

input: Passive-aggressive behavior is a way of expressing anger or frustration in a way that is not direct or open. It's a way of acting that can be difficult to recognize and deal with, because the person behaving this way might not admit that they are upset or angry. Imagine that you asked a friend to help you with a project, and they agreed to do it. But then, instead of completing the project on time, they keep finding excuses to delay it. They might say things like "I'm too busy" or "I can't do it right now." This can be frustrating, because it's not clear what the problem is. You might feel like your friend is being unhelpful or not taking your request seriously. This is an example of passive-aggressive behavior. The friend is not openly saying that they don't want to help with the project, but they are also not following through on their promise to do so. They are expressing their feelings indirectly, rather than directly saying what they mean. Passive-aggressive behavior can be annoying and confusing, and it can create tension in relationships. It's often better to communicate openly and honestly, rather than using passive-aggressive tactics.

output: Everyone else 's answers are correct and the gist of their answers is that cancer cells are your own cells . That is completely correct , but that only looks at one aspect of the problem : curing it in an individual . But why has n't cancer been cured completely ... in * everyone * ? Well , that 's because everyone 's cancer is different . Your cancer is very different from my cancer . * Even if * we have cancer of the same organ or tissue , our cancers are different . That 's because cancer is a disease of genetics and everyone 's genes are very different . This means that everyone 's cancer is a different disease . So because of that , cancer will never be * * completely * * cured . Terrifying . I know . But that is n't the end of the story . We 've discovered * some * similarities between cancers in different people . Certain cancers take their cue to grow from certain hormones . For example , there is a very specific type of breast cancer which looks for estrogen or progesterone ( two different hormones in everyone 's bodies ) as a signal to grow in the same way that whenever you feel your stomach grumble , you eat . Whenever estrogen or progesterone touches those cells , the cell divides and the cancer grows . Scientists have shown that if you minimize the activity of estrogen or progesterone in your body , the cancer has fewer signals to grow and it causes the cancer to shrink ( and maybe even die out ) . This only shows up in certain cancers : a very specific type of breast cancer , a very specific type of ovarian cancer and a couple others . In those cancers , this may work as a possible cure or treatment , but again , everyone 's disease is different , so it might not work for a given individual and it definitely wo n't work in a cancer that does n't show those markers . Things like this show up in a lot of cancers , and so doctors and scientists try to attack them in order to beat cancer in an individual . But those drugs do n't always work and a patient may have a relapse . This happens for two reasons : 1 . The drug does n't work on the cancer because the patient 's cancer is different because of genetics or 2 . the cancer evolved to become immune to the treatment . Imagine two people fighting . One of them is slightly stronger ( John ) , so he wins , right ? But what if the weaker one ( Mike ) picks up a baseball / cricket bat ? He becomes stronger than the unarmed one , right ? But then , to fight back , John would create a shield to protect himself against the bat . Since the bat no longer works , Mike might pick up a gun that breaks through the shield , so to protect himself , John needs a bulletproof vest , right ? This increasing of protection is what happens in cancer . The doctors try stronger and stronger drugs , but the cancer evolves to be immune to those treatments . The doctors have to resort to even stronger drugs and the cancer finds better ways to avoid the treatments . At some point in time , the doctors have used all the drugs that are available to them and the cancer has won out . It happens from time to time . And at that point , there is n't much more a doctor can do . Once the cancer takes over an important organ , the patient passes away because their body just ca n't function anymore . So researchers are always looking for new drugs or treatments that work . But cancer keeps changing in each individual , as well as in the species as a whole with different cancers showing up . It 's sad that we will probably never completely cure cancer . It 's pretty much impossible . Maybe with the popularity of individualized medicine that is tailored to your genes specifically , we 'll be able to defeat cancer once and for all . A doctor would simply send out a sample of your tissue to scan for your DNA and then get a report with all of your genes . They would then be able to tailor their treatment specifically to you and not to these trends that researchers have found in the lab .

input: Prime numbers are special types of numbers that are only divisible by 1 and themselves. For example, the first few prime numbers are 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, and 13. Prime numbers are important because they play a unique role in mathematics and computer science. One of the main reasons why prime numbers are important is because they are used to create secure communication systems. For example, the RSA algorithm, which is used to encrypt and decrypt messages, relies on the fact that it is very difficult to factorize large prime numbers. This makes it hard for someone to intercept and read a message that has been encrypted using the RSA algorithm. In addition, prime numbers are also used in other areas of mathematics, such as number theory and algebra. For example, they can be used to help prove theorems and solve equations. Overall, prime numbers are an important and fascinating topic in mathematics because of their unique properties and the many ways in which they are used.
