Cancer is not the most common disease associated with DNA mutations. In fact, most DNA mutations do not cause any significant problems or diseases. However, some DNA mutations can cause problems with the way cells function, and can lead to the development of cancer. 
Cancer is a disease in which cells in the body grow and divide in an uncontrolled way. Normal cells in the body follow a specific pattern of growth and division, but cancer cells do not. They continue to grow and divide even when the body does not need them, and they do not die when they should. This can lead to the formation of tumors, which can be harmful if they grow in certain parts of the body or if they spread to other parts of the body. 
There are many different factors that can increase the risk of developing cancer, including genetics, environmental factors, and lifestyle factors. DNA mutations can play a role in the development of cancer, but they are not the only cause. 
So, to answer your question: DNA mutations can sometimes lead to cancer, but this is not always the case. There are many other factors that can also contribute to the development of cancer.

Bison are a type of large, hoofed animal that are native to North America. They are related to cows, but they are generally bigger and can produce more meat per animal. Bison are also known for being hardy animals that can survive in a variety of climates and environments. 
However, there are a few reasons why bison are not raised as widely as cows for meat production. One reason is that bison are generally slower to mature than cows, which means that it takes longer for them to reach slaughter weight. This means that it takes longer for farmers to raise bison for meat compared to cows. 
Another reason is that bison can be more challenging to raise than cows. They are generally more independent and can be more difficult to handle, which can make it harder for farmers to manage them. In addition, bison require more space to roam and graze, which can be a challenge for farmers who have limited land available. 
Finally, bison can also consume more feed than cows, which can be more expensive for farmers. Overall, while bison have some advantages as a source of meat, they are generally not as practical to raise as cows for a variety of reasons.

input: Multiple sclerosis, or MS, is a disease that affects the brain and spinal cord. These are important parts of your body because they help send messages between your brain and the rest of your body. In people with MS, the protective covering (called myelin) around the nerves in the brain and spinal cord gets damaged. When this happens, it's harder for the messages to get through and the body doesn't work as well as it should. This can cause a person with MS to have problems with things like walking, seeing, and thinking clearly. MS is a chronic disease, which means it lasts a long time and there is no cure for it. But there are treatments that can help people with MS manage their symptoms and live full lives.
output: So let 's say there are 10 people who want to get elected . It 's a winner take all election , whoever gets the most votes is going to win and gets to make all the decisions . Well , when you go through the votes , its ' going to be clear some of those 10 people are more favored than others . Bob is not very favored , they estimate only about 5 % of people are likely to vote for Bob . If Bob just stays by himself and loses , he ges nothing . Bob mainly wants to make it a rule that everyone gets to eat pizza on friday . He has other stuff he 'd like , but that 's the important one . So Bob , knowing he ca n't win and wanting to make sure everyone gets pizza on friday goes to Tom . Tom is a front runner in the race and does n't particularly care what people eat for dinner on Friday . Bob tells Tom if he promises to make the pizza on fridays rule , he 'll drop out of the race and tell all the people who would have voted for him to vote for Tom instead . Now , this is great for Tom , remember he does n't care who eats what on Fridays , but those votes will help him get elected , so why not ? He agrees , and now Tom and Bob are in a political party . Bob is n't the only one to figure this out . Pretty soon everyone has asked for something from the two most likely to win , because there is no reason for them to continue running on their own when they know they wo n't win . It makes more sense to ally yourself with a more powerful candidate more likely to win in order to gain some influence even if you do n't agree with all their ideas , instead of running on your own , losing , and getting nothing . That 's how the American system works . Now the European systems are all different in different countries but basically instead of a winner take all , it 's weighed to how popular you are . Instead of electing one leader , everyone votes to decide how much say each leader gets . So Tom wins the election with 40 % of the votes . The does n't seem like much , but remember there are 10 people splitting up the votes , so the average would be only 10 % . Now , Bob brings up the pizza on Fridays rule for a vote , and gets 5 % of the vote . Now he can make deals with Tom and others to make sure his rule passes , but he does n't need to join Tom . He can stay his own party and influence votes on many different issues , not just pizza on friday . His 5 % of the vote might be the difference in a contested issue , so he can make deals to exchange his support for support on the stuff he wants . In this case it makes sense for him to stay seperate instead of joining Tom . By not being winner take all and leaving some consolidation for Bob , he 's more likely to stick it out alone .

output: Imagine you have a list of names you 're trying to remember : Mary Mary Mary Dave Mary Mary Mary Mary Dave Mary Mary Mary Mary Dave Mary Fred You could memorize each word ( or even each letter and space ) in order , but there are lots of ways to organize the data to make it easier for yourself . The first thing you notice is that there is a lot of repetition . You might try to think of the list like this : Mary(×3 ) Dave Mary(×4 ) Dave Mary(×4 ) Dave Mary Fred You 've reduced it from a list of 16 items ( each name ) to one of 11 ( each name plus the repetition notes ) . We can even reduce it further . You notice that this pattern appears three times : Mary Mary Mary Dave Mary So let 's define this : A = Mary(×3 ) Dave Mary Our list now becomes : A(×3 ) Fred Depending on how you want to count , that 's about 8 or 9 things to keep track of . We 've cut what we have to remember in half , and yet we have n't lost any of the original data ! As long as we know what method we used to make our shorthand list , it 's easy to take it and expand it back into the original list . File compression on computers works in a similar way . A computer uses a little program ( called a utility ) to compress the data . The utility has an algorithm ( a method of processing the data ) that looks for patterns and shortcuts in the binary code . ( We were concerned with discrete words above , but the utility analyzes the string of 1s and 0s that actually make up a file . ) The more sophisticated the algorithm , the smaller the compressed file can be . ( It also helps if there are lots of patterns or repetition for the algorithm to latch onto . Certain files compress better than others because of this . ) To decompress the data , a companion utility reverses the process .

input: Certainly! I'd be happy to give you a basic overview of how computers work. Computers are machines that can process and store information. They do this by using tiny electronic components called "transistors" to perform calculations and store data. Inside a computer, there is a central processing unit (CPU) that acts as the "brain" of the computer. The CPU is responsible for executing instructions and performing calculations. These instructions tell the computer what to do, such as open a program or save a file. Data is stored in a computer using a memory system. There are different types of memory, such as RAM (random access memory) and ROM (read-only memory), each with its own specific purpose. RAM is used to store data that the computer is currently using or processing, while ROM is used to store data that is permanently or semi-permanently written onto the memory, such as the computer's operating system. The computer also has input and output devices, which allow you to interact with it. Some common examples of input devices are keyboards, mice, and touchscreens, while examples of output devices include monitors and printers. Finally, computers are connected to each other and the Internet through a network of wires and wireless signals. This allows them to communicate and share information with each other. There are a few reasons why the audio on two-way radio systems used by emergency services might not always be crystal clear. Here are a few possible explanations: 
1. Interference: Two-way radio systems operate on specific frequencies, but these frequencies can sometimes be affected by other electronic devices or even physical obstacles, such as buildings or mountains. This can cause static or other interference that makes the audio sound fuzzy or unclear. 
2. Distance: The further away a radio user is from the transmitter, the weaker the signal will be. This can lead to poor audio quality, especially in rural or remote areas where there might be less infrastructure to support strong radio signals. 
3. Equipment: The quality of the equipment being used can also affect the audio quality. If the radios or other equipment being used are old or not well-maintained, it can lead to poor audio quality. 
4. User error: Sometimes, the person using the radio might not be holding it properly or might be speaking too softly or too quickly, which can also affect the audio quality. 
Overall, while it is important for emergency services to have clear and reliable communication, there are a number of factors that can affect the audio quality of two-way radio systems.

The QWERTY layout comes from the layout of old typewriters and was meticulously designed in such a way to minimize the chance of two commonly consecutive letters being close to each other . This was done because the levers that ultimately sprung out to hit the toner and the paper after pressing a button could jam because two or more collided with each other - so in order to minimize the chance of that happening Christopher Latham Sholes , who was a publisher but also an inventor , set out to invent the QWERTY layout . Nowadays of course we do n't have to worry about the computer jamming when we press two letters too quickly - but the people were already used to the QWERTY layout and so it became the standard for modern computer keyboards even though there are many different layouts like the [ dvorak or colemak layouts ] ( URL_0 ) that a small number of people will swear by because they are supposedly much faster .

output: A lot of the time they do things like gold - plate the connectors . That kind of thing is good for old - style ( analogue ) connections , because gold does n't oxidise nearly as much as most metals , so a gold - plated connector keeps a better quality connection for longer . However that is * not true * for HDMI . HDMI is digital , the information is effectively sent as 1s and 0s . So having the 1s and 0s be neater or clearer or bigger or whatever , does n't matter at all . As long as the TV can tell what 's a 1 and what 's a 0 , the entire signal comes through perfectly . In other words , HDMI cables either work completely perfectly or they do n't work at all . If a $ 10 cable gives you any kind of picture on your screen at all , that picture is exactly as good as the picture from a $ 100 cable .

String theory is a theoretical framework that attempts to describe the fundamental nature of the universe using strings, which are tiny, one-dimensional objects that vibrate at different frequencies. These vibrations create different particles and forces, such as quarks, photons, and electrons. One of the main ideas behind string theory is the concept of unification, which means that it tries to bring together all of the fundamental forces in the universe (such as gravity, electromagnetism, and the strong and weak nuclear forces) into one single theory. This is a difficult task, because these forces seem to act very differently from one another. Another concept that is important in string theory is supersymmetry, which is a type of symmetry that relates particles of different types. For example, according to string theory, every particle in the universe has a "partner" particle, known as a superpartner, that is related to it by supersymmetry. These particles are thought to be related because they have the same mass, but different properties. Overall, string theory is a very complex and abstract concept, and it is still being developed and tested by scientists. It is not yet known if string theory is correct, but it is a promising idea that could help us understand the fundamental nature of the universe.

input: Washing machines have different temperature settings so you can choose the right one for your clothes. Here's what each temperature does: Cold/Cold: This setting uses cold water for both the wash and rinse cycles. It's good for clothes that are very delicate or have bright colors that might fade if they get too hot. Cold/Warm: This setting uses cold water for the wash cycle and warm water for the rinse cycle. It's good for clothes that are not very dirty and don't need a lot of hot water to get clean. Warm/Warm: This setting uses warm water for both the wash and rinse cycles. It's good for clothes that are not too dirty, but need a little bit more heat to get clean. Warm/Hot: This setting uses warm water for the wash cycle and hot water for the rinse cycle. It's good for clothes that are very dirty or have stains that need to be cleaned with hot water. Hot/Hot: This setting uses hot water for both the wash and rinse cycles. It's good for clothes that are very dirty or have stains that need to be cleaned with hot water. As a general rule, you should use cold water for delicate fabrics like silk and rayon, and hot water for heavily soiled or greasy clothes like work uniforms or towels. Warm water is good for most other clothes. You can also check the care label on your clothes to see what temperature the manufacturer recommends.
output: Often , people say ' that 's ironic ' when they see a coincidence occur . Seen as you 're 5 years old for the purposes of this explanation , I 'll elaborate on what I mean by giving an example . " We were just talking about you , and now you 've called me ! How ironic ! " This is incorrect , and is probably the most common way of using English wrongly . Another way of using this word wrongly is by noting something unusual . For example , " It 's ironic that you actually washed the dishes after dinner today . " It is most wrongly used as to strengthen something interesting . " Ironically , the Philippines has over 7000 islands within it ! " So , how do we actually use it ? How do you avoid being quoted with resentment in posts like this ? Well , an ironic thing to say would be to say something and mean something completely different ! ( For a 30 year old , what I mean by this is that it is to say something that disagrees with its literal dictionary definition ) . For example , if you are in hospital and suffering from a terrible cold or illness , saying " I feel extremely well " is an ironic statement . Furthermore , saying " What a beautiful day it is outside ! " when it is raining and thunder is emerging is an ironic statement . You may be thinking , as a naive 5 year old , that sounds like sarcasm ! Well , you 're right , it bloody well does . The difference between them is simply how you deliver your contradictory statement . If it is harshly and mockingly , it is sarcasm . If it is not , it is irony . Now , that is irony . There are other types of irony , such as dramatic irony and situational irony . Dramatic irony is used in a play , when the audience is aware of the irony in a speech or situation , but the characters are not . Situational irony is when something turns out different to how it is expected . I hope I helped , if you would like me to clarify something , let me know . I would n't focus too much on situational or dramatic irony , they are not used nearly as often as you might think .

The feeling of "pins and needles" or tingling occurs when there is decreased blood flow to a specific area of the body. This can happen when a limb is in an awkward position and the blood flow is cut off, or if there is some other issue with the circulation of blood. 
When the blood flow is cut off, the cells in that area don't receive enough oxygen and nutrients. This can cause the cells to become damaged or die. As a result, the brain may interpret this as a sensation of pins and needles. 
The sensation of pins and needles is actually a way for the body to alert you that something is wrong and that you should move the limb to restore blood flow. If the limb stays in the same position for too long, it can lead to more serious problems, such as muscle damage or even nerve damage. 
It's not really possible for the limb to feel numb or pleasant when blood flow is cut off, because the lack of blood flow is what is causing the issue in the first place. The sensation of pins and needles is just the body's way of letting you know that something is wrong and that you need to take action to fix it.

The Dalai Lama is the spiritual leader of Tibetan Buddhism and is traditionally believed to be the reincarnation of his predecessors, dating back to the 14th century. The title "Dalai Lama" means "Ocean of Wisdom," and the person who holds this title is considered to be a spiritual leader and guide for Tibetan Buddhists. 
In Tibetan Buddhism, the process of finding and recognizing the reincarnation of a high lama, such as the Dalai Lama, is called "tulku." When a Dalai Lama dies, a search is undertaken to find his reincarnation, often through the use of certain rituals and divination practices. The reincarnation is then traditionally enthroned as the next Dalai Lama. 
The current Dalai Lama, Tenzin Gyatso, is the 14th person to hold this title. In 2011, he announced that he may be the last Dalai Lama, stating that the institution of the Dalai Lama may be abolished in the future. This decision is up to the Tibetan people and the Tibetan Buddhist community to decide, and it is not within the Dalai Lama's power to declare himself the last to hold the title.

input: Manual transmissions generally get better gas mileage than automatic transmissions because they are more efficient. This is because manual transmissions allow the driver to control the gears, which means that the engine can operate at its most efficient speed for a given road speed. For example, if you are driving up a steep hill, you can shift into a lower gear to give the engine more power to climb the hill, while if you are driving on a flat road, you can shift into a higher gear to use less fuel. Automatic transmissions, on the other hand, use a complex system of gears and hydraulics to automatically shift gears based on the speed and load on the engine. This can lead to less efficient operation, especially if the transmission is not perfectly matched to the engine and the driving conditions. There are also some technical reasons why manual transmissions may be more efficient. For example, they typically have fewer moving parts and less friction, which can reduce wear and tear on the transmission and improve efficiency. In addition, manual transmissions do not use a torque converter, which is a type of fluid coupling that is used in automatic transmissions to transfer power from the engine to the transmission. Torque converters can be less efficient than a mechanical connection, which is what a manual transmission uses to transfer power. Overall, the main reason why manual transmissions get better gas mileage is because the driver can control the gears and choose the most efficient gear for the driving conditions.

Toilet tanks are typically attached to the bowl with a rubber seal because rubber is a flexible material that can create a tight seal and is resistant to leaks. However, the base of the toilet, which sits on the floor, needs to be able to move slightly to accommodate small movements in the floor or foundation. If a rubber seal were used in this case, the toilet might be too rigid and might crack or break if the floor moves. 
Wax is a soft, pliable material that can create a good seal without being too rigid. It also has some stickiness, which helps it stay in place and prevents it from sliding around. When the toilet is installed, a ring of wax is placed around the base of the toilet, and the toilet is pressed down onto the wax. This creates a tight seal between the toilet and the floor, and allows the toilet to move slightly without breaking. 
Overall, wax is used to seal the base of the toilet to the floor drain because it is flexible and sticky enough to create a good seal, while also being able to accommodate small movements in the floor.

input: Asbestos is a type of mineral that was once used in building materials and other products. It is made up of very small fibers that can break off and float in the air. If these fibers are inhaled, they can get stuck in the lungs and cause serious health problems, including cancer. When asbestos is in good condition and left undisturbed, it is usually not a problem. However, if it is damaged or broken, the fibers can be released into the air and inhaled. This can lead to the development of serious health problems, including lung cancer, mesothelioma (a type of cancer that affects the lining of the lungs, stomach, and other organs), and asbestosis (a lung disease that causes scarring of the lung tissue). It is important to be careful when handling materials that may contain asbestos, and to have them properly removed by trained professionals if necessary. If you think you might have asbestos in your home, it is a good idea to have it tested and removed if necessary to protect your health.

input: If you break a bone and don't go to the hospital, it means that the bone is not being treated by a doctor. This can be a problem because the bone may not heal correctly. However, if the bone is not properly aligned while it is healing, it may heal in the wrong position. This can cause the bone to be weaker and more prone to breaking again in the future. output: They live in trees , and are GIANT compared to most other tree - dwelling animals . A primary reason why tree - dwelling animals are often small is that there is n't a huge amount of food available up there . This is where the sloths speed is a huge benefit to them . Thanks to sloths being so slow , their metabolism is ridiculously slow ( supposedly a few sloths have actually starved to death while they had full stomachs , because their body could n't process the food fast enough - dunno if that 's true though ) , so they do n't need to eat very much at all . This even makes them very good at surviving vs most animals biggest problem - the loss of natural habitat . A sloth does n't need a lot of room or food to survive . Where sloths live , there is very little that can actually get to & kill them in the tress . They are also very good at hiding where they are . They do things like climb to the ground to defecate , so that they ca n't be ' smelt out ' in the trees ( but this is also when they are most vulnerable to attacks , because they 're pretty much useless on the ground ) . Everything about the sloths evolution is all about conserving energy . From moving super slow , to having really strong bones so that its bones support its entire weight while hanging ( no need to waste energy on muscles for that ! ) , and even the fact that it has very long arms means it does n't need to move very often to reach food . The tl;dr of it is : Slow metabolism + not many predators that can get them in the trees = pretty damn smart evolution .

input:
The phrase "Am I being detained, officer?" is a common one that people use when interacting with law enforcement officers in the United States. It is a way for people to ask whether they are being held against their will by the police, or if they are free to leave. This phrase has become more commonly used in recent years as people have become more aware of their rights when interacting with law enforcement. 
The phrase "Am I being detained, officer?" is not necessarily a bad thing to say to a police officer. In fact, it is often seen as a way for people to assert their rights and to ensure that they are not being detained without cause. 
There is a legal precedent for this phrase in the United States. The Fourth Amendment to the United States Constitution protects people from unreasonable searches and seizures by the government. This means that law enforcement officers must have a valid reason, or "probable cause," to detain someone. If an officer does not have probable cause to detain someone, the person has the right to ask if they are being detained and to ask for the reason for the detention. If the officer does not have a valid reason, the person is usually free to leave. 
It is important to note that while the phrase "Am I being detained, officer?" is a way for people to assert their rights and ensure that they are not being detained without cause, it is not a guarantee that a person will be allowed to leave if they are being detained. It is always a good idea to cooperate with law enforcement officers, even if you believe that your rights are being violated. If you feel that your rights have been violated, you can seek legal advice or file a complaint with the appropriate authorities.

input: Universities and colleges are both places where people can go to learn and get a degree, but they are slightly different. A university is a school that offers a wide range of academic programs and typically has a larger student body than a college. Universities usually offer undergraduate and graduate degree programs, such as bachelor's degrees, master's degrees, and doctorates. They also often have a number of research facilities and may have a medical school or law school. A college, on the other hand, is usually smaller than a university and often focuses on a specific area of study, such as liberal arts or technology. Colleges may offer undergraduate degree programs, but they usually do not have graduate programs. There is some overlap between universities and colleges, as some colleges may be part of a larger university system and may offer both undergraduate and graduate degree programs. However, in general, universities are larger and offer a wider range of academic programs than colleges.

input: A "ghost city" is a term used to describe a city or town that has been built up with lots of buildings and infrastructure, but doesn't have many people living in it. This can happen for a variety of reasons. One reason that ghost cities have been built in China is because the government has encouraged development in certain areas as a way to boost the economy. This has led to the construction of many new cities and towns, some of which have not attracted as many residents as expected. Another reason for the existence of ghost cities in China is that the country has experienced rapid urbanization in recent years, with many people moving from rural areas to cities in search of work and better opportunities. This has led to a demand for housing in urban areas, and developers have responded by building new cities and towns to meet this demand. However, in some cases, these new cities have not been fully occupied, leading to the creation of ghost cities. Ghost cities have not been as common in other parts of the world for a variety of reasons. In some cases, economic or population growth has not been as rapid as it has been in China, so there has not been as much demand for new housing and infrastructure. In other cases, governments and developers have been more careful about planning and building new cities, ensuring that they are well-populated and sustainable.

input: Sure! Salts are used in cooking to add flavor to food. Different types of salt have different characteristics, such as taste, texture, and size of the crystals, which can affect how they are used in cooking. Here are some common types of salt and their differences: Table salt is a very fine, white salt that is often used in cooking because it dissolves easily and is easy to measure. Some people prefer the taste of sea salt because they find it to be less salty and more flavorful than table salt. Kosher salt is a type of coarse salt that is used in cooking, especially in kosher cooking. It is called "kosher" because it is used in the process of koshering meat, which means preparing the meat according to Jewish dietary laws. Kosher salt has a milder taste than table salt and is often used to season meats because it sticks to the surface of the food better. In general, the main difference between these types of salt is their taste and texture. Table salt is the most common type of salt used in cooking, but sea salt and kosher salt can add different flavors and textures to dishes. It's a matter of personal preference which type of salt you use in your cooking.
output: > Imagine a person with high anxiety sensitivity . She leans over a ledge of the Grand Canyon . input: The price of gasoline is typically listed in terms of the price per gallon, but the pumps at gas stations often display the price for a fraction of a gallon. The reason for this is that the pumps are measuring the volume of gas in terms of hundredths of a gallon, rather than full gallons. Imagine you are filling up a gas can at a gas station. The gas can might hold one gallon of gas, but the pump is measuring the volume of gas in smaller increments, like a hundredth of a gallon at a time. When you see the price listed as "9/10" of a gallon, it means that you are paying for nine-tenths of a gallon of gas. So, if you see a price listed as "$3.00/9/10 gal," it means that you will pay $3.00 for nine-tenths of a gallon of gas, which is just a little bit less than a full gallon. This way, the gas station can charge you a little bit more accurately for the exact amount of gas that you are pumping, rather than just rounding up to the nearest full gallon.

input: Restarting a computer can often help solve problems because it gives the computer a fresh start. output: Because Lambos are form following function . To put it another way , Lamborghinis are styled the way they are because of what they 're designed to do . If you want to build a car that goes from zero to fast in almost nothing flat you 've got to put a huge engine into it and you 've got to transfer a massive amount of power to the wheels using as little weight as possible . If you want that same car to be able to go around tight corners in the Italian Alps at completely unreasonable speeds without flying off into space you need to put the engine smack dab in the middle of the vehicle , shove the passenger compartment forward , and muck about with the rest of the car to ensure that the balance is n't too terribly far off . If you expect this thing to have any prayer of staying on the road at north of 150 miles per hour you need it to be low to the ground . A Lambo Gallardo is about 2 inches lower than a Camry is to the ground ( and , as I understand it , when it 's moving fast it 's even lower ) . All of that combines to make a car that has a very different kind of space available to it than a standard passenger sedan . The car must be lower , it will have a shorter hood , different transmission , and different weight distribution . All of those things will force body designers to make certain decisions which someone designing a Toyota Camrey wo n't make . Then there 's the market . Someone buying a Lambo will likely tolerate less leg room , lower head clearance , and limited trunk space if it means that the car performs better . People shop for different things when they 're considering a family sedan versus a quarter - million - dollar super - car . As a result , Toyota ca n't get away with using all of its trunk space to house an engine and Lambo can afford to jack up the price by $ 10,000 to put in its characteristic doors . But all that aside , it 's marketing that really closes the deal . Because when you 're selling a super - car you 're probably selling it to a wealthy guy who wants to spend his money on a sexy beast of a car : it has to * * look * * fast , exotic , and exciting . But when you 're selling a four - door family sedan you 're selling it to a married guy and his wife . Fast , exotic , and exciting are n't selling points then ; they 're liabilities . Toyota wants people to look at a Camry and see " safe , " " dependable , " " comfortable , " and " practical . " A car that looks like a testosterone fueled star - fighter that runs on money is n't going to sell nearly as well to the audience their cars tend to target . By the way , that 's why lots of car companies own more than one car brand . Volkswagen , for example , is part of the same company that owns Audi ( Luxury ) and Bugatti ( fast - sexy - supercars ) .

input: Sure! A standard deviation is a measure of how spread out a group of numbers is. Imagine you have a bunch of marbles in a bag. If they are all the same size, it's easy to grab one and say "this is a typical marble." But if the marbles are different sizes, it's hard to say which one is typical because some are big and some are small. That's what a standard deviation measures - how much the marbles differ from each other. Now, imagine you have a special machine that can measure the size of each marble and tell you how big they are. If you put all the marbles in the machine, it will tell you the average size of all the marbles. This is like the "mean" of the group. But the machine can also tell you how spread out the sizes are. It does this by finding out how far each marble is from the average size. If most of the marbles are pretty close to the average size, then the sizes are not very spread out. But if some of the marbles are much bigger or smaller than the average size, then the sizes are more spread out. The standard deviation is a way to measure how spread out the sizes are. It's like a ruler that you use to measure how far each marble is from the average size. If the standard deviation is big, it means the marbles are very spread out. If the standard deviation is small, it means the marbles are all pretty close to the average size. Now, let's say you have a bag of marbles and you want to know how rare a certain size is. You can use the standard deviation to help you figure it out. If a marble is one standard deviation away from the average size, it's a pretty common size. But if a marble is three standard deviations away from the average size, it's a very rare size. That's because if the marbles are all pretty close to the average size, it's very unlikely that you will find a marble that is three times bigger or smaller than the average size. I hope that helps! Let me know if you have any more questions.

input:
Sending large emails with attachments can be difficult because of the way that email works. When you send an email, it has to travel from your computer or device to the email server and then to the recipient's email server. Along the way, it can pass through many different servers and networks, and each of these servers and networks has limited resources. If the attachment is too large, it can use up too much of the resources of these servers and networks, making it difficult to send the email. 
Additionally, many email providers have size limits on the attachments that you can send in order to prevent people from sending very large attachments that could cause problems for the servers and networks. These size limits are often set to around 10 MB or less to ensure that emails can be sent and received quickly and reliably. 
So, while we do have the technology to send large attachments, it can be difficult because of the way that email works and the need to ensure that it is fast and reliable for everyone who uses it.
output: I 've posted this before but it covers the important points so here it is again : Let 's say you have a friend who runs a lemonade stand and it 's grown so successful that he sells shares in it . Now , things seem to be going pretty well for the lemonade stand and everyone seems to think that it will have a glowing future but you are n't so sure . Instead , you think that something bad is about to happen and that the stock is actually about to drop . Being an unscrupulous five year old , you 'd quite like to be able to profit from this and thanks to the process of shorting , you can . The first thing you need to do is find someone who holds shares and ask if you can borrow some of them . Obviously , this being an equally unscrupulous five year old , he 's not going to this purely for the fun of it but says he will agree if you agree to pay him a fee . In the end , he decides that he 'll lend you £ 100 worth of stock for a week if you give him £ 10 . So you 've got you 're £ 100 composed of 100 borrowed stock ( so each stock is worth £ 1 ) and the first thing you need to do is sell them . As a consequence , you now have £ 100 cash in your pocket but you still need to return 100 stocks to the lender + the £ 10 fee . Happily for you though , things start going to plan when word gets round that the lemons your friend 's using are actually mouldy and have been making people sick . Suddenly , the stock price of the stand goes through the floor ( lets say they drop to 50p per share ) and those £ 100 of shares you borrowed are now worth £ 50 . So , what you need to do now is buy 100 stocks ( to return to the lender ) but as the price has fallen , this only costs you £ 50 . That leaves you with holding 100 shares ( which you 'll return ) and £ 50 in your pocket . Granted , you 'll have to pay the lender a tenner as a fee but at the end of the day you 're £ 40 the richer . Well done my little five year old ! You 've just taken your first step on risky - yet - potentially - profitable - ethical - questionable - tightrope !

input: BMI stands for Body Mass Index. It is a measure of your weight in relation to your height. It is used to determine if you are at a healthy weight for your height. To calculate your BMI, you take your weight in kilograms and divide it by your height in meters squared. For example, if you weigh 60 kilograms and are 1.65 meters tall, your BMI would be calculated like this: 60 kilograms / (1.65 meters x 1.65 meters) = 22.49, A healthy BMI is usually considered to be between 18.5 and 24.9. If your BMI is below 18.5, you may be underweight. If your BMI is above 24.9, you may be overweight. It is important to have a healthy BMI because being either underweight or overweight can increase your risk of certain health problems, such as heart disease and diabetes. However, it is important to note that BMI is not always a perfect measure of health. For example, athletes and bodybuilders may have a high BMI due to their muscle mass, but they are still generally healthy.

input: Concorde was a supersonic passenger airplane that was used to fly very fast and cover long distances quickly. It was used by several airlines and could fly at speeds of over 1,300 miles per hour, which was much faster than most other airplanes. However, there were a few problems with Concorde that eventually led to it being taken out of service. One of the main problems was that Concorde was very loud and caused a lot of noise pollution when it flew. This made it difficult for the airplane to fly over certain areas or at certain times, because it would disturb the people living or working nearby. Another problem with Concorde was that it used a lot of fuel, which made it expensive to operate. This meant that the airlines had to charge very high prices for tickets on Concorde, which made it difficult for many people to afford to fly on it. Finally, in July 2000, there was a major accident involving a Concorde airplane in which all 109 people on board were killed. This tragedy made many people worried about flying on Concorde and led to a decline in the number of people using the service. As a result of these problems, the airlines that operated Concorde decided to stop using it and it was taken out of service in 2003. As for why it is common to refer to Concorde as a single airplane, even though there were a few of them, it is because Concorde was a very unique and special airplane. There were only a few of them ever made, and they were all very similar to one another. So, even though there were multiple Concorde airplanes, they were all considered to be part of the same group or "family" of airplanes, and it became common to refer to them as a single Concorde.

input: Numbers are a universal way of representing quantities, so it makes sense that they would be the same in every language. If every language had its own way of writing numbers, it would be very confusing and difficult to communicate about quantities with people who speak different languages. Think about it this way: if you wanted to tell someone that you have 3 apples, you would want to show them the number "3" no matter what language you are speaking. If every language had its own way of writing the number "3," it would be hard to understand each other and you might end up with a different number of apples than you intended!, On the other hand, letters are used to represent sounds and words, and different languages have different sounds and words, so it makes sense that they would have different ways of writing them down. Just like different languages have different words for the same thing (like "apple" in English, "pomme" in French, and "manzana" in Spanish), they also have different letters to represent those words.
output: Your car has a gas pedal , a brake pedal , and a clutch pedal . The brake stops the car , the gas speeds up the car , and the clutch allows you to level up your gas pedal . Think of Mario Kart . Princess starts zippy and but her top speed is n't so good . Koopa starts slow but at top speed he is very fast . Mario is in the middle category . Switching gears from 1 through 5 is like switching from Princess to Mario to Koopa as you are driving . The way you switch gears is to hold down the clutch while you move the stick shift to the number you want . If you are at a dead stop , you want to be in 1 so that you can take off fast ( like Princess ) . Then , when you hit Princess ' top speed you want to switch to 2 , and then when you hit that top speed you switch to 3 , then to 4 , and then finally if you are getting on the highway then you want to use Koopa because he has the best top speed . input:
The unemployment rate measures the percentage of people in the labor force who are looking for work but cannot find a job. It is calculated by dividing the number of unemployed people by the total number of people in the labor force, which includes both the employed and the unemployed. 
Sometimes, the number of people who are unemployed can go up, even if the unemployment rate goes down. This can happen when the number of people in the labor force decreases. For example, if a lot of people stop looking for work because they are discouraged or have given up hope of finding a job, they are no longer counted as part of the labor force. As a result, the number of unemployed people is divided by a smaller number, which can lead to a lower unemployment rate. 
On the other hand, if the number of people who are employed increases, the unemployment rate can go down, even if the number of people who are unemployed does not change. This can happen when more people start looking for work because they are more confident about finding a job. In this case, the number of unemployed people is divided by a larger number, which can lead to a lower unemployment rate. 
It is important to look at both the unemployment rate and the total number of people who are unemployed when evaluating the state of the job market. Together, these two measures can give a more complete picture of the employment situation.
output: Voting needs to be anonymous and have a trusted trail . Paper ballots fit this bill perfectly . You walk into the booth alone and cast your ballot . input: Reddit bots are programs that are designed to perform automated tasks on the Reddit platform. They are created by people called "developers" or "programmers," who use computer programming languages to write the code for the bot. The bots can exist in different places, depending on how they are set up. Some bots may run on the developer's own computer, while others may be hosted on a remote server or in the cloud. To function, Reddit bots need to be able to interact with the Reddit platform. This typically involves using the Reddit API (Application Programming Interface), which is a set of tools and protocols that allows developers to access and modify Reddit data. Once a Reddit bot is set up, it can perform a variety of tasks, such as posting comments or messages, upvoting or downvoting content, or tracking and reporting on certain activity on the platform. To explain further: a Reddit bot is like a computer program that is designed to do a specific job. Just like you might ask a person to do a task for you, you can also ask a bot to do a task for you (or for itself). The person who writes the code for the bot is called a programmer, and they use a special language to tell the computer what to do. The bot can exist on a computer, a phone, or even on the internet. When you want the bot to do something, you send it a message telling it what to do. The bot then does the task for you.

input: Scandinavia has a long history of social democracy, which is a political ideology that combines elements of socialism and democracy. There are several factors that have contributed to the development of social democracy in Scandinavia: A strong tradition of cooperation and consensus-building: Scandinavian countries have a long history of working together and finding solutions that benefit the common good. This has allowed for the development of strong social welfare systems and a more equal society. High levels of economic development: Scandinavia is home to some of the wealthiest and most technologically advanced countries in the world. This has allowed for the implementation of expansive social welfare programs, such as universal healthcare and education. A high level of unionization: In Scandinavian countries, labor unions have a strong presence and play a significant role in shaping economic and social policy. This has contributed to the development of policies that prioritize the needs of workers, such as strong labor protections and high minimum wages. A commitment to social justice: Scandinavian countries have a long history of promoting social justice and equality, and this has played a role in the development of social democracy. To add the ELI5 (Explain Like I'm Five) tag to the title, you can simply include it in the title of your question, like this: "ELI5: What is it about Scandinavia that caused its particular development of social democracy?"

input: Animals can eat raw meat because their bodies are built to handle bacteria and parasites that may be present in raw meat. They have stronger stomach acids, which helps them to kill off any harmful bacteria that they may consume. Humans, on the other hand, are more susceptible to getting sick from bacteria and parasites that may be present in raw meat. This is why it's important for humans to cook meat thoroughly to kill off any harmful bacteria. It's also a good idea to wash your hands before and after handling raw meat, to help prevent the spread of bacteria. So, the bottom line is that animals are better equipped to handle the bacteria and parasites that may be present in raw meat, while humans are more vulnerable and need to take extra precautions to avoid getting sick.

input: Shooter games, like Battlefield 3 and Modern Warfare 3, are not very realistic when it comes to weapons and attachments. These games are meant to be entertaining and fun to play, so they often take creative liberties with the design and capabilities of the weapons and attachments in order to make the game more enjoyable. For example, in real life, guns do not have unlimited ammunition and cannot be fired indefinitely without needing to be reloaded. In shooter games, however, players often have access to large amounts of ammunition and can continuously fire their weapons without needing to stop and reload. Additionally, the attachments that are available in these games, such as silencers and scopes, do not always function in the same way that they do in real life. In some cases, the attachments may not have any effect on the weapon at all, or may give the weapon abilities that it does not have in real life. Overall, while shooter games can be fun to play, it is important to remember that they are not meant to be realistic and should not be used as a source of information about real-world weapons and tactics.

input: Great question!, Ice does float in water because it is less dense than water. Density is a measure of how much matter is packed into a certain amount of space. A substance that is more dense has more matter packed into a given space, while a substance that is less dense has less matter packed into the same space. Water is a very strange substance when it comes to density. When it is a liquid, it is denser than ice. But when water turns into a solid (like when it freezes into ice), it actually expands and becomes less dense. This is because the molecules in a solid are usually arranged in a more orderly way than they are in a liquid, which means that they take up more space. So when water freezes into ice, it becomes less dense and will float on top of the liquid water. That's why ice floats in water, even though most solids are denser than the liquids they are placed in.

input:
When there is an oil spill, it can be tempting to try to burn the oil because it might seem like an easy way to get rid of the oil and the mess it causes. However, there are a few reasons why burning the oil is not a good solution: 
1. Burning the oil creates air pollution. When the oil is burned, it releases harmful chemicals into the air. These chemicals can be harmful to people, animals, and the environment. 
2. Burning the oil can spread the spill. When the oil is burned, the fire can cause the oil to spread to other areas, which can make the problem even worse. 
3. Burning the oil can cause damage to the environment. The heat from the fire can damage plants and animals in the area, and the smoke from the fire can also harm these living things. 
Instead of burning the oil, it is usually better to try to clean up the spill and remove the oil from the environment. This can be done in a number of ways, such as using special chemicals to break down the oil, using booms to contain the oil, and using absorbent materials to soak up the oil. Cleaning up an oil spill is a big deal because it is important to protect the environment and the living things that depend on it.

input:
Sure! When you turn on your WiFi, your phone is able to connect to nearby WiFi networks. These networks can be used to help determine your location. 
Your phone uses something called "triangulation" to figure out where you are. Triangulation is a way to find out where you are by measuring the distance between you and a few other things. In this case, the "other things" are WiFi networks. 
When your phone connects to a WiFi network, it can figure out how far away it is from that network. It does this by measuring how strong the signal is. The stronger the signal, the closer the WiFi network is. The weaker the signal, the farther away the WiFi network is. 
Your phone can connect to multiple WiFi networks at the same time. By measuring the strength of the signals from all of these networks, it can triangulate its position and figure out exactly where it is. This is much more accurate than just using the cellular network, which can only give a rough estimate of your location. 
So, when an app asks you to turn on your WiFi, it's because it needs to use these nearby WiFi networks to help determine your location more accurately.

These new groups act differently , because of the new members . These new groups in the case of crude oil becoming plastic start off fluffy , so more people need to join the group and it needs to become heated . When it 's heated the people become closer and form a more solid group . A catalytic reaction then takes place between these two which produces a " fluff " of polymer . Additives are added and finally this product is heated and shaped into plastic pellets which we are used to make plastic products . Feel free to correct any errors I have . input: The placebo effect is a phenomenon that can occur when a person believes that they are receiving a treatment, even if that treatment is not actually a real medical intervention. For example, if a person has a headache and they are given a sugar pill that they are told is a pain-reliever, their headache might go away simply because they believe the pill will help. There are several theories about how the placebo effect might work. One theory is that the brain's own natural healing abilities can be activated by the belief that a treatment will work. Another theory is that the placebo effect may be related to the release of chemicals in the brain, such as endorphins, which can help to reduce pain. So, when a person "believes away" a headache with the help of a placebo, it is likely that their brain is releasing chemicals that help to reduce the pain they are experiencing. This can happen even if the treatment they are receiving is not a real medical intervention.
output: " when " is complicated : in modern cars , the engine control unit ( a specialised computer that controls all the functions of the car related to the core functions of being a car , rather than ones like running a satnav or the stereo ) monitors speed , the engine RPM and the position of the gear selector at a minimum , and potentially a whole bunch of other factors like tyre grip , to select the most appropriate gear ratio for the current conditions . In older cars ( without a computer - controlled transmission ) , the number of sensors monitored was smaller ( usually just the three mentioned above , or even just RPM and selector position if the transmission does n't skip gears , which is a fair assumption ) and they were monitored with mechanical sensors which would operate either relay logic or very simple discrete logic . ECUs have been around for a long time , however : anything after about ' 95 will probably have one , unless it 's a land rover or something else designed to work After The End . In both cases , after the selection logic has done its ' thing , the mechanism to actually select gears is pretty much the same : most automatic gearboxes use a system called a " planetary gearbox " , where there are three sets of gears : an outer pawl ( toothed track , like the reverse of a gear ) , a " planet carrier " with a number of identically - sized " planet gears " spaced around it ( usually four , sometimes more , almost never less ) which engages the outside pawl on the outside of the planet gear and the " sun gear " on the inside . The clever part comes in the fact that any one of the rings or gears can do one of four things at any one time : it can be locked still , allowed to turn freely , connected to the input shaft ( to the engine ) , or connected to the output shaft ( to the diff and the wheels ) . So to get one gear ratio of 1:1 , the planetary gears are locked , the sun gear is connected to the input and the pawl is connected to the output , and the whole thing moves as one . If we wanted a higher ratio , we would connect the planet carrier to the input shaft , and the outer ring to the output , lock the sun gear and leave the planet gears free : now as the planet carrier moves , the planet gears have to turn like wheels to catch up , so the outer ring has to move faster to stay engaged with the planet gears . Wikipedia has a much more elegant summary and shows [ all the ways you can hook up a planetary gearbox :] ( URL_0 ) there are actually twice this number , since you can also swap the driving and driven shafts to get the reciprocal ratio ( 2:1 rather than 1:2 ) . Most automatic gearboxes wo n't use all of these ratios , and will have four or five plus reverse , although there are bigger gear sets possible . Neutral is obviously selected by disengaging clutches so that the output is disconnected . Each of the parts of the gearbox is usually engaged by a system of hydraulic clutches and solenoids : the amount of pressure involved means there 's not much loss through the clutches ( a little more than a manual transmission loses , but not much at all in a decent gearbox ) . Automatics also use a torque converter in place of a normal clutch , which is sufficiently complicated to need its own ELI5 , but it provides a way for the engine to change rotation speed smoothly without too much loss of power , rather than you having to clutch - let off the gas - change - declutch ( in an upshift ) to match the engine speed to the gearbox . They use a viscous fluid , and exploit fluid coupling to do this , losing energy to heat along the way , but again modern TCs are quite efficient . Very modern automatic transmissions use a completely different method , which actually bears more resemblance to a manual than an automatic : two ( manual ) gearboxes are driven in parallel by the engine , each going through a traditional clutch . One has gears 1,3,5 and the other 2,4,6 .You could have more , but that 's getting silly on things that are n't massive trucks : more gears means more rotational inertia and lower efficiency , so because car engines have reasonably wide power bands and are n't required to hit near - perfect efficiency for cost reasons , it 's more cost - effective to have less gears and make the engine work more . The ECU controls each clutch and gearbox independently : if we imagine starting in first , the ECU will engage first gear on gearbox 1 and second on gearbox 2 , but keep gearbox 2 's clutch disengaged : we hit the gas , and accelerate in first . Now when we change up ( most of these gearboxes are in cars with " electronic manual " , or flappy - paddle gearboxes ) , or the engine RPM gets high enough , the ECU disengages clutch 1 and engages clutch 2 in the space of a few milliseconds , while simultaneously blipping the throttle down to match engine speed precisely . Gearbox 1 now selects third , and waits for you to reach the right speed / RPM to engage it . The ECU is constantly monitoring what you 're doing ( through huge numbers of sensors , including things like turn rate ) to figure out whether you want to go up or down a gear next , and usually gets it right ( and gives you an incredibly fast shift ) - if you can fool it , it catches up in a few hundred milliseconds ( tenths of a second ) , which is about as fast as most drivers can make any change : race drivers can do sub-1/10th - of - a - second changes if they 're good , but it 's not easy . These dual - clutch gearboxes are popular in very high performance cars ( the Bugatti Veyron uses a very , very good six - speed for instance ) because they lose less power in the transmission itself , but they weigh about the same as a normal automatic and require much more advanced ECUs than a traditional automatic . They are starting to appear in lower - end cars , though : Smart have been using them for a while , and I think the new Ford Focus has one : they give a lot of the " feel " of using a manual ( the direct link between engine and transmission helps ) while providing some of the efficiency benefits of an automatic , since the engine can decide for itself what gear is most appropriate to put the engine closest to the " power band " ( where the engine is outputting the most horsepower ) or the point of maximum efficiency ( which is usually close ) . I 've explained to a five - year - old driver in places : hopefully 5YO you can reach the pedals .

input: Eugenics is the study of ways to improve the genetic qualities of a population, usually by encouraging people with desirable traits to have more children and people with less desirable traits to have fewer children. Some people think that eugenics could be used to create a society of healthier, smarter, and more successful people, but others believe that it is a dangerous and unethical idea. One pro of eugenics is that it could potentially lead to a population with fewer genetic diseases and disabilities. For example, if people with genetic conditions that cause serious health problems were less likely to have children, the overall health of the population might improve. Another concern is that it is difficult to predict the long-term effects of eugenics on a population. Changes to the gene pool can have unintended consequences that are difficult to predict or control. In summary, eugenics is a controversial idea that involves trying to improve the genetic qualities of a population, but it has the potential to cause harm and discrimination.

Flat black paint is often used to increase an object's emissivity because it absorbs a large amount of light and heat. output: Not really ELI5 - able because it 's a complicated subject but : So , whenever you sell property ( subject to an insane number of rules ) you calculated the amount that you gained or lost on your investment , and if you had a gain ( called a " capital gain " ) they you pay taxes on that gain . As an example : you buy $ 10,000 worth of stock , then sell it a year later for $ 30,000 ( good investment ) and pay taxes on your $ 20,000 ( $ 30,000 - $ 10,000 ) profit . Now , for inherited items , this gets a little tricky . So instead of you buying that stock , lets say your grandfather bought it at $ 10,000 and when he died it was worth $ 25,000 , and when you finally sold it you got $ 30,000 for it . Under the current tax plan , you 're only required to pay taxes on a gain of $ 5,000 , because you 're allowed to use the value at the time of inheritance as your " basis " in the stock ( " basis " is the technical term for basically " the value you calculate your gain / loss from " ) . * * Under the new plan :* * you would instead pay taxes on the $ 20,000 gain , using the original $ 10,000 basis from the original purchase of the stock ( with exemptions of something like $ 250,000 per person , to spare the middle class ) . Also , note that capital gains have generally been taxed at a lower percentage than ordinary income ( currently 15 % for anyone in the 25 % income tax bracket or up ) , with the rationale being basically that this will encourage business investment . Of course , since many very rich people make the vast majority of their income in the form of capital gains and not salary , it also causes the famous case of billionaires paying lower tax rates than their secretaries ( see : Buffett , Warren ) . In a related proposal , Obama has suggest raising the capital gains rate to 28 % for couples with incomes in excess of $ 500,000 . Those two changes would result in the government raising a lot of extra revenue ( as well as long - term macroeconomic consequences that I am not qualified to speculate on ) . Source : I prepared taxes professionally for five years

Sure! In general relativity, gravity is not a force that acts between masses like it is in classical physics. Instead, gravity is a manifestation of the curvature of spacetime caused by the presence of matter or energy. Imagine that spacetime is like a rubber sheet, and objects with mass or energy cause a depression in the rubber sheet. The curvature of the rubber sheet then causes other objects to be "pulled" towards the massive object, as if it were a gravitational force. 
Now, imagine that you are standing on a trampoline with a bowling ball in the center. If you were to roll a smaller ball towards the bowling ball, the smaller ball would be "pulled" towards the bowling ball due to the depression in the trampoline caused by the bowling ball's mass. This is similar to how objects are "pulled" towards each other due to gravity in general relativity. 
But what does this have to do with acceleration? In classical physics, acceleration is the rate at which an object's velocity changes over time. In general relativity, an object's velocity is determined by the path it follows through spacetime. If the path is curved, the object will appear to be accelerating, even if it is not actually changing its velocity. 
For example, imagine that you are standing on the trampoline again, but this time you are rolling a ball in a straight line towards the bowling ball. As the ball approaches the bowling ball, it will start to follow the curve of the trampoline, and it will appear to be accelerating towards the bowling ball. This is similar to how an object orbiting a massive object, like the Earth orbiting the Sun, appears to be accelerating towards the Sun, even though it is actually following a straight path through curved spacetime.

input: Taxation is important in a government fiscal policy because it is one of the main ways that the government can raise the money it needs to fund its various programs and services. These programs and services can include things like schools, roads, hospitals, and social services. Broad-based taxes are taxes that are applied to a wide range of goods, services, or activities. An example of a broad-based tax is a sales tax, which is applied to the purchase of most goods and services. Other examples of broad-based taxes include value-added taxes (VAT) and consumption taxes. Progressive taxes are taxes that take a larger percentage of income from people who earn more money. For example, someone who earns $50,000 per year might pay a lower tax rate than someone who earns $100,000 per year. The idea behind progressive taxes is that people who earn more can afford to pay a higher percentage of their income in taxes, and that this can help to reduce income inequality. It is difficult to say whether broad-based taxes or progressive taxes are more ideal, as it depends on the specific circumstances and goals of a government. Both types of taxes can be useful tools for raising revenue and achieving policy objectives, but they also have their own unique benefits and drawbacks. Ultimately, the best approach will depend on the specific needs and priorities of a particular government and its citizens.

input: Eigenvalues and eigenvectors are important concepts in mathematics that are used to understand how certain systems change over time. Imagine you have a matrix, which is just a fancy way of saying a grid of numbers. An eigenvector of this matrix is a special kind of vector (a list of numbers) that doesn't change direction when the matrix is applied to it. So if you start with the eigenvector pointing in a certain direction, it will still be pointing in that direction after the matrix is applied to it. An eigenvalue is a special number that tells you how much the eigenvector changes in size (but not direction) when the matrix is applied to it. So if the eigenvalue is a big number, it means that the matrix will make the eigenvector bigger. If the eigenvalue is a small number, it means that the matrix will make the eigenvector smaller. Eigenvalues and eigenvectors are used to understand how certain systems change over time. For example, if you have a system that changes the size of things, you can use eigenvalues and eigenvectors to figure out how the sizes of things will change over time.

When water goes up your nose, it can cause a burning sensation because the water irritates the sensitive tissue inside your nostrils. This is called "nasal irritation." When you dive into a pool or take a shower, the water can also cause your sinuses (the air-filled spaces in your skull) to become pressurized. This pressure can cause a burning sensation in your nose, as well as a feeling of congestion or stuffiness. 
Your nose and sinuses are lined with tiny hair-like structures called cilia, which help to filter and moisten the air you breathe. When water gets into your nose, it can disrupt the function of the cilia and cause them to stop working properly. This can lead to irritation and inflammation, which can cause the burning sensation you feel. 
In addition to the physical sensations, the feeling of water going up your nose can also be a bit scary and uncomfortable. It's important to be careful when diving into pools or taking showers to try to avoid getting water up your nose. A hypoallergenic animal is one that is less likely to cause an allergic reaction in people who are sensitive to pets. The term "hypoallergenic" does not mean that the animal is completely allergy-free, but it usually means that it produces fewer allergens, which are proteins that can cause an allergic reaction. 
Dogs and cats have both hair and fur. The main difference between the two is that hair is usually longer and coarser, while fur is shorter and softer. Hair also tends to shed less than fur. Some people believe that animals with hair, rather than fur, are less likely to cause allergies because they produce fewer allergens. However, this is not necessarily true for all hypoallergenic pets. 
There are several breeds of dogs and cats that are considered hypoallergenic because they produce fewer allergens than other breeds. These breeds may have hair rather than fur, or they may have a different type of fur that is less likely to cause an allergic reaction. Some examples of hypoallergenic dog breeds include the Bichon Frise, Poodle, and Schnauzer. Some examples of hypoallergenic cat breeds include the Devon Rex, Sphynx, and Siamese. 
It's important to note that while these breeds may be less likely to cause an allergic reaction, they are not completely allergy-free. Radiation can be harmful, but it can also be used to treat certain medical conditions. When used in the right way, radiation can help kill cancer cells or shrink tumors. It can also be used to treat other conditions, like an overactive thyroid gland or certain types of blood disorders. 
Radiation works by damaging the DNA inside cells. This makes it hard for the cells to grow and divide, which can help kill cancer cells or shrink tumors. Normal cells can also be damaged by radiation, but they are usually able to repair themselves. Cancer cells, on the other hand, may not be able to repair themselves as well, which is why radiation can be effective at killing them. 
When doctors use radiation to treat someone, they carefully plan the treatment to make sure that the right amount of radiation is used in the right place. They also try to protect normal cells as much as possible to minimize side effects. Even with these precautions, side effects can still happen, and that's why radiation is usually only used when the benefits outweigh the risks.
